 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                             2




                      Vowel Initial and Zero (Ø)


   Dene Lexware dictionary comparisons:    AAD1990:63-69 , KAD2000:1-13, LTDD: zero-V, YMM92: no
  section, various
    Vowel initial roots in Dena'ina consist of postpositions, nouns and enclitics. PA vowel initial verb
  roots are y- initial in Dena'ina, and are found in the section
   Vowel initial verbs can be detected when D- classifier appears as a stem initial consonant gheyuł he is walking
  along; nugheduł he is walking along returning
   vowel initial nouns and postpositions have epenthetic elements when preceded by
         na'unkda UO navuntda I our mother; dialects differ, here UO insert glottal stop before
  prefixes
  -un 'mother' whereas I inserts -v

Ø¹ /zero noun-enclitic suffix/     /ⓐ::sf/
  Ø (n.sf.) possessed suffix in nouns
  Ø (sf.) zero enclitic suffix Holton and Lovick 2000 analysis of evidentials
  di, shi, yi (enc.) zero evidentials as noted in Holton, Lovick 2000 sdul yinhdi bada dalchin
  di? who made the table- evid?; q'u shi liq'a na'a k'iydlan shi now the fish have come to us it
  seems; dihdi tlegh yel dghinih yi that grease he would call"tlegh" evid.; SP-ch'anqet'

Ø² /zero verb suffixes/        /ⓐ::ⓐ::vsf1/
  Ø (v.sf1.) conclusive, to a conclusion; durative; distributive; continuative

Ø³ /zero-classifier/       /ⓐ::vpf+/
  Ø (v.pf.) classifier
    Ø (t.f.s.) derived intransitive
    INC#Ø (t.f.s.) derived intransitive with incorporate
    O+Ø (t.f.s.) derived transitive
    INC#Ø (t.f.s.) derived transitive with incorporated object
    INC#O+G+Ø (t.f.s.) derived transitive with incorporated subject

Ø⁴ /zero (overt) third person/         /ⓐ::vpf+/
  Ø (v.pf.) he, she, it, third person subject łiq'a t'us s/he is cutting fish; yet'us he is cutting it
  Ø (v.pf.) him, her, it, third person sg. direct object yet'us he is cutting it; łiq'a t'us he is cutting a
  salmon; ye- deletes when object is stated yet'us he is cutting it; vada nghił'an? whom did he
  see?; vada is the object vada yenghił'an? who saw him?; vada is the subject

Ø⁵ /unmarked PAS/     /ⓐ::PAS/
 Ø (v.pf.) zero imperfective mode; zero aspectual derivations called the primary aspectual strings,
 Kari 1979
 Ø (a.d.s. conc) conclusive, to a conclusion dhełchiin I made it
 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                  3

  Ø (a.d.s. dur) durative gheshghun I made them
  Ø (a.d.s. nmom) arriving; extending niyu he arrived
  Ø (a.d.s. neu) Ø neuter ni'u it (linear) extends
  Ø (a.d.s. cust) customary qedił they cust. go

Ø⁶ /zero-qualifier/   /ⓐ::vpf+/
  Ø (v.pf.) zero qualifier
    Ø (n.d.s.) zero gender most commonly, all animals, persons are Ø- gender

Ø⁷, a¹ /allative/   /ⓐ::DIR-sf/   /allative/ Ø suffix in directionals
  Ø (sf.) to, toward, allative null suffix in directionals yun'e towards upriver; yutsen towards
  wated, downland

Ø⁸ *hə~Ɂə /verbal peg/      /ⓐ::vpf+/
   in Den. and Ahtna this is always a zero morpheme
  Ø (v.pf.) peg prefix, 3rd person subject chegh UO chagh I s/he is crying

Ø⁹ *ə# /null postposition/    /ⓐ::PP#/
    This is a zero morpheme in Den. and Ahtna. This is ə in other lang. such as Koy. and LT
  P+Ø# (t.f.s.) null postposition, derived ditransitive forms subthemes
    yek'ełqat he is feeding sth. to him; yenuk'ełqat he is feeding sth. to him again; John
  nuk'ełqat he is feeding John; yeyighiqat he bought it from him
  P#Ø (t.f.s.) thematized oblique (with no direct object)
     P+nu#n+tun help P yenunetuh s/he is helping him/her
  P+Ø, P+e (p.p.) against P, in contact with P qek'di'un sth. (the sun) is shining on a
  place; shk'di'un the sun is shining on me; shdidu he is sitting against me; shdi'un I am wearing
  a badge; it is hanging on me; shek'di'un I am wearing it (badge); shnitun it (rifle) is leaning
  against me
  P+Ø#O+ł (t.f.s.) give, attach O to P yeyiłkez he has it tied onto it; yeyiłyuch' he tied it onto it
  P+Ø#d (a.d.s. gh mom) leaving P behind yedghiyu he left it behind; yenududyu he left it
  behind again; shedghiyu he left me behind

[+voi] /negative suffixation rule/
  Ø+[voi] (v.sf.) not VERB in Den. this is best treated as voicing of the stem final C in Kenai dialect
  often glottal follows the final voiced C nch'u iyul he did not arrive; ł+Ø → l

a² *eˑ /in contact with/   /ⓐ::PP#/
  P+a# (p.p.) against, up to surface of, in contact with P yaniyu he went up to it, up to surface of
  it; vantudushi deqakena ghin yanadaniłtl'in he tied wide grass against his leg (a splint); ts'iłten
  yanichet he pulled the bow string back; qanqildatl' they moved up to the place
  (wall); hyaqendaniyel on it they set it on fire; shahdani'un they named me, put a label on
  me; ni ghini hyaghaninik island that to it they went by boat
  a# (t.f.s.) applicative with no direct object yaghidghach he bit it


 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                     4

  P+a (p.p.) P spends time va shan hghizet he spent the whole summer day; sha tets hghizet I
  spent the whole night
  P+a (p.p.) on, for P yaghelich' q'u na'a tałqundu is it a good sunrise for us; a common good
  morning expression na'a qenelquh it cust. is hot for us; yuzhun na'a qilan it is a clear sky for
  us, we have a clear sky; na'a k'i'un we have a new moon; hva łitl'en qizdlan ha' on them spring
  it became; na'a nututchił looks like we will have snow; na'a tułkun it will rain on us

a³ *ə /kinship suffix/      /ⓐ::n.sf./
  a (n.sf.) kinship suffix the final syllable in several ancient kinship terms, a possible n.f.st. -
  uda older sister; -ingha older brother; -kela younger brother; -yuzha woman's sister's son; -
  tsa'a man's daughter
  -ey'a (n.) man's son
  -ez'a (n.) uncle, mother's brother

a⁴ *-əɁ /possessive/     /ⓐ::POSS/
  -a, -a' (n.sf.) UI -'a -a, possessed noun final glottal is heard at times but is not distinctive in Den.
  and is not usually written dialect differences in O, esp. with nasal stems, CVn tets', -
  tets'a all cane, one's cane; ven, -vena I ben, -bena U ben, ben'a O lake

a⁵ /PP compound/ perhaps distinct compounding affixes grouped here
  a (c.) postposition compound
     -adet (p.p.) without P
     -aq'a (a.n.) place, area
     -azhu (a.n.) OIl-U sign, marker tinitun qughest'a qazhu qadil'an the trail is well
     marked; shazhu qadil'an I have a mark on me; qazhu qadil'ani hqugh niniyu he went as far
     as the mark
     qazhu hdił'ani, qazhu hdil'ani (n.) OIl marker, sign
     ajeh (exc.) O hold up, stop a dog call

a⁶ /a-neuter/    /ⓐ::vpf+ ∀/
    the a-neuter string, or a-neu theme category, 1st noted in Koy., found with four qualifier prefixes;
  important Den. archaism
  a (v.pf.) a-conjugation Dena'ina forms are limited k'ada'u it is sticking out from it (e.g.
  branch); vahda'u he has a hump, bump (sticking out); vahna'u he has a lump on his
  face; qada'u it (tree) is standing up; bech' hda'u he is lucky; uk'uch' hda'u he is
  unlucky; t'najuq it turned out wrong; e.g. batch of beer went bad hnat'an U he is playing

➡a⁶ in ch'qenaxa I don't talk (negative imperative) see i, a⁶


egh, ghe¹ *-ɑgh, *-ghɑ-n /in relation to/         /√ⓐ::PP¹-space/time‴/
    Den. does not make much use of *-ghɑ-n very productive postposition, with all lexemes placed
  here
  d.f.: egh, geh, ghet, ghena
  Grp. 1. postpositions

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                             5

 P+egh (p.p.) by, to, at, about appears as egh following a prefix but as ghe following a noun
   shegh ninyu you came to me; Peter ghe ninyu you came to Peter; qegh nuqulnish he used to
 tell about it; begh ghestnu I work for him; yegh ghetnu he is working on it; dnaghelt'ana egh
 niqaghaninik lots of people landed in a boat; yegh nudyux deghk'isen ghun he keeps coming
 back to that woman; yadi ghet? what about?; dilihi ghe shidghiłqet he asked me about
 hooligans; velik'a ghe ninik'ezet he is thinking about his dog; qegh ht'idulchet ha' by them
 inside the hole he reached; k'tsen nihdi hvegh k'qisil meat and other things on them it ran
 out; shegh k'deli he is singing about me; nił'egh qach'eltax we sleep together; q'uyehdi
 duch'ehdi k'i nagh dileh then some suckers swim by us; shegh enduyiniłtan she brought him in
 to me; qegh nishełdaniłtuk' to them he ran
 -eghna, -ghena (a.n.) nearby, close2 to from egh+na noun forming suffix ven ghena ezdu he
 lives near the lake; guduh qeghna ezdu he lives nearby; sheghena ezdu he lives near
 m; sheghna sht'a k'eltim O it's thundering right near me; tuvugh qeghena near the
 beach; vinłni hqeghna near the water; tubughh qeghna sht'a right near the beach
    q'udi hghena (adv.) recently Tiduqilts'ett yit q'udi hghena chik'enaq hbetudinzet O at 'bad
    thing happened place' recently sickness struck them
 niłghena (adv.) time after time, regularly, constantly with the areal/temporal noun suffix -
 na niłghena sht'a qenax he talks repeatedly; qya ghetnuh niłghena q'u qunuldush he is
 working on him he comes out time and again; niłghena q'u yeł k'dghiłtetl' he shot sth.
 rep.; niłghena q'u nughenał he is rep. moving; niłghena q'u shidełqit he repeatedly asks
 me; niłghena viq' t'qetni busy signal on telephone
    izin niłghena deltetl'i (n.) I automatic gun, rifle
 gh (n.f.sf.) by, at forms areal nouns, postpositions
    -lugh (a.n.) holding on to; at the end of
    t'ugh (a.n.) area beneath
    yuvugh (a.n.) horizon
 -qegh (a.n.) behind, nearby from q+egh qenq'a hqegh behind the house; qeghna nearby
 people; qeghna qisen there are no people nearby
    Qeghnen (p.n.) North Kenai area lit. 'adjacent land'
    qagh (a.n.) at the foot of
 gh-DIR (pf.) in relation to DIR forms postpositions from directionals roots sheghduch'en on the
 downriver side from me; qeghnich'en to the upriver side of it; Htsit qeghenich'en it is upstream
 from Htsit; yeghnehch'en upland from him
 Grp. 2. strings with egh, ghe
 P+egh##, P+egh# (a.d.s. n mom, n neu) through P qeghnilen current flows through; svargas'a
 ghentuzdlen my boat is leaking; k'elis ghini el vegh dadetech' the spruce bough poked through
 the bladder; yechutl'a ghenituk' ch'q'u ey yegh dnalen he cut a hole through his belly and flew
 through him; ghidesha qatl'ah vegh inalghel ha' fangs were stuck through him on the bottom
 P+egh##i+d+D+ (a.d.s. gh mom) striking at but missing P shegh iduttutl' he kicked at me and
 missed; shegh idnuljeq' he tried to punch me but missed
 Grp. 3. other lexemes
    ts'iłghetna (n.) twenty lit. 'one person' i.e. ten toes + ten fingers
    tahqegh'a (n.) I clay lit. 'the one next to the under water area'
    tahqegh'a dudlik (n.) clay pot

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                     6

     k'eghenen (n.) stick-game partner on the same side
     qeghet dalts'ina (n.) neighbors lit. 'the ones next door'

➡eh 2nd person plural subject see hen, hin, eh, nh


eł, 'eł, ł² *əł, *ł /with-instrumental/   /√ⓐ::PP¹‴/
     Den regularly has this as a vowel-initial syllable the ł² instrumental noun suffix is placed here;
  see the ł¹ causative is historically related
  Grp. 1. Postposition with
     P+eł, -'eł, -'ił (p.p.) with P, using instrument for P comitative and instrumental when without a
     pronoun the stem is glottal initial, 'eł used in many nominalizations of the type, ‘ 'with it one
     VERB' in U -qa is also used as an instrumental meaning with, instrument for but qa this is not
     used with personal pronouns sheł qinash talk with me; izin eł with a gun; sheł niyu he
     arrived with me; yeł gheyuł he is walking with him; nełch'indaqna beł itighiłyesh you will
     save your relatives with it; qunsha ghini nał qisil ground squirrels disappeared on us; tali eł
     nghanshnik I went with the waves; qaniłch'eŷi ił tanshu O I went with the wind; vargas ił
     t'eyił'an he is doing it with a boat
  Grp. 2. common for nominalization of instruments
  P+eł VERB+vsf (n.f.st.) with it one VERB
     veł k'ilkedi (n.) pliers lit. 'sth. is grasped with it'
     veł qeltlihi (n.) paint brush lit. 'with it area is painted'
     veł k'deldesi (n.) scale lit. 'with it sth. is weighed'
  eła, idi'eła (enc.) I and also, and then q'u idi'eła qevqen ghun t'qełni and then they said this to
  their husbands; quyeshi nihdi eła t'anch'q'u chidełt'ix ey yan he always kills beluga and
  everything; hełch' idi'eł hvegh nuydnalen łu and that evening to them he flew back; ndu'ilgguk
  idi'eła hteh yeh hnił'an he went in and then he looked around among them
  P+eł O+t+VERB (a.d.s. s mom) fall1ing, toppling P yeł k'taztsatl' he chopped it down; ch'vala
  eł dnaztsatl' he chopped down a spruce; k'eł tałqutl' he chopped sth. down; yada eł q'u k'eł
  tułqeł? what will he chop anything down with?; beł ntałqutl he chopped mult. trees down
  Grp. 2a. insturmental nouns
  ł (n.f.sf.) instrumental, instrument for forms instrumental nouns and a few derived verb roots
     tl'ił (ins.n.) rope lit. 'instrument for binding' from tl'i bind + ł
     tsi'uł (ins.n.) pillow lit. 'instrument for keeping the head in position' from tsi head, 'o classify
     compact object + ł
     hał (ins.n.) pack lit. 'instrument for packing'
     k'uł (ins.n.) snowshoe footstrap lit. 'instrument in opposition'
  -ził (ins.n.) tube, pipe, larynx zi tube-shaped
     veł (ins.n.) I sleep lit. 'his/her instrument' ve 3rd person
     tał (ins.n.) mat, mattress lit. 'instrument for reclining'
     hchił (ins.n.) bridge, fish weir lit. 'instrument that is built'
     nał (i.) moving nomadically, nomadic band
  Grp. 2b .NOUN+ł; but STEM-ROOT uncertain
     chuł (n.) Chinook wind
     duł (n.) firewood

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                  7

      cheghjił (ins.n.) I sheghjeł Il ice chisel the meanings of jił jełare uncertain
  Grp. 3c. implied instumentals (ł has deleted) evidence via Koyukon cognates ..i-n ges I fishing
  hook ł-suffix is implied from Koy. term geyhtl ≡
  sex (ins.n.) caribou fence cf. Koy. tleyhtl
  Grp. 3 . derived verb roots with ł
  l, ł (v.f.sf.) movement of NOUN uncommon, creates derived verb roots forms derived verb root
  from an instrumental noun; rare, only attested in these two roots
       O+Ø+tl'il to braid O from tl'i tie
       O+G+ł+ghał (conv.) bundle, bale, package O qeluzhun łiq'a zełghal I bundled ten fish

(n)en *(n)ən /sg. human relative/         /ⓐ::ENC¹/
  (n)en (v.enc.) sg. human relative suffix, the one who VERB ezdunen the one who is sitting,
  staying; yin k'i zdunen shin he is the one sitting-EVID; ghidu'en the one who was
  sitting; dnasdu'en the one who got hurt
      duhdełdihen (n.) teacher lit. 'the one that teaches'
      tak'enelashen (n.) priest lit. 'the one that baptizes'
  (n)en (s.cnj.)
  (n)enh (n.sf.) sg. person
  n (sf.) sg. person
      shin, din, (sf.) 2nd component enclitic Gunhti ch'anigen ggwa shin that child is small
      EVID; SS2005 Yin k'i zdunen shin he is the one sitting-EVID; Vada din? who is
      that?; asking about one person

➡et at a place see t, dQ specific


(y)i *(y)i /non-human relative enclitic/    /ⓐ√::ENC¹/
    can appear in derived roots
  (y)i (v.sf.) non-human relative suffix (sg. or pl) y is inserted when i follows a vowel
    zduyi the one (animal) that is sitting; ghidu'i the one that was sitting
     chinaggi (n.) sickness
     dunidi (n.) the upriver one, wind that blows from an upriver place
     qeltleghi (n.) paint, linseed oil, varnish
  CiC (v.sf.) customary non-human relative suffix forms many nouns, i can replace the stem vowel in
  nominalizations tach'enił'iy dipet; cf. tach'nił'u we are holding it (net) in the water, tach'nił'uyi
  → tach'nił'i taz'in fishtrap; cf. taz'uni object in the water, taz'uni → taz'in qiz'in razor
  clam; qiz'uni → qiz'in
  iyi (v.enc.) I VERB by oneself possibly with the (y)i relative suffix shiq'u tgheshliłiyi I'll do it
  myself, on my own; yen dyiluggiyi, yen dyiluggi shi he did it himself; vinuhdghełdełiyi I'll put
  wood in the stove
  (PP) VERB+(y)i (n.f.st.) with it VERB is done several common patterns neldegi, be'ał
  nech'edeneldegi pencil; lit. 'with it something is written' ndenadledegi writings

i¹ *iˑ, *yiˑ /inside/   /√::AN¹‴/
    presesented as two related entries, also see yi¹ inside

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                    8

  -i (a.n.) inside, inner duyeh hdi biqilan underside it's got an inside, a tunnel
  i (c.) inside
      P+idi, idu (a.n.) interior of mouth
      -inazdli (n.) guts, insides
      biqilani (n.) water pipe
      viqidin (n.) one hole kayak lit. 'that which has a hole inside'
      viqilani (n.) pipe, tube, tunnel lit. 'that which has an interior'
  -iq' (a.n.) on the inside of P
      viq' (a.n.) muzzle of gun
      biq' qach'eltałi (n.) sleeping bag lit. 'we lay down inside it'
      hviq' ggih'a (n.) intestinal worms
      -iq'di (n.) OI heart
      viq' niłeni (n.) water pipe
  -ich' (p.p.) towards the inside of P hyich' duhdałne' they made him mad; vich' duhdełne' I
  make him mad
      P+ich'a (p.p.) I more than, superior to P see ch'aQ for examples shich'a qit'aynizen he knows
      more than
  Grp. 2. disjunct prefix
  P+i# (v.pf.) inside P
      P+i# (a.d.s. gh mom) into, inside P yighiyu he went into it (e.g. sack, car); vinuk'eshnił I am
      filling it; qighiyu he went into enclosed area (room); Joe yighiłghel he put Joe inside it
      P+yi+ch'a (a.d.s. n mom, n neu) out from inside P yich'ahdaniłen he hollowed it out
  Grp. 3 misc. compounds
      k'iha (n.) L k'eyiha IU k'eyigha OI marrow lit. 'inner grease'
      -insghedla (n.) I main artery of heart, heart string
  -inughu (a.n.) O happiness Kahtnuht'ana nuhqedetitl' shinughu qilan I am happy that the
  Kenai people are potlatching again; see ghu²Q
  -isha (n.) I-OU well, wellness unique to I, perhaps with i¹ inside nisha k'ilan, nisha k'ilan you
  are well; nisha k'ilan du? is everything OK?; hvisha k'ilan they are well; visha
  ch'k'ghighilu we rescued him; visha ch'k'ghulani let's rescue him; vishach' k'ghulan el'an I'm
  supporting him (helping him)

i² *� /transitional/   /ⓐ::vpf+/ in the inflectional zone
  +- (v.pf.) transitional; perfective-negative mode
  i+STEM+ł+[voi] (if.s.) perfective-negative mode nch'u iyul he did not come; nch'u k'i'elqeł I
  didn't eat; nch'u shi'igh he didn't see me; nch'u shi'igh he didn't see me
     i+gh (if.s.) transitional-progressive ighetq'eł it is turning white; nighelgeł he is getting scared
  n#i+ (if.s.) I many plural, multiple, larger number only in I, seems to be like Koy. multiple, distinct
  from distributive k'tsen nch'ilqat we (many) are eating meat; tl'ił nnidechik pl. lines are yellow
  Grp. 2. derivational strings
  i+ (a.d.s. trans) transitional, change of state or condition, turning note that the ads remaining does
  not occur in LT itgguy it turned white; isdedel it turned red
  i+ (a.d.s. finding Ø mom) yiłtanh he found her, it (animate); shgeluja nu'i'eshdlu I found my
  keys

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                  9

  i+ (a.d.s. tran) remaining ilu pl. objects are left, are remaining; yeh ts'iłt'an idu one person was
  left staying
  P#d+i (a.d.s. Ø mom) to a place ven diyu he came to the lake
  i+ (a.d.s. sem) do VERB once, semelfactive yiztseł he chopped it, he poked him; intseł chop it
  (once); itighestseł I am going to chop it once; ighestseł I should chop it once; yizt'es he cut it
  once
  Grp. 3. noun formation strings
  (k')+i+STEM+(y)i (n.f.st.) transitional noun formation, gerund formation often with k' indef
     k'izhak'i (n.) OI k'iyagi U knife
     k'itsey (n.) UI k'itseyi O iron, metal
  PP/ADVERB+(y)i (n.f.st.) that which, the thing that q'udidi a new, modern thing

i³ *u /conative/   /ⓐ::vpf+‴/ Dena'ina forms determined from languages with u, oo forms (Ahtna,
Koy)
  i+ (v.pf.) conative
  i (a.d.s. smom) pass1ing yeh izyu he passed there; sheł iziyux IO sheł iyiyush U go past me
     P+egh##i+d+D+ (a.d.s. gh mom) striking at but missing P shegh iduttutl' he kicked at me and
     missed
     OU, P+zhex## i+d">P+yesh OU, P+zhex## i+d (a.d.s. ghmom) passing near P nayesh
     idilghuzh UO nazhex idilghuzh I the fish swam past us
  i (a.d.s. dur-cona) at, towards Den. conative is not a superaspect as in Koy qeyeł k'idełtuł they
  shoot at them
  P+iqu (p.p.) I -uqu UO for, in quest of P, hunting for P lit. 'in quest of surface (resources)' a
  compound with the conative prefix and qu³ surface area niqu hnił'an he is looking for you; buqu
  tiyush go after him; hyiqu k'tełdex at him they were shooting arrows; k'iqu qel'ih the cust. hunt
  for sth.
  O+i (t.f.s.) thematized conative
      O+i+Ø+ten hold onto O
      O+i+Ø+zi° call O's name
     -izhi (n.) IO -iyi U name
  (d)#i+gh+ł/l (a.d.s. pot) potentialitive, have tendency, trait in some potentialitives , i- is the
  conative prefix, first noted in Carrier in Morice 1932 highly interesting, Dena'ina has many pot.
  forms, cf. in passim dek'ighghiłghal it peels (bark) easily; t'qinaghiłden, deqitnaghiłden he is
  handy, dexterous; k'inaghiłdlech, k'idnaghiłdlech, k'idagheldlech he knows how to
  snare; k'itnagheldets it is twistable; chik'ighel'ul he is
  playful; chik'idaghiłt'ik I chik'idaghił'ik UO he has an ability to kill game, he knows how to
  hunt

i⁴, a⁶ /negative imperative/ several multi-morphemic structures occur
  a, ilay (v.enc.) I idghay U igu O ilagi Il negative optative, don't VERB, one should not,
  negative imperative
   with ch' 1pl ch'qenaxa I ch'qenashigu O ch'eqenashidghay U don't talk; ch'ghełnuyigu
  inten don't move it and you hold it; enge qu nich'ubałigu O it is forbidden to swim!; yada
  ye'uh ggagga nih ghe 'inch'qeł'ana don't be mean to any wild animals; shech'nił'ana don't look

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                      10

  at me; nch'bayigu O don't swim; shełtun nuch'k'dulchela I don't get yourself wet for
  nothing; quch'dechedigu O q'ench'decheda I don't break it, smash it
   neg optative iy ch'niyu da hghinaxilay when we get there, don't talk; eqech' t'ghit'anilay don't do
  that when you are there; ch'uqighił q'u ki dghił'anilay ki di don't treat him badly again

i /evidential stem/    /√::ENC/
    root vowel of three-part evdential enclitics see Holton and Lovick 2000
  shi, di, yi (enc.) evidential stems
     shit, dit, yit, shin, din, yin, shina, dina, yina (enc.) three-way evidential enclitics see di²
     evidential:hearer uncertain see yi³ evidential:3rd person asserts see shi² evidential:speaker
     asserts see entries, examples at shi², di², yi³ Holton Lovick 2000
    shi shida it is me, it is really me; dnayi shida it is us, it is really us; nen dida it is you, is that
  really you?; yehin dida it is that really you (pl)?

idi
  idi (v.enc.) emphasizer, that one, which one not used with first sg. subjects nen t'inlugg idi? are
  you the one that did it?; ndahqugh ighinqat idi? how much did you pay?; yadi ghinlqet'
  idi? what did you eat?; na'a tułkun idi gheli qit'a it looks like rain; nzhah tidułkeh idi gheli jan
  gu it really looks like the snow will get deep today
  k'idiki, k'idiki gheli (adv.) too much, very much k'+idi+ki k'idiki gheshezh' O I drank too
  much; k'idiki gheshtnu I work too much; nuch'udeł ghu k'idiki gheli qenghalch'ich' da'a
  hdelch'ich' we walk around and if it (ground cover) is very dry, it makes a crinkling sound; AA-
  04 shlik'a gini k'idiki gheli qut'ana ih ch'u idenlghen my dog here if he sees people, he growls
  too much; AA-04

ini:ni¹, yni *ini /thoughts/         /√ⓐ::N-anat/
    important disyllabic root in most Dene languages
  d.f.: yni, ini, eyn, yen, in
  Grp. 1. nouns
  yni, ini (c.) mind, thoughts Dena'ina łu k'huqidudli niłk'uch' deyninqidghiłtey the D. they say
  are competent in many things they are strong-minded; PK91:202
     -iniq'ak' (n.) anger, angry a compound with -ini mind viniq'ak' qilan he is angry, disgusted

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                               11

     -ghuynisdi (n.) mind, thoughts
     naghuynisdi (n.) U chickadee (any), or boreal chickadee (Poecile hudsonicus) lit. 'our thoughts'
     ghuynisdi k'echegi (n.) computer lit. 'written thoughts' kiq'u naghi hkuk'eł'anen nunettuhen
     yeq' k'izhchex ghuynisdi k'echegi another dental assistant wrote on the computer
  -ini (n.) courage, bravery ch'u qbini k'ghila and they were brave; ghu shini k'ilanch' qit'ah
  shqiduni ni thus my bravery can let them know about me
  h'ini (n.,i.) OU in fear, afraid interesting as INC duh'ininiłdatl' they came in being
  afraid; duh'ininiłdatl'na the ones who came in with fear
  iyni (n.) U izen arrow, gun U version of arrow, gun puns on 'thoughts, mind', see ini:ni¹, yni
  Grp. 2. two types of verb prefix
  yn+ (v.pf.) thought a conjunct prefix in several themes with zen intend
      COMP yn+Ø+zen intend to, think of COMP, vuqu ch'eynizen we want it
  yni# (v.pf.) thinking a disjunct prefix in the INC position neynik'ezet he is thinking; dnigi
  nitaydaq U moose are mating; iynitazdaq IO initaydaq U they (animals) came into heat,
  became aroused

u¹ /for/
  P+u (p.p.) for P navu k'dili sing for us; nu hch'agheshla ni let me take care of it for
  you; chik'ish nu ch'tułchił we'll make a cap for you; yu ghetnu he works for him; vu
  dak'inił pour for him
  -uł'i, -uł'a, -uł'e (n.) OU -uł'e I excitement unusual compound of two postpositions u for and ł
  with shuł'a qilan I am excited; vuł'e qilan he is excited; nch'u vuł'e qghile he is not
  excited; qbuł'a qilan O they are excited

u² /kin compound/
  -uq'eya (n.) U aunt, mother's sister
  -uda (n.) older sister
    udla (voc.) I udala UO sister!
  -uzha (n.) OI -uya U nephew, neice, man's sister's child see zha:uzha

➡un food, eating see k'un in duna animal's food, forage see dun


un¹ /eat/ occurs in a few nouns, the PA verb *(h)on eat is not used in Den.
  k'un (n.) food from the verb root (y)un which is not productive in Dena'ina
  k'undet (n.) famine
  k'duna (n.) animal's food
    chu duna (n.) I beaver's feed pile
    chudyi (n.) eaten beaver food
    duni (n.) Il dry fish

     niłvunkda (n.) mother and daughter

'¹ /perfective suffix/
  ' (v.sf1.) perfective not phonemic in D. ghidu', ghidu s/he stayed; usually not heard;
  occasionally is overt and audible
  ' (v.sf1.) distributive suffix not overtly heard in D.

'² *Ɂ /noun-forming suffix/          /ⓐ\n.f.sf./
     usually not phonemic in final syllables in Den
  ' (n.f.sf.) forms nouns
      qa (n.) foot qa+'
      -lu (n.) limb lu+'

  -tsa'a (n.) man's daughter
  -ey'a (n.) man's son
     nił'ey'a (n.) son and father
     chulyin vey'a (n.) NIl black brant (Branta bernicla) lit. raven's son

'a:ez'a /mother's brother/
  -ez'a (n.) uncle, mother's brother
  -ey'a (n.) man's son
     -ey'a eł zdunenh (n.) man's son's wife, daughter-in-law
  Grp. vocative forms mother's brother
  zhala (voc.) I zhazha O lala, iy'ala U uncle! note 3 reanalyses for the vocative form

  lala, i'ala (voc.) older brother a term found in all D. dialects

'an¹ *Ɂeˑn /do; see/     /√::V-subsidiary‴/
  dur     'an 'an 'ił 'an          / 'ih
  durD    t'an t'an t'ił t'an      / t'ih
   neu      'an 'an 'ił 'an/'ih
   neuD t'an t'an t'ił t'an / t'ih
   mom 'ix 'an 'ił 'ił/'ix
   cust     'ih 〃
   cont 'en 'an 'en 'en
   rev      'ih 'an 'ih 'ih/'ih
   trans 'ih 'it 'ih 'ih
   transD t'an t'an t'ih t'ih
  Grp. 1. act, happen, do, perform
   COMP d#D+'an (op.) do, act, happen, behave, take place in manner of COMP
     nda nt'an? what are you doing?; yaghelich' shughu t'ent'an da you are doing the right
  thing; ghu t'et'an ch'q'u nik'eldex qich'a shełdaniłtuk' he kept on until as far as they can shoot
  farther he ran; nda tghit'ił dit k'tghitsał da? how come you are going to chop
  wood?; qinaghelt'ach' t'ech'tut'ił we will have lots of things to do; hey ghu iqech'
  t'qet'an winter then that's what they kept doing; Ts'anhdghulyał shtunyu ha', la'u
  t'ghet'an Ts'anhdghulyał used to hunt, he always used to do that; shełtun q'u ch'ut'an we did it
  in vain; ch'ut'ani let's do it' ^let's have sex; k'enyich' t'et'an he is doing funny things; henu
  t'et'an there is a lot of work; tey t'et'an he is using his strength; qena t'et'an there is a lot of
  talk, a controversy; ggul t'et'an there is a running race; k'enli t'et'an UO kunch'nazdli t'et'an
  I it is hailing; yada iqu t'ent'an gheli dit? what are you doing that for?; t'qet'an they are doing
  sth. (sex); t'qet'an it is happening; shghu t'ent'an keep my pace; vghu t'ent'an keep up with
  him; nihut'an he is trying for you, hustling you; k'a t'et'an it is resisting (animal is fighting in a
  trap); shiq'ech' inighet'an he wished me bad luck, hexed me; yek'uch' q'u t'et'an he acts
  different; gudałtin bidi t'et'an he is hemorraging in the mouth; uq'ay t'esht'an I am trying to
  avoid him; łik'aha eł t'et'an he is using dogs; en'ich' t'esht'an I'm doing poorly; ush at
  t'ghet'an he was using snowshoes; vinłni q'ach'it'an I we need water; venen nch'u
  t'ehtust'il I month when there is no bad weather or snow
      t'anch'q'u (adv.) I all, everything, everyone qudi ki t'anch'q'u yuyeh łhdnaldaq hqidni they
      (themselves) thought that they all were sleeping inside; Dutna guna t'anch'q'u łdnaldaq ha'
      k'ehdenłghiq' all of the Eskimos were sleeping and they were snoring


 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                               18

     OPT VERB+ni q'a##t' (coll.) trying to VERB chigheł'ulni q'at'et'an I chighel'ulni t'et'an
     UO he is trying to play; tgheshyuni q'a t'et'an I tgheshuni t'et'an O he is trying to
     go; k'dgheshlini q'at'an I k'dghełini t'et'an O he is trying to sing
  P+q'e(+d)#D+'an (op.) do, act, seem like P yeq' t'et'an he acts like him; k'elqadi q'u

'at /in, on surface/     /d√::AN/
    unique, not found in surrounding Dene languages, assume from a¹+t place
  -'at (p.p.) UI -n'at O in, on the surface of the O form -n'at is interesting nuti at k'iqu l'an he
  hunts in, on saltwater; ush n'at tazu O he went on snowshoes; hetl n'at qeytałtan they took him
  in the sled; hetl n'at ztan he is laying on the sled; yashik n'at ezdu O he is sitting within the box
  (not on it); ben at ezdu he is sitting on the lake; ben at tutghelggesh I will run into the
  lake; hdasdyu Chiyałchin Nila Vena at the giant Ch. started walking on Iliamna Lake

  brava I guess it's true

   l+'atl' (conv.) deadfall trap falls, collapses

  a deadfall
     L'ałi Vena (p.n.) Lachbuna Lake, 2.3, 129 lit. 'deadfall collapses lake'

'atl'², ('wł) *Ɂʷeˑtł', Ɂʊł /plural swim/        /√::Vmot (SgPl)/
   mom 'ał 'atl' 'eł 'ał/'ił
   cont 'ał 'atl' 'ał 'ał/'ił
   prog 'eł
   per 'eł 'atl' 'eł
   G+l+'atl' (mot.) pl. swim, float on surface; U sg. swims il'atl' they arrived
  swimming; nughel'eł pl. animals (ducks, beavers) are swimming around; niqa'il'atl' they swam
  ashore; nghel'atl' U they, he was swimming; sheł tuk'ul'atl' some animals ran into water (e.g.
  moose)
   caus. nyedeł'ał he is floating pl. O (e.g. logs); nishdgheł'atl' I floated some logs down
  river; hkayidgheł'atl' I floated them into a bay
     beł naqak'il'atl'i (n.) OU yearling seal (Phoca vitulina) lit. 'with it they swim ashore'
   yis#k'+n+l+atl' (mot.) wade in snow k'ninyisch'k'nal'atl' I he waded in snow; high language

'ats Ɂeˑtth /step/      /√::Vsuc/

   prog 'es "
   per 'es 'ats 'es 'as
   cont 'ats "
   sem 'es "
   cons 'ets "
  dur 'as 'ats 'as 'as
  Grp. 1. step, hold foot themes (all dialects)
   O+G+Ø+'ats (suc.) step on, trample, kick O, touch O with the foot yeghi'ats he stepped on it (for
  awile); yeghi'ets he stepped on it consecutively; yiz'es he stepped on it once
   Ø+'ats (mot.) move the foot, step ełnen z'es he steps, travels all over the country; nini'ats he set
  his foot at a point; hughi'ats he is holding his foot up and out; qani'ats he is holding his foot
  against it; beł qani'ats O hold it with your foot; niłq'a qani'ats he is standing with legs spread
  apart; ts'iq'ez'ats his leg is extended out straight; k'enini'ats porcupine stepped on sth. (the
  kill); AW "If a person kills a porcupine he will burn it. After this he would pull off the thick skin. If the feet
  are red he is sure to kill a big animal"

'en¹, 'u *Ɂən, Ɂo /beyond/      /√::dir‴/
d.f.: 'en, 'u, 'uh, 'uch', 'un
      basic directionals with 'en,'u are uncommon in Dena'ina, but many lexemes including time before
   past
   en (adv.) off, away apparently does not take directional prefixes en qanilghuł U-L move there
   endi (exc.) scat, get away
   -'u (dir.) out in the open, nearby no examples with prefixes na'en ch'ghednu' we are working
   outdoors
       na'unen (a.n.) a different area na'unench' na'eł q'antudush he will go with us to a different
       area
   'u (c.) outdoors
       uch'en (a.n.) outside, outdoors
       Dghili Uch'en Ht'ana (n.) UO Stony River Dena'ina people lit. 'people outside the mountains'
       uch'eh (a.n.) U towards outside uch'eh q'ana'eshdish I cust. walk around outside
   -'u (p.p.) I off surface of P be'u nuk'iłqat erase sth. off of it; ve'u nuk'niłchut peel it (potato),
   peel skin off of it; nił'u nik'ila he is butchering sth; i'unuk'ezah he erases sth. off of it; sh'u
   nuk'ghizhegh he scraped sth. off of me; qeytnazyit hnuyu ghini łu ye'u ntikahdałen they
   started to chase them when the animals started moving out
       na'un'i (n.) O storm
   -'uch' (dir.) from, away, off, from outside ey'uch' niqadalghel he turned around (toward me)
   'un, ye'un (adv.) away, in direction away from seems not to take the full set of directional
   prvfixes ye'un ti'ilgguk he went out the door; ye'un dakaq' ghu łu hdazchek' out doorway it got
   too small; ye'un hch'adalen it fell awayex ye'un yetałt'eq'; k'iztin ye'un tałt'eq' he threw the
   boneit away; nunigi ghin unch' veł yuyuł ch'q'u that fog was moving with him over the
   hill; yenił'an hnuyu yudeq unch' venuqeł'i qiyulgguk he was watching her as up over the hill out
   of sight she walked
   -'ut (dir.) away, off, nearby at a specific place du-, yu- prefixes do not appear ey'ut ghu ch'vala
   hnidghi'u there is a spruce tree out there a little ways; ts'i'ut straight out
       ey'ut ni'iyiden (n.) U expert hunter, outdoorsman lit. 'one who stops at places'
       ey'ut qenq'a, ey'uh qenq'a (n.) menstrual hut, shed, outbuilding


 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                29

  -'uh (dir.) away, off in an area ey'uh nugheł'esh they (herd) are migrating out there; ts'i'uh
  tayu U he went straight out
  -egh'ut (p.p.) at a place off from, nearby P bida ghun ghe'ut ghu san his friend is standing out in
  the open; ey ghu hvegh'ut gu ki łu dezdu there near them he was he was sitting on a bush
     nagh'utna (n.) strangers
  nagh'ut dalts'ina (n.) I uh dalts'ina U neighbors
     ey'utna (n.) U bogeyman, woodsman, evil nakani; neighbors
  -egh'uh (p.p.) in an area around, near P hvegh'uh tiqin t'etni łu jit shla a wolf howled near them
  a little bit
  Grp. 2. temporal and adverbial extensions
     en'ugha (proc.) L not yet with negative inflection cf. elugh in other dialects en'ugha
     dnilghech it hasn't boiled yet; n'ugha nizhelch'il I haven't closed my eyes ye
     ADV qegh'ut (adv.) TIME before, after tl'adu qegh'ut O q'ayteh qegh'ut I q'ayt qegh'ut
     U day before yesterday; lit. 'next to yesterday' tałqun qegh'utda IU tsukan hegh'utda
     U niłqun qegh'utda O day after tomorrow
  nił'u (adv.) separating, taking apart nił'uni'ilyu it was butchered; nił'u nuk'ila butcher, skin and
  cut it
  dnu'u (v.enc.) L shall we VERB used with optative, apparently derived with 'u
  beyond nunuch'ehdnusex dnu'u ch'iłch'en gu? shall we tear it down on one side?; chitdatda
  tl'unqin tl'ineh qegh nch'ghulkit dnu'u yuyeh qech'? shall we take you inside through the eye of
  grandma's needle?
  un-, unh (c.) the most, primary
     unhyaqah (n.) U unhzah O unhtsah I first
     unhzahden (n.) leader
     -'unhchikda (n.) IU -'unhchukda O -unqeghyeh -chikda I great grandmother
     -'unhcheyakda (n.) great grandfather
     -'unhbach'a (n.) great aunt
     unhłik'a, unhtsahdi (n.) lead dog
     unhq' vushlat (n.) overcoat
     -'unhkela (n.) U little finger lit. 'littlest brother'
  undat (adv.) last undadi the last thing; undaden the last person; undat Dena'ina nutihna
  be'uqa qghila łu last Indian two wives he had they say
     unht'uhdi (n.) IO unqayehdi U underwear lit. 'one farthest below'

'et¹ ('ut) Ɂˑɑd, Ɂaˑd /throw fabric/    /√::Vclass-throw/
   mom 'ut 'et 'eł 'ut / 'it
   rev 'et 'et 'et 'it
   O+G+Ø+'et (mot.) move throw flat, flexible, fabric-like O quickly
     yetaz'et he threw it (paper, blanket); veydegh k'ighi'et he threw it (blanket) off
  him; nuyghi'et he threw, put it (coat) down on the floor; nuk'i'ut put sth. down; niłyighesh'et I
  put it in with another; e.g. in letter in envelope galeq shlaq'a n'ut give me some paper; beł
  qak'ighin'ut O cover it up; ch'da yach' naqadi'et turn over the blanket; heł'i yedghi'et he hid
  it (paper); yach' naqani'ut turn it (paper) over; tuqeyde'et they are soaking it; nuyilkit ch'u
  tuqeyde'et nuqeydalggesh he took it and they put it back in the water and stretched it; nalqeni
  atuqeyda'et qeyenesh qeyenesh they put it in warm water and they pulled it, and pulled it; ch'da
  desdghi'et he threw blanket on the fire; ch'da desdghesh'et I threw the blanket in the
  fire; q'aydit'et he pulled blanket over himself; shq'ak'di'et he pulled it over me; benutnghijash
  t'enuyut'et it healed up (formed) onto him, and he put it in his pocket; ch'u ey gu deqizdlu gu
  yach' qak'ni'et and here open up the curtain; veq' duch'yetu'eł we will cover it (with cloth, tarp,
  etc.); hnutdghesh'et I hung it over pole, string
   with D nununt'ut put your coat on; degh k'ut'et he moved sth. (blanket) off
  himself; dek'ut'et bark pops off easily; k'isen ghun ułchena ggits'a t'un ch'adintun ch'u yi
  hdadat'et woman took out that angelica (Aleut celery) she put that on herself (as clothing); ch'u
  yeł kut'e k'ut'et and he put it inside his clothes; q'aydit'et he pulled blanket over
  himself; benutnghijash t'enuyut'et It healed up (formed) onto him, and he put it in his
  pocket; shq'ak'dit'et he pulled sth. (blanket) over me; q'aydit'et he pulled it (blanket) over
  himself; nvit'a nu'u ninunit'ut put your sleeve over your eyes again; nvit'a nu'u ninit'ut' ha'
  qatsiniyił' ł ch'etni ki łu put your sleeve over your eyes and your head against the house someone
  said
   INC#G+Ø+'et (mot.) move INC fabric-like object nuhał'ighi'et he undid the snowshoe
  webbing; k'inihnidaghi'uyi yeyesquq' qeyeł nitałdin'et they carried him on a mat to the side of
  the fish drying tree; she put the skin by the water to (get it wet again); HD-swb

'et² /when/ source is unclear
  nda'et (int.) IO da'et U when (past, cust.) nda'et q'u hch'aninnu when did you move out
     nda'et da (int.) when (future) nda'etda q'u nuntnghel'ił when will I see you again
  etq'u (adv.) L-NOU always etq'u la'a ezhi qilan it is always cold

  G+Ø+'u (stat-clas.) compact object is in position
    z'un compact O is in position (e.g. hat, trap); nli z'un there is a steambath; nli ł'un he has a
 steambath; k'qan ez'un (beaver) lodge is there; k'iłkedi z'un trap is there; stsila kiyiq' qu q'u
 daz'un hq'u it (word) is on the tip of my tongue; k'usht'a is'e O it is not there; qenq'a
 qiz'un house is there; ch'uqughuł qiz'un it is bum weather; yaghelich' qiz'un it is good

 qitu'uł tomorrow we will have bum weather; tałqunda qighishin na'a qitu'uł tomorrow it will be
 nice weather; tl'ił naz'un coiled rope is there; shch' hghaz'un I am in debt; k'qan dez'un his
 lodge is elevated; nantl'iłi dehdał'un bee has an elevated hive; k'duyun yinaz'un he has a full
 beard; łqitnaz'un it is hidden; tuz'un it is in water sticking up, e.g. island; taz'un it is under
 water; beq'ayhnaz'un they ignore him; nch'u i'un trap is not sitting there; degh dghi'un it is
 hanging up; lach'gheli q'u veydaz'un ch'q'u ghizhel he really hollered with all his
 might; ndahqugh z'un? ndahqugh qiz'un? how much does it cost?; 5.00 z'un it costs five
 dollars; chayi q'ach'ghi'un I we need tea; tuntaz'un it is break-up; niłq'daz'un they are

  Grp. 1.
   G+Ø+'u° (ext.) linear, elongated, rigid object extends; geographic feature (ridge, stream)
  extends ni'u it extends to a point; idi eła yutsit qeyan gu ni'u vejex vayighalnik and then down
  below an open ridge extending there is covered with caribou; yinił'u he is keeping it (boat) in
  place; nudani'u pole extends across; qini'u it extends over an area (e.g. house); tets' dakaq'
  ghu niłghu gheli ndunqeydał'u ha' they had spears sticking in the doorway next to one

'uh¹ /fern root/     /√::N!-bio-veg ∀⇨/
     NWC diffusion, Chugach Alutiiq arpaayak fern root Term only found in Ahtna and Den. and in
  Babine-Witsuwit'en in B.C. But also found as Ɂaax ̣ in Nisga, Tsimshian, and Gitksan, Risby Kari
  1989: Appendix H:3 common in Dena'ina, may be a rare Dene archaism
  uh (n.) fiddlehead fern root (Dryopteris dilatata) AAD 1990:78 "rare term in Ahtna, cited in Pinart
  1872 but not known by any of the numerous elders queried; ...; interestingly this word has been
  borrowed by the Chugach as arpaayak"
   from N. Chickalusion 1980:36 qenq'eya k'dilanh uh quqelish wherever there are alders, they dig up
  fern roots; yethti uh ghini q'ahyiłdeł ndalyuh then they put the fern roots into the cavity (in
  embers) standing them end up; dichin na ghuna qeyech' itujesh those people who are starving
  can save themselves with it (fern roots)
      uh t'una (n.) various ferns

     ch'it'un (n.) I natural tunnel, overhang on stream bed

   l+'en (ext.) dens extends an obsolete verb related to 'un² den
     l'eni (n.) U large porcupine den in rocky place a group of porcupine live there
     Venen K'el'in (p.n.) hill along creek which runs into Kijik Lake, 2.3 142 lit. 'hillside with dens'

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh

                                               b, v, w


   Dene Lexware dictionary comparisons:   AAD1990: b 96-108, KAD2000:b 74-102, LTDD: b, m w, MTDD:
  b, m; YMM92: b 40-70
    This section combines stems that begin in v, b, and w. The general trend in the Dena'ina dialects: v
  prominent in Inland; b prominent in U and O w prominent in Seldovia; w is also used often in
  Kenai. Roots in all dialects are listed with v in roots, stems sets and themes; Roots, stems confined
  to O or U are listed with b. Loan words are listed as pronounced in each dialect, as here there can
  be contrasts between b or v

v¹, b¹ *b /third person sg./      /ⓐ√::pro/
    classic PD affix-root based on current analysis
  d.f.: d√: veł, ves, vis
  v, b, u, w (pf.) his, him, her, it, its, third person sg. object/possessive pronoun velik'a IIl belik'a
  O ulik'a U welik'a S his/her dog
  b (v.pf.) him, her, it, third person sg. object of bound postpositions vedgheshchet I quit it/him/her
  v (v.pf.) third person non-human (animal, inanimate) subject prefix kadałtin t'eveyeł'an he has a
  hemorrage; lit. 'blood did to him'
  qeve (pf.) I qu OU their, them, third person plural object/possessive prefix qevlik'a,
  qulik'a their dog
  qeve (v.pf.) them, third person pl. direct object qevenetl-'anh I see them
  qeve (v.pf.) them, third person pl. postpositional object qevq'eza alongside of them
  v- (n.f.af.) AF→NOUN, it, s/he, 3rd pers. sg creates several interesting derived roots
     veł (n.) I sleep lit. 'his-her instrument'
     ves (n.) skin toboggan lit. 'its skin'
     vił (n.) large game snare lit. 'instrument inside it' b-i-ł 3rd sg-inside-instrument
     vada, bada (int.) who v+d 3rd sg. ~reflexive

➡ba, va grey, off-white see vu°, va³

    mainly used in OU and rare in I

   per    bash       bak bash bash~besh
   cont   ba'        "
   cust   bish~bix   〃
   neu    ban        ba' ba' ba'
   prog bał
 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                 56

   concl ba       ba' ba' bah
   Ø+ba OU (mot.) sg. swims on surface uncommon in I; replaced by odd theme tsi#gh+l+kit
    niban he arrived swimming; sheyesh idnastban he came by swimming and missed
  me; err. q'angheshbesh O I wish I could swim; nuch'ubałi ch'nelden I don't know how to
  swim; dujemi tayeh nughebał the loon is swimming underwater; k'usht'a ntustba' there is no
  swimming; niłdeq niban he swam across; nughebał he is swimming about; k'usht'a ghesbal
  da O he should not swim
      per:         I             P               F                O
   1s: nugheshbał q'angheshtbak q'antgheshtbash q'angheshtbesh
   I swim around UO
     nugheshbał I am swimming around; q'angheshtbak I was swimming around; q'antutbash he
  will swim around; shan haq'u q'an'eshbish I swim in the summer; ben'at q'anch'etbish we
  cust. swim in the lake; k'usht'a nuzgheshbal I am not swimming around; shqiłdih
  q'angheshtbash ni teach me how to swim
   cont. neba UO he is swimming, keeps on swimming; n'eshba I keep on
  swimming; nch'bayigu don't swim
      tak'ełbasha (n.) OU betach'ebasha O adipose fin
      nba' (n.) O nbał U swimming ability nbał ldenen U nba' ldenen O he knows how to swim
   ">O+n+Ø+ba (mot.) swim after O
   ł/l+ba (desc.) be able to swim, like to swim dghiłba'en he is a good
  swimmer; pot. dghelban O dghiłban U he is able to swim, it (lake) is swimmable; pot.
      Naqatsiba (p.n.) OU fictitious, humorous place name FS "said to children who keep asking 'where
      are we going?'" lit. 'the head is swimming in a circle'
      ts'ilbi (n.) OU ts'elvi' I porpoise, Dall's porpoise likely from swim
      ts'ilbi ht'in (n.) OU dolphin, Pacific white-sided dolphin lit. 'like a porpoise'

bash, vazh *beˑshʳ /portable stone/       /√::N!-lith/
   perhaps portable stone; not used as plain stem Den, At. use lach cook by boiling
  vashla (n.) bashdla O ulu, semi-lunar knife lit. 'little stone'
    vashla viluhi (n.) ulu sheath
  Vashla Yetnughizhelitnu (p.n.) creek south of Tanalian River; 2.3 14 lit. 'stream in which Vashla
  went hollering' Vashla is a man's nickname
    chabash (n.) OU tavash L stone skin scraper, ulu
    bash t'uts'a (n.) U a sharp rock, used as a knife lit. 'cutting stone'
    bashtl'ech'a (n.) U clay lit. 'sticky stone'
    bashda (n.) coal an inportant compound, shared between D. and A.
    Navash Tuyana (p.n.) stretch on Kuskokwim R, below Stony R; 1.1, 6 lit. 'our stone straight
    stretch'
  vazh'a (n.) type of stone only in a few place names in Lime Hills area; cognate in UK
    Vazh'atnu (p.n.) North Babel River, 1.6 55 lit. 'type of stone'
  bay'a (n.) U quartz, agate meaning uncertain; seems cognate with vazh'a cf. also Lime vazh'a,
  Ahtna bezyae possible cognates

    yhis is the common noun for 'sleep' in Alaska Dene; not used in UO

veł² *bəł /lips/      /√::N!-anat/
    perhaps also a derived root
  -duveła (n.) lips
     Bel' (ps.n.) name used in Quch'nushjun story perhaps related to lips, used as an
     incantation "Bel' eldu yit'anni" ggagga i'un qiyghiłt'eq' "Bel' has quit" and he threw the bear
     from there; "Debel' eldu yit'ankdi!" "His own Bel' has quit!"; SP-HN addressing this bear
     with Debel' implies that the bear has Bel' as his own son.

bis² /large mammal stomach/          /d√::N!-anat/
    a derived root from vi- inside +s skin; not in other Dene langs.
  k'vis (n.) O k'visa I large animal stomach; stomach storage bag; a moose gut I seal, beluga,

     tied, with it it floats-stomach'

  ekdu my stomach aches

   mom-U: bus, buts' bes bus
   neu        bits', buts' 〃
   dur        bis 〃
   G+l+vits' (dim.) be round, circular, cylindrical, chubby; with n+ be spherical; with d+ be
  cylindrical dghelvits' IO dghelbuts' U it is round, circular, he is chubby; daghelvits' it is

  tsilbesi (n.) O bufflehead duck (Bucephala albeola) lit. 'round head'
   G+l+buts', vits' (mot.) roll, revolve, spin nuyułves he is rolling it around; ilvits' it rolled to a

  buts'a (adj.) U round
     k't'un buts'a (n.) U aspen, little cottonwood lit. 'round leaves'

   -vugh, -bugh (a.n.) edge belu bugh edge of knife (all around blade)
       tuvugh (n.) I tubugh O tabagh U shore, beach

       lit. 'beach people'

       trail important indication of long distance travel
       nuti vugh (a.n.) seashore,
       yusdi vugh (a.n.) fireside

       chu q'uvugh (n.) I platform in front of cache

vu°¹, va³ *baˑ°, beˑ° /off-white mix/   /√::Vdesc-color‴/
    ablauting root, the verb theme is not commonly used in D. cf. also d√ vaq' vomit also related is
  verb to mix, see vu°², va⁴
  va, vu, vaya, vuyi (c.) white, grey, off-white very common in compounds

  bajegha, k'washggatl'a (n.) hard white punk

     ggeh
     hey hvaya (n.) winter rabbit
     shani hvaya (n.) summer rabbit

  ba (c.) grey, off-white
     deba (n.) U Dall sheep primarily an Ahtna word, as nuji is more commonly used in U as well as
     in OI.
     deba chiga (n.) U low grade ochre lit. 'sheep's ochre'
     k'evajeghetl'a (n.) I basqetl'a U k'washggatl'a O hard white birch fungus (Fomes
     applanatum) each form is a separate compound
     chebay (n.) U trout, non-salmon
     -chibaya (n.) U calf of leg an interesting extendion, fish to anatomy
     dghelay chebaya (n.) U Dolly Varden trout (Salvelinus malma)
     hvu, qevu (n.) colorful sunrise, sunset hvu dnasdneq the sky color faded (into darkness)
     łch'ivay, łch'ivaya (n.) whirlwind1

  batter); nunhk'niłvex they are mixing it; tunilvu' water got murky (O), it became color of half-
  tanned moose skin (U); nuhnilvu' (ground) is soft, murky, swampy; nuk'ni'ełvix I am mixing
  dough batter; suy ch'u chu andaz'i eła nuqeyeniłvex they mixed sand and beaver hair with clay
  (to make a clay pot); nuyniłvey, nuynałvey he mixed, stirred, kneaded it

chagh *chʳeˑgh /cry/       /√::Vn-anat/
  durO chegh 〃
  durIU chegh chagh chegh chegh \ chih
   mom chih chagh cheh chih
   Ø+chagh (op.) sg. cries, mourns
    ghichagh UI ghichegh O he was crying; q'ak'ninchih you quit crying; q'ak'nichagh he quit
  crying; yeq'achegh he is mourning her; yegh chegh he is crying about her; q'anlcha sheł
  hughichagh a fox cried out (mating call) at me; ichagh he cried out; dujemi ghichegh the loon
  was crying, hollering; ch'anichagh he burst out crying; it (dog) cried out; sign of bad
  luck hughedechagh, hughetchagh he cried for himself; hunughetchagh he cried for himself
  over and over; tazhchagh he's starting to cry; tayeshchagh I am starting to cry; yen shugu
  veq'a'ehcheghen he is the one you're crying for; jani k'ghichagh he cried all
  day; jak'ulchegh he (baby) has cried himself out; t'ighiłchegh, tighiłchagh,
  dighiłchagh I k'idaghelchegh O he tends to cry; dighiłchagh he tends to cry; eshchih I cust.
  cry; ntałchegh he began to make you cry; k'usht'a ezteshchegh he is not about to cry
     dzeł yicheghi (n.) U dus yicheghi O white-tailed ptarmigan
     tiq'a chihi (n.) Il crab
     tiq'a chihi (n.) Sl American black oystercatcher (Haematopus bachmani) lit. 'it cries in the mud
     flats'
     qizhcheh (a.n.) mourning place
     Ch'chihi Ken (p.n.) ridge S. of Mt. Susitna lit. 'crying ridge'
  chegh (n.,i.) tears, grief, crying nucheghdulkeł he is going about crying; cheghhdelghesh they
  pl. are crying; hnucheghadulyaxen he is the one who will make them
  cry; ducheghdaghełch'ik' I cry easily
     chegh k'eli, k'q'achegh k'eliga (n.) mourning song
     nucheghta'a (n.) tears

chan² *tseˑnɁ /Graminae/       /√::N!-bio/

chen⁶, cha∞ /alien lower CI people/    possible substrate morpheme∞ cf chen smell, chen lowlands;

  Ułchena (n.) people of Lower Cook Inlet, Kodiak,"Aleuts" a lower Cook Inlet people (Kodiak,
  Chugach) who are main adversaries in ancient war stories Radloff Scheifner 1874
  Kadjaker variously translated Sugpiaq, Eskimo, Alutiiq, Aleuts, Kodiakers Osgood 216: "any
  Eskimo people" PK (Kari Nb18) "Seldovia Fitka (Balashoff) once said it may mean 'u wife, ł with"
     ułchena gizha (n.) O ułchena ggaggashla U Stellar's jay, blue jay lit. 'Aleut camprobber'
     ułchena ggits'a (n.) O angelica lit. 'Aleut celery'
     Ułchena Yits'a (p.n.) Caribou Hills and Ninilchik dome lit. 'Aleut Ridge'
     Ułchena Huch'ilyut (p.n.) Pt. Woronzoff lit. 'where we dug up the Aleuts' a war site
  ułcha (c.) compund form
     Ułcha Dghil'u (p.n.) Roadhouse Mt. lit. 'Aleut mountain'
     Ułcha Tudeł Vena (p.n.) Old Schoolhouse Lake lit. 'Aleuts come lake'
     ułchena ch'enlahi (n.) Sugpiaq Aleut stick game
  ułchaq'a (n.) drudge, poor man, serf Osgood 1937:134,220 possibly related to chaq'a corner; PK
  translated this as an 'unworthy woman'

     shdechik it is marked, it is yellow-orange; k't'un didechik the leaves turned

  colors; ch'enłq'ena dnidechik the bunchberry is orange; dudechik it had been yellow; tl'ił
  nnidechik pl. lines are yellow
      qadelchigi (n.) U mallard duck (Anas platyrhynchos) lit. 'the one with yellow feet'
      didechigi (n.) yellow-orange color

      turns yellow'

   O+G+Ø+chik (suc.) paint O with ochre, make mark on O, to tatoo O, to write O

  marks; huydghichik I quydghichik UO he marked, scribbled all over it; hudnudechik she

  shnazhichex da chix eł when you paint me. Then I'll tell you when you have painted me with

   write examples k'ghichik he wrote sth; nhk'ighichik I they wrote sth; k'ezhchex he made a

  sand); tali dełchuh the storm made waves; bich'ak'nanłchuq' I cleaned it off (as feces from
  intestine); hq'ntiłchuh scatter them out; hq'entanłchuq' I scattered them
  out; hq'enudetghełcheh I will scatter them again; vich'anełchuq' I sprinkled it (salt) onto
  it; nyah hqatadiłchuh U ice pellets are floating down
     laq'a dadenlchuq' I filled my hand up; laq'a datidighelcheh I will fill my hand up; laq'a
  daydnalchuq' he ate a handful of it; laq'a dadghelchuh ni let me fill up my hand; laq'a
  dadghelchih I filled my hand up
   INC#D+chuq' (mot.) granular or amorphous INC moves
      tsaychuq' (n.) I rockslide
   G+l+chuq' OU (dim.) to be obese, sloppy, soft, swampy
     t'elchuq' I am fat; t'i'ilchuq' he got fat; dghelchuq' he is obese; t'qughelchuq' it is swampy,
  soft; qenighelchuq' he sank in soft substance
  chuq'i (adj.) chubby (nickname)

   O+G+ł+chut' I (suc.) cut, slice O quickly with knife not too common in U, not used in O
     yeqa k'niłchut she cut his foot off; yeq' k'niłchut' he cut it (foot) off; nda'ich' tsa yeq'
  k'niłchut tsadi deqa ghini yedni łu how I hope he makes up his mind to cut off his
  foot; izhełchet I cut him once; yiłchet he cut him, sliced it once
     ve'u nuk'niłchut cut it off with knife; ye'u nuk'ghiłchut' he cut it off, peeled it with a
  knife; yeniłnighiłchut he cut into joint; galduxa unuqełchut he peels potatoes; unuk'enłchut I
  am peeling it; huhudulchut' he cut himself up in a bunch of places; hunuhudulchut' he cut
  himself up again; yada kadałtin nlani ch'ayiłchet ch'q'u yenish idesex he cut off what was
  bloody he hit him with it; hunulchet, nuhulchet he cut himself again; stl'ustla
  shqach'aziłchut cut my pants off; naqa veq' ch'k'ełchet ch'iłch'en we cut off our feet on one
  side; yetsiq' nuk'naniłchut' she cut his head off again; yetsi ki ch'ananiłchut' she cut his head
  off again; chitdatda gun yegh niyu ha' q'uyehdi yetsiq' k'naniłchut' ha' devashla
  eł grandmother went to him and then she cut off his head with her round knife; q'uyehdi q'u ki q'u
  ghil'i k'kalt'aq' k'ehniłchut ha' ey nihyeniłggat then they cut off a different fish tail and they laid
  it down there; huydghiłchut he cut it up for cooking (small animals); he sliced meat off
  it; ik'niłchut' he cut it off; degguna k'niłchut' he cut his hand off; degguna titułcheł he will
  cut his hand off; yeq' nuk'niłchut' he cut it off again; yeq' nuk'ełchet he cust. cuts it off
  again; yiniłnighiłchut the seal cut into his joint; ch'ayiniłchut' he sliced it off; łiq'a shu
  ch'aniłchut cut a piece of fish off for me; janghełchut' I cut it and split it in half; yeghiłchut' he
  sliced it repeatedly; huydghiłchut' he sliced it into pieces; hulchet he cut himself
   P+pp#O+G+gh+ł+chut' (suc.) slice, cut O (attached limb, body part)

ch'¹ *ts'ənɁ /toward, since, as/          /√::PP¹,ENC-space/time‴/ mainly a suffix in Den., very versatile
  Grp. 1. as PP
  -ch' (p.p.) to, toward, until P shch' qghinak he spoke to me; dghilich' taznu he went up to the
  mountain; tubughnench' dnalnen he flew toward the beach; shch' niyu he came to
  me; niłqundach' k'qdetulił O they will sing until tomorrow; hech' gheyuł he is walking
  towards you guys; nach' gheyuł he is walking towards us; q'uyehdi ts'iłt'an qevch' tazyu and so
  one man started out toward them; vench' tazyu he walked towards the lake; vech'
  danich'ey towards the wind; nch' htudech'el from you will be life, brightness; uhch' di'u he
  craves fern root; guritsach' desh'u I crave chicken
   qech' towards a place qech' tazyu he walked towards a place; qenq'a qech' gheshyuł I am walking
  towards the house; ch'q'u yutsen qayeh ghu qech' hdehtashjay łu then they started running
  down the mountain toward the village; Huch'altnu qeł qetnih qech'hdi shtunuqudedił they
  would go out to hunt as far as Swift River; beqatl'uh qech' to the bottom; a toast ey ghu
  ventudeq ghu qech' ghu qizteł there he kicked off from the back wall
     qech' ni'i (n.) O July lit. 'towards month' shorter days, back to winter
     jan qech' (adv.) sometime today, during the day yi ghuda jan qech' gu negh shtutghelggex
     ha' that's why some time today I'll go away from you; jan qech' gheshtnu I worked all day
  Grp. conjunctions, subordinators
  ch' (n.enc.) since, from, until, toward tl'aduch' ghutnu' he worked until yesterday; tl'aduch'
  k'qdedli they have been singing since yesterday; niłqundach' k'qdetulił O they will sing until
  tomorrow
  ch', qech', qech'a (s.cnj.) since, until gini inten negh nutgheshjuł qech' hold onto this until I
  come back to you; nutiha yił q'ahdulnen ghesh'an qech' two days have passed since I saw
  him; shan ghu qtułahch' he will work until summer; tułkunch' gega tghet'ił I will pick berries
  until it rains; yuh zidu nutgheshjuł qech' stay until I return; nutiha jani qiłchin tahbił taniłkiz
  qech' it has been two days since he set a net; nutiha tsi qiłchin łuq'a ezhdlach qech'a two hours
  have passed since he cooked fish; nutiha yił q'ahdulnen ghesh'an qech' two days have passed
  since I saw him; idi eła yeh qech' hdilghuzh and then in that direction they started hollering; gini
  intenna nutgheshjuł qech' keep this until I come; itihqun betukda begh tuyuł ch'u ninya ya
  chitułnił qech' he keeps him there while his master comes and until he kills it for him; nutiha heyi
  q'u hdałchin tughanshnik qech' two years have now passed since I came
  Grp. suffixed to directionals
  -ch' (sf.) from, to a direction suffixed to directionals and demonstratives deggech' up, in upwards
  direction; yuhch' qaduk'dgheshnesh I heard sth. from inside; yunich' from an upriver

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                               113

  direction; dunich' from upriver; yuduch' from a downriver direction; duduch' from
  downriver; yuneqch' from an upland direction
  ch' (sf.) -ly, manner of forms many adverbs
     dach' (adv.) thus, this way
     qenq'a hdyidehch' (n.) roof top of house
     qilch' (adv.) badly
     niłk'ech' (adv.) zigzag, back and forth
  ch' (v.enc.) in the manner of, the way of yech' duyuq ghilach' in this way he is a
  chief; ghednuch' qit'a'ideshni I know that he is working; t'anch'q'u ghu t'esht'ach' gheli sheq'
  tiłchił you will make everything exactly like me; łik'aggwa nlanch' while it is a
  puppy; nutsighighelkitch' nch'u t'qit'a it does not appear he can swim across; dach' łik'aha
  niqghilakch' this is the way they used to raise dogs; qilach' they were that way; ghełach' I was
  thus; ghilach' he was thus
     yaghelich' (adv.) nicely yaghelich' hva ninuhdalen it turned good weather on them again
     -ch'kdi (v.enc.) one should have VERB used with optative verbs k'ghilqat ch'kdi you should
     have eaten; yeh q'u ghidu ch'kdi you should have stayed there; yeh tgheshu ch'kdi I should
     have gone there; ghednu ch'kdi qit'a ideshni I know she should be working; nuhughiltah
     ch'kdi you should have bathed; nhin q'u chik'a t'ił'ih ch'kdi k'eldunteh you guys should
     gather wood sometimes
  uhch'q'u (cnj.) I and with, along with with nouns shgech'a uhch'q'u shchik'ich'a my gloves
  and my hat
  ch'q'u yehdi, ch'q'uhdi (cnj.) and then
  iqech' (adv.) I yaqech' OU thus, similarly iqech' ghu nch'u k'i tak'estsex as before he wasn't
  even paddling; yaqech' ch'a t'htunił it will be the same; yaqech' qenash he talks that way, like
  that; yaqech' nuch'ułtałigu don't act that way; nudgheltan qech' gudikidi yaqech'
  t'shqiluq since I was born this is the first time such a thing has happened to me
  ch'adach' (adv.) U ch'aduch', nda'ich' O what, how see ch'a
  ch'qeyan (adv.) always

ch'a⁴ /ques. what, how, why/
 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh

  ch'adach' (adv.) U ch'aduch', nda'ich' O what, how ch'aduch' qilchin shnuqulnek he

  ch'at'nt'a? how is your arm?; ch'aduch' kisht'a k'ghinlqet how did you eat so much; ch'a

     ch'aduhu, ch'ahu (int.) how come, why ch'ahu yehtiyush why are you going
  q'uch'a, q'ushich'a (exc.) that is all, it is all gone q'u t'ent'a shich'a that's the way it is

ch'a¹ *chʳeˑ /female/ *chʳeˑ
d.f.: ch'a, ch'ey
   -ch'a (c.) woman, female uncommon
   łich'a (n.) bitch, female dog
   -yuch'a (n.) U woman's daughter
   yuch'a (n.) Il shuch'a NL sow, female black or brown bear
   vach'a (n.) man's sister, aunt, girl friend also see bach'a
       chishch'a (n.) female of paint tribe
       nuhch'a, nuhch'ey (n.) female of the nuhzhi clan
       tulchin ch'a (n.) woman of the tulchina clan
       ggahch'a (n.) woman of the crow clan
       -tełch'a (n.) U man's sister or female parallel cousin “Yagheli, ugha dach' q'u niłtech'a qa,
       niłtech'a qa ghuna qubeni'ehdał, nakiyitiłał." “Good, come this way, those two clan sisters,
       those women, you go to them, they will be our clan leaders.”; SP76-clan
   ch'ay, ch'ey (n.) plural females
       duch'ay, duzhch'ay (n.) Eskimo woman
       gashtąn ch'ey (n.) white women

     yich'a (exc.) I watch out! be careful, danger also said when coming out of steambath

     k'ich'anda kishi, ijanda ki shi (exc.) I what might this be? here is the riddle the 2nd form was
     used in A Wassillie 1978 but the 1st seems to be the most accurate

   with me; uch'eh ł'an he is avoiding him

       ch'igh (n.) OU ch'ighet U obscured, hidden object, timbered spit on lakeshore or

       away; ch'ighe katnatan he was laying on his stomach facing away; Ses Ka'a q'ay ch'ih
       hghaztunt 'Big Ridge' is obscured; huch'ih tiq'ets'dnaz'iy an obscured silhouette; ch'ih
       ninuk'niłdał close the curtains; nutiha ch'eh k'ghugheł it is two minutes from the hour

       extends obscuring Niłdink'et'a

     k'ich'ighi (n.) riddles A Wassilie 1978, AW's classic collection of D. riddles Pulu AW78:1 "the
     Dena'ina people... used to delight in telling each other riddles... Riddles are of special interest as a form of
     creative expression for they challenge the listener into active participation. They stimulate the brain to
     bring all its resources into the resolution of mysteries presented by their creators."

ch'en *ts'ən /from, side/   /√::PP¹‴/ CHK
  P+ch'en (p.p.) side of P vech'en zdu he is sitting at his side; ey gu yech'en gu yusdi vuna gu łu
  deseydenghichet there next to him by the fire she put it in the fire; gu hch'en ezdu he lives on
  this side; ghu hch'en ezdu he live on that side; yunch'dich'en ezdu he lives on the other
  side; qaniłch'eŷi ank'uch'enh batanshu I went against the wind; Tsałt'eshi nuti ch'en
  hghaztun 'Black Stone Axe' points toward the ocean side; k'etnu hch'en side towards the
  river; ven hch'en lake side, the side of the lake; tinitun ch'en roadside, trail
  side; ni'ich'en O sunny side; t'uch' hch'en shady side

     uch'eh he went outside; uch'eh q'ana'eshdish U I go outside customarily; cust.

  nu'iłtlaq' he is wet behind the ears

  Grp. 2. number six

     k'uych'en'i n'u (n.) U March lit. 'sixth month'
  Grp. wings, fins note reanalyses

  'side limb', reanalyzed from lu+ch'en limb+side
     łuq'a k'ch'elna' (n.) O łiq'a k'ch'enla I fish fin

  ch'en (i.) wings of bird dalika'a deyes ghini yayidalchet ch'u efdi nch'elna ghini ch'ennelya he
  wrapped him up in that bald eagle skin and then he flapped those wings; ch'u k'idiki

  CHK
  NOUN + ch'en'i (n.f.st.) object on the side
    -luch'ema ch'en'i (n.) fourth finger

  ch'en (sf.) side with drectionals

    yinch'en (a.n.) O yunch'dich'en I the other side, across side
    yudehch'en (a.n.) top side, top
  Grp. 4. stick game, hand game

    Dena'ina ch'enlahi (n.) stick game with four people, no singing, and no betting with four sticks,
    three white and one burnt or black
    ułchena ch'enlahi (n.) Dutna/Aleut hand game

ch'eŷ, ch'ey¹ /wind blows/      /√::Vext-env/
  mom ch'ix ch'eŷ ch'ex chix ch'ix
  neu ch'eŷ "     /     ch'ix
  rep ch'ex ">
   rev ch'ex ch'eŷ ch'ex
   ł+ch'eŷ O Ø+ch'ey UI (ext.) wind1 blows, be windy yuduch' nich'ey it is blowing from
  downriver direction; qanich'ey UI qaniłch'eŷ O wind is blowing against it; nishghich'ey wind
  is blowing down the river; nudnanich'ey wind is blowing across; tu'ich'ey wind is blowing over
  water; qidaghich'ey wind blew inside; qighedazhch'ey it is drafty (doorway); dunich'ey wind
  blew inside; beghdinch'ex air is escaping through a hole; tu'iłch'eŷ wind on water; detałch'eŷ a
  light breeze started; k'itaych'ish U it is windswept; k'chan anich'eyi wind on grass; qa'ich'ey
  yiq' ki łu t'qit'a łu it looks like there is wind; tu'ich'ey iq' qit'a łu it looks like the wind was
  coming on the water; vadich'ey ey ghu the wind blew on him there; nuti'at nt'i qanich'ey on the
  saltwater it was blowing well; beghdiłch'ey air escaped through it; qenihniłch'eŷ it blew
  in; duniłch'eŷ wind blew in; k'idghiłch'eŷ the wind blew into it (ravine); ketnazhch'ey wind is
  coming up the lake; nutnanich'ey wind is coming across the lake; yada hnuduch'eshi
  gheli U just like the wind (the bear was going); q'a'idch'ey UI q'a'ilch'eŷ O the wind
  stopped; qtunich'eŷ the is wind in the area, the sound of rustling wind; qadich'ey there is a
  draft; qeydełch'eŷ there is a stronger draft; ch'eghe nuhdich'ey, ch'eghe ndazhch'ey drafts, the
  wind is changing directions; tl'idazhch'ey there is a fair wind, a tail wind; shtl'idazhch'ey I have
  fair wind (behind me); sht'uy dnazhch'ey I have a head wind, i.e., a wind is blowing at my
  chest; ut'uyudaghits'ey the wind is blowing against him, he has a head wind; bin'ich'eŷ the wind
  blew into it; ut'uyudaghich'ey the wind is hitting him; vin'ich'eŷ he is floating (inflated with
  wind); bindałch'ix it blows through pipe-like O; k'idghiłch'eŷ wind blew into an enclosed
  area; qadinłch'ey wind hits it (house); qandałch'eŷ wind hits pl. O; bandałch'eŷ wind hits pl.

ch'uk' /swing/
   dur ch'ux ch'uk' ch'ux
   mom ch'ux ch'uk' ch'ex ch'ux

    enłch'ux swing him; niqadulch'ex it swings around; yeghiłch'ux he swung
  him; nughelch'uk' it swung down (as meat roasting); niłgguna qełch'ux they swing with joined
  arms; ch'ulch'uk' we swung (yesterday); ch'ulch'ux ni let us swing!; ch'tulch'ux we will

  niłqelch'ux I they are playing teeter-totter

     lch'uxi, valch'uxi, heyalch'uxi (n.) I swing

     kaych'uk'a (n.) old-style shirt with pointed tails in front and back lit. 'tail hanging'
     qeyet'u nulch'exi (n.) I rope or branch swing for leaping streams
     nughelch'uk'i k'tsen (n.) IO meat barbecued on suspended line
   INC#l+ch'uk' (op.) INC waves, sways, wags, shakes lulch'ux he swings (waves) his
  hands; lughelch'uk' he did swing (wave) his hands

➡d specific place affix see den¹, t, de


D /D-classifier/
  D+ (v.pf.) classifier
    D-effect (n.d.s.) passive nasdedes it is twisted
    P+pp#(#)(d+)D-effect (n.d.s.) indirect reflexive yu tanat'un he is baptized for him
    nu#D (n.d.s.) iterative, again nu'idyu I nu'iju OU he returned, came back
    D (t.f.s.) derived intransitive
    INC#D (t.f.s.) derived intransitive with incorporate
    INC#d+gh+D (t.f.s.) derived intransitive with incorporated body part
    INC#D (t.f.s.) derived transitive with incorporate
  D+root (v.f.af.) D-class. verb root formation rare, appears in V-initial roots
     q+G+D+un (ext.) hole, trough, cavity, cave, space, U-shaped object, landform extends sdin'a
    baqidun there is a hole in the log; qanqisdun there are pl. holes, caves
  D (c.) noted in compounds with D-classifer function
    dnes (n.) U bearberry lit. 'being alive'
    dnigi (n.) moose lit. 'one that is aware'

d¹, t'¹ /proverb prefix/ disjunct prefix
  d# (t.f.s.) proverb prefix, thus main trait of the proverbs or subsidiary verbs
     COMP d#D+'an (op.) do, act, happen, behave, take place in manner of COMP tey t'et'an he
    is using his strength
     d#O+ł+'an (op.) do, gather, collect, have O d, t' vary conditionally dech'eł'an we do
    it; deyił'an she is doing that; ełnen t'eł'an he is working the ground, digging
  d#i+gh+ł/l (a.d.s. pot) potentialitive, have tendency, trait in some potentialitives , i- is the conative
  prefix dek'ighghiłghal it peels (bark) easily; t'qinaghiłden, deqitnaghiłden he is handy,
  dexterous

d² *d /d-qualifier/ many functions, much haplology
 Grp. 1.
 d (v.pf.) d-qualifier
    d+ (n.d.s.) gender chik'a daz'un a block of wood is there
 d (c.) d-compunds forms various nouns, reflecting d- gender functions
    dken (n.) stick, wood
 d (v.pf.) d reflexive, medio-passive
 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                 133

     d+ (n.d.s.) benefactive, for oneself
     hu+d+D-effect (n.d.s.) reflexive hudnil'an he is looking at himself
  Grp. 2. strings
  areal noun d+i (a.d.s. Ø mom) out into an area ben diyu he went in the lake
  P+e#d (a.d.s. gh mom) leaving P behind, releasing P bedghichet he quit it, released it
  d (t.f.s.) thematized gender dazq'en it is buring; fire, burn, smore themes always have d
     d+l (t.f.s.) onomatopoetic, be noise, sound of delch'ich' there is a squaky sound
     INC#d+l (t.f.s.) go making noise in manner of incorporate
     du#d (n.d.s.) verbally, oral noise, speech niłduch'ehdeldih we are teaching each
     other; iduhdełdih he is teaching her; shduhdełdih he is teaching me
     d+l (t.f.s.) move oneself
     O+d+D-effect (n.d.s.) benefactive, for one's own benefit yedalkit he has it stored (food) for his
     own benefit

d³ /d-connective/ several functions may be detectable; cf. also n⁴ n-connective for parallel word
formation
   d, de (c.) meaning uncertain in various compounds, not a verb prefix
      dgeł (n.) U partition
      dghak, dak, -dghaga, -dghak'a, -degh'a (n.) coat, garment see ghak:dghak, dak
      dghayi (exc.) careful, negative imperative see ghay$
      dna, dnay, Dena (n.) person, people see na::dena

d⁴ *d /reflexive/      /ⓐ√::PRO/
d.f.: d, de, di
   de- (pf.) oneself, one's own used with nouns and postpositions, aslo see hu reflexive direct
   object detukda his own father; dich'atl'ughalghel he took off his pants
   deh- (pf.) their own dehtukda their own father
   d+ (v.pf.) benefactive, for one's own benefit in the same slot as the d-qualifier; we present it here
   to feature it
       d+D/l+VERB (if.s.) do VERB for one's benefit k'dalkit he is keeping his own food
   udiq'u, udihdi (pro.) by one's self udiq'u chihudalyuq he killed himself; udihdi łuhtsitghulkeł
   lay? does he want to swim by himself?

da¹ /thus/    /ADV Q/
  dach' (dem.) thus, in this way cf. yach' that way łik'aha dach' qeyghundghiłt'a they used dogs
  in that way; dach' łuq'a uqughel'ih thus they used to fish; gu dach' this way (pointing); yeh
  dach' that way (pointing); dach'q'u yitsa it is always barking in this way; ch'q'u yeh dach'
  chench' dach' venuqeł'i qeyghiyu then in that direction down the hill he went out of sight
  dach'u (adv.) only, merely dach'u ezdu she is just sitting
     ch'adach' (int.) U ch'aduch' O how, why ch'aduch' kisht'a k'ghinlqet'hu shegh
     ninyu why did you eat so much when you came to me
  undat (adv.) last, final undat beq'estsiq' quqeyedghuqish they sew up the last seam; undat
  łuhneshyu the last time I walked around; undat chinudghełt'ik'i ishi it is the last time for my
  lwring (bears)

da³:kda, tda /dear/       /r√::ADJ/ This is a Dena'ina reanalysis expansion of the PA adjective *gəd
great; see KAD:188
   -tda (adj.) I -kda UO dear, shabby, no good!; kinship suffix of affection
   Grp. 1 lexicalized in the following terms: father, mother, grandmother, grandfather
      -tukda (n.) UO -tutda I father
      -cheyakda (n.) OU -chyatda I grandfather
      qelchenikda (n.) U stink beetle lit. 'dear smelly one'
   Grp. 2 emphasis affection, endearment, shabby chitdatda grandma, old lady; here there is a
   double enclitic nuntaldaqkda I overslept!; yixkda silly me!; dnikuhkda it's way
   expensive; bingha qenq'a ghu ts'itl'uhqinił'uhkda the end of his older brother's house was
   sticking out over the bank shabbily; nelnikda the shabby steambath
      isankda, k'isinkda, idzu ki (exc.) U riddle call note three versions used in U sources uncertain

da⁴:ada /affection/ This is very common in Den., but seems to be absent in otrher Dene languages
  ada (p.adj.) dear, loved one; affection, love ki nagh tunghintnik da ada ił nagh tghiyuł again to
  us if you return friendly to us you'll come; un'i ada ndgheshni ni come let me speak tenderly to
  you; ada ił nagh qinqudatl' they returned to us with love; ada dnaghelnik love is fun; ki
  quht'anach' hdanutghelcheł ch'u ada shqidituni I'll return again to the people and they'll care for
  me
     ada qa (n.) pretty ones (pl.)
     adada, adadashla (exc.) dear one a common pet name

    rattling noise larch is limited to I dialect and is uncommon
    ch'da q'ediłkidzi (n.) Ty bed of razor clams lit. 'blanket extends upon'

da¹⁰ /when/
  da (enc.) in the future, when forms various adverbs hey da next winter; shan da next
  summer; hełch' da tonight
     tałqun da, niłqun da (adv.) tomorrow
     nda'et da (int.) when (future) nda'etda q'u nuntnghel'ił when will I see you again
  da (s.cnj.) when, if CHK too many yagheli ch'taznu da q'u it is good if we go; yuqush
  qudghighel da nuydełq'un it was before daybreak when I built a fire; tuq'i jani ch'adintun da
  Kahtnuh tgheshuł in three days I will go to Kenai; ełkun da k'usht'a ezghetgheshtne O if it
  rains I won't work; tanshnu da tahwił tatgheł'kes O tayeshyu da tahvił tatghełkes I if I go I

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh

  will set a net; łuq'a ghelqet da chitgheshnah if I eat fish I will get sick; tułkun da gega
  tgheł'ił if it will rain, I will pick berries; yagheli qilan da gega ch'intuya if it is nice we will pick
  berries; gini heyi ch'u ki heyi da k'u this winter and also in other winters; k'ghelqet' da
  qatgheltał when I get done eating I am going to bed; ugha qighishin sht'a k'adilyax da' qełni
  łu now you sing really good, he told them; ki nih hq'u da some other time; tałqun q'ut'un gheli
  da yu ghu tghigheshnex da' next day in the morning for sure I'll start off; yagheli qighishin sht'a
  tghił'ił da ki yagheli if you treat him all right, it'll be good; enli qighila da this coming

     niłqunda qegh'ut da (n.) day after tomorrow
     da (coll.) one should VERB occurs with optative mode nughidgha da you should pack (would
     that) it back; yetghu ghidu da you stay there; shegh ghidu da come and see me; q'anaghilzet

  daq'u if that guy calls you don't go; daq'u nułtu shughu ch'edyuq that is for life or death what
  we did
  dahdi, yedahdi, yedaqdi (adv.) then, at that time niłqun da dahdi' tgheshuł tomorrow I will get
  there then; nutujuł yedaqdi neł nuqtulnek when he returns he will explain to you; ki

  away; nqayeh nunindyu it dahdi Ts'anhdghulyał ghun Jilughun chiydałyuq sheł dghini
  da when you get back to your village he should tell about me that Ts'anhdghulyał killed Jilughun
  da'a (adv.) I still, remains as da'a gheshdleh I am still smiling; qut'an shla da'a
  dnaghelghazhen shla łu the little person is still the little round-faced one
