 Grp. 1. plural move, fall, drop
  G+Ø+daq (mot.) pl. objects move independently, fall; pl. vehicles, move; sg. rope-like object
 moves independently tazdaq pl. boats started off; ninidaq pl. vehicles stopped; nughedeh pl.
 boats, cars go about, move; nughidaq pl. O fell down; k't'un elugh nudesdagh O the leaves
 haven't fallen yet; ch'elqadi nalaq'a ghidaq we received some foods; ten ta'idaq ice went
 out; tudghidaq (logs) fell in water; niyniłdaq he put pl. O into a pile; łi vedendah glacier
 calves, chips off ice; galeq shegh ninidaq I got lots of mail; yach' qitihdeh deghu qech' ch'u
 sh'aq'ach' da yeh da'ilnesh ch'u iti'ihjes before you fall down go there in my absence and you
 will save yourselves; hvaydidahi what they have; nagh nitudeh it comes in (every AM) to
 us; nitudeh it will come (clothes, mail, etc.); shduk'ghidaq pl. animals got away from me; ey ki
 q'u k'duhghidaq and again they got away from him; qeydughidaq they got away, escaped from
 him; ts'iłten qevla q'aghidaq they received bows and arrows; niłteh ch'ach'itdah I now and
 then we people divided ourselves up, separated among ourselves; without realizing it, high
 word yagheli baydalgi k'inudedeh qilan it is a good harbor; hch'anidaq pl. boats came
 out; k'inughetdaq pl. boats went back into harbor; ey ki q'u k'duhghidaq and again they got
 away

  O, t+n+l+daq U, ł#t+n+l+daq I">n+t+l+daq O, t+n+l+daq U, ł#t+n+l+daq I (stat/op.) to

 ha opposite from there they stay at night (bear hunting); gheljayi qighishin ch'adelzexi eła yehdi
 qighishin heyegh ni'adeł ha with a good shining moon coming out and they can stay there for

z-degh¹, s-da *th-daˑ /point of land/      /√::N!-geo ∀/ A rare root, z- indicates this is a fossilized
verb, common in Ahtna sdaa bend, point on river; also in U as sda
   qezdegh (a.n.) IO qeydegh U point, peninsula on river bend or on the Inlet
      Qezdeghnen (p.n.) OI Qeydeghnen U Kustatan qezdegh nuzu O he walked around the
      bend
      Qezdeghetna (n.) IO Qeydaghetna U Kustatan people PK91:212-213 Qezdaghnen
      Hdults'ihts'
   sda (a.n.) U-IO large river bend direct borrowing from Ahtna in U; related to qezdegh peninsula
   also cf. Ahtna sdaa peninsula LT thedo timbered area
      sdakiq'e, sdaghuyq'e (a.n.) U point of river bend
      sdates (a.n.) U portage across river bend
      sdaghene (n.) U large river bend

degh² /burst/
  sem deh "
  cons degh "
   G+l+degh (suc.) burst, crack get a cramp, a spasm ten naldeh ice cracks; zeldeh I got a cramp,
  a spasm; stsen naldeh I got a muscle spasm; shgguna eldeh my hand cramped; shqakena
  eldeh my leg cramped; zeldeh my arm cramped; itigheldeh my arm will be
  cramped; nneldeh I got cramps rep.; gudałtin nuldehi k'ughuda in order to avoid a blood
  clot; qevenlana ildah I they got cramps
   qiy'unch' qełdegh U he makes speech, talks importantly
   G+l+degh (mot.) amorphous substance (fog, smoke, breath, scent, flavor) moves QQ
   INC#G+l+degh (mot.) amorphous INC moves

des¹, duz *d�ˑth /heavy/       /√::V-dim/ dialects diofferences are noticeable
   neuOU des 〃 dis
   neuI  duz 〃
   tran    diz
   G+ł+des, duz (dim.) be heavy dghiłdes UO dghiłduz I he is heavy; chik'a daghiłdes the
  wood is heavy; dench'iłdes we are heavy; deghełdes O I used to be heavy; de'iłdiz it got

  weight as it; sh'ighiłdes as heavy as me; yighi yinghiłdes he is as heavy as it; ts'ełq'i ghini
  inłdes you are as heavy as that one

  it; hugheldes he weighed himself

des², s /into fire/   /d√::vpf#/ interesting prefix, likely d-gender wood, plus s, nes ahead, into fire
  des#, s# (v.pf.) into fire
     IU, s#d UO">des#d IU, s#d UO (a.d.s. gh mom, z neu) into fire, building fire deszdu he is
     standing in fire; k'tsen desdulnen meat fell in fire; chik'a desdnaz'un block of wood is in
     fire; ch'da desdghesh'et I threw the blanket in the fire; ch'u yaghelisht'a
     desnuk'ehdelish and they put more wood on the fire; desdaztun log is in fire; sgulida
     desdghiłggeł you will put the frypan on the fire; destl'uhqeydghighiłtl'it he dumped it onto the
     fire
  O seems to have either des, s qałnigi destnutnghełik ki ghilna niłtu I put rocks on fire for other
  people; PK91:2 en'ushna niłtu neli snudghełik O I would fire up the steambath for the old
  men; PK91:2, PK contrasts des, s
   des#ł+zen I (desc.) be a good, kind person may be related to the previous desghisen he is
  kind; des-hghisen they are good people; nch'u desnqisen they mult. are not good
  people; desnuqulzen they were kind; desghinsen you are kind; destusin he will be kind; k'tsi
  eł'ani łik'a desghisen my lead dog is very kind

di¹ /a thing/   /d√::ENC/ seems to be from d specific place + (y)i inanimate
  Grp. 1 demonstratives, adverbs, nouns
  di, hdi (enc.) this, there, the one gundi this one; guhdi ch'en on this side; guhdi ch'en k'etnuh
  nelgguk I ran to this side of the river; yet htl'eghhdi after that time; niłqunhdi the next
  day; idahdi hello friend!; ida'inahdi hello friends!
     yedahdi (adv.) at that time
     ndahdi (int.) how many
     yedahdi (adv.) at that time
  idihdi, yethdi (adv.) O yethti, ch'q'u yehdi, q'uyehdi U idi'ił hdi I and then, next
  Grp. 2. found in stem-position in various nouns
  di (c.) the one
     k'inghildi (n.) I low bush cranberry, mountain cranberry, lingonberry (Vaccinium vitis-
     idaea) said to mean 'eaten by animals' P.R. Kari 1995:67-68
     k'eq'ebughdi (n.) U closest birch to the perimeter of the camp
     yeghedishla (n.) I ghedishla U black bear lit. 'little one over there' an interesting tabooistic
     innovation in UI dialects
     yeghdishla k'inazdliy (n.) black bear intestine baby bottle
     ghedishla ts'iłq'a (n.) U brown bear lit. black bear's partner
     veq'di (n.) plus nutiha veq'di twelve; lit. 'plus two'
     veq'edi (n.) covering, tarp
     tseldi (n.) UO funnel basket on fish trap
     ta'adi (n.) IU soaked dry fish
     t'uyedi (n.) dentalium necklace
     k'qaydi (n.) duffel grass
     unht'uhdi (n.) underwear
     -iq'di (n.) heart
     dukaq'di (n.) door
     yulq'adi (n.) window pane
     tl'uhdi (n.) saucer tl'uhdi shlaq'a diłggat get me a saucer
     tiq'adi (n.) seafood
     yusdi (n.) fire, open fire, fire place
     yunch'dich'en (n.) O yinch'idich'en I across, the other side ch'u yunch'dich'en Tikahtnu
     q'anqghetnish and they would go across the Inlet
  di, -hdi, -qdi (enc.) then, next, tag question, how about ntukdahdi how about your
  father?; nenhdi how about you, and you?

di² /evidential-hearer uncertain/    /d√::ENC/ analysis in Holton Lovick 2000, three sets of three-
part derived enclitics may be derived from d reflexive prefix
  di- (enc.) evidential enclitic, hearer uncertain Holton Lovick 2000 "they indicate speaker
  acknowledgment that first-hand evidence is lacking. Rather, they mark the hearer as the source or
  potential source of information. They predominantly occur in sentences with second person subject
  referents."
     dit, dida (enc.) inanimate evidential, possibly, one guesses, it seems shegh ninyu dit [I guess
     you came to me; Balluta and Evanoff 2005 q'u lach' dini dit? are you kidding? 'is what you
     say true?; nda eht 'a dida? what's wrong with you guys?; łiq'a nlqat dit [I guess you are
     eating fish; gindu chashga di? is this a cup?; vada galeq'a di whose paper is this; yadi di,
     gini yadadi? what is this?; ginihdi yada di? how about this one?; zisan dit? are you
     standing?; shi shida? is it me?; vada inlan dida? who are you?; nen dida? it is
     you?; endaha tiyux dit? where are you going?; ghidnu dida? are you working?; coffee
     tudghinlu dit? did you make coffee?
     din (enc.) sg. human evidential vada din? who is it?; ghunhdi Ralph din? is that
     Ralph?; nda nt'an din? what's wrong with her?; nda'ih du nch'u vech'ałet-'i'e'n din? how
     come there is no one with smoke coming out of it?
     dina (enc.) plural human evidential vadna dina who are they
  di, dit, dida (enc.) tag-question

➡ndi O where see t'i:nt'i what in ndi ndaja hdi? and where is your sister? see t'i:nt'i where

du¹ /orifice/
  -du (n.) lip, beak, chin
    -idu, -idi (n.) mouth
    -idu qelyana (n.) O palate
  du- (c.) opening, orifice, verbal
    -dubunu (n.) lower lip
    -dubeła' (n.) upper lip
  dughushq'a (n.) L labret holes
    duni, -dun'i (n.) O -duyni U -duyun I beard, whiskers bedun'i qul O he doesn't have a
    beard; bedun'i qdilan he has a beard
    tałin dun'i (n.) O k'duyni U baleen lit. whale's whiskers

  ch'du, shdu (adv.) oddly, comical, funny shdul'an he is comical; shduk'deli he sings funny

du² U /tendency/ This seems to be a vowel initial root with a D-classifier.
   dun -
   q+i+t+D+u (stat.) have an attraction, a plan hbech' qitasdunh t'qut'ik to him who starts to
  receive things it happens; łuq'u yadi qilan hbech' qitasdunh t'qut'ik everything there is starts to
  be attracted to them

du³, dit /interrogative/ seems to be a particle or enclitic ant can function as a prefix
  du# (v.pf.) how, what du'it'a? how are you?; du'it'anh? what are you doing? QQ
  ADD; dughiłduz how heavy is it?
  du#z+STEM+ą́ (n.d.s.) don't VERB forms negative commands, with only a few verb
  themes duzit'an don't do it; dudzine don't talk
  du (enc.) OI da U yes/no question, tag question marker du? is that so?; yagheli du? is it good
  (greeting)?; q'u du? are you ready?; nda tazehni du gu t'cheł'an dih q'u what's the matter with
  you guys doing that time like that
  duh (a.n.) at a place, in an area both dem. and enc. gu duh gheyuł he is coming here; gu duh
  shu ez'un shu it is probably around here somewhere; batna ghuduh idiłqet ask anybody around
  here
     ghuduhdi (n.) those łik'aha ghuduhdi k'net'ish those dogs are stealing
  duh (enc.) forms interrogatives
     ndahduh (int.) O where ndahduh ch'aninyu where are you from; ndahduh tihdał dit where
     are you going
  dughu (adv.) due to, for a reason yadi dughu? what is the reason?; yin dughu dach' t'qijuq due
  to him it happened this way

du⁴, t'e¹ *daˑ, *deɁaˑ, *t'ə /downstream/     /√::DIR (suppl)/ interesting suppletive roots
d.f.: du, dut, duh, duch', t'e, t'eh, t'ech'
   -dut (dir.) at a place downstream yudut at a place downriver; dudut at a place
   downriver; qudut the next place downriver
       Dusdu Bena (p.n.) Tustumena Lake lit. 'downstream peninsula lake'

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                  166

     -ghdut (a.n.) UOIl downriver from P sheghdut downstream from me; naghduch'en the side
     downstream from us
  -duch' (dir.) toward downstream yuduch' toward a place downriver; Tsałt'eshi yuduch'
  hghaztun 'Black Stone Axe' (ridge) points downstream
     duduch' (adv.) O yiduch', yuduch' U south wind1 lit. 'from downstream'
     duduch' (n.) I yuduch' U west wind1 duduch' niłch'ey a west wind is blowing
     duduch'eni (n.) southwest wind1
  -duh (dir.) in the general area downriver yuduh area distantly downstream
  du (c.) downstream
     dutna (n.) Yupik Eskimos
     duch'ay (n.) I duzhch'ay L Eskimo woman
  -t'e (dir.) toward downriver with motion verbs yut'e łuhneshyu I walked downriver; yut'e'
  nuhghunix they would go downriver in a boat; dut'e in a nearby downriver direction; yut'e' yi eł
  nik'un'idlagh he swam downstream with him; dghili yut'ech' nanilu the mountain extends
  downstream; ts'it'e heading straight downriver; tit'e with the current
     yut'etna (n.) Lower Cook Inlet Dena'ina lit. 'downstream people'
     Dut'ech' (p.n.) Bristol Bay
  -t'ech' (adv.) toward downriver with motion verbs t'ech' yach' towards river direction, down that
  way
  -t'eh (dir.) in a downriver area with motion verbs dghili yut'eh nanilu the mountain extends
  downstream; yut'eh nuhghunix they would go downriver in a boat

du⁵ *doˑ /elevated surface‴/       /√ⓐ::AN¹/
d.f.: du, dugh, duh, duq', da, dun
   Grp. 1. areal nouns
   du, da (c.) elevated surface
       -duquq' (a.n.) on top of, on elevated surface yedendlach veduquq' ghu tlegh shla qilan she
       was cooking it on top there oil little there was
       tiduquq' (a.n.) on water, water surface
       duves (n.) gunwale
       hdakaq'di (n.) UL hdukaq'i O door lit. 'elevated stream mouth' implies doorway faces a
       stream
   -duq' (a.n.) elevated surface some variation in OU with t'uq', <t'u²Q ulu duq' flat side of
   knife; yuduq' k'ilchin it was made up in the sky; yashik duq' edge of box
       -qanduq' (n.) top of foot
       k'duq' naqadaz'uyi (n.) canoe gunwale, top frame of kayak
       tsaduq' (a.n.) U drop-off, ledge
       tl'uduq' (a.n.) all ledge, bluff on river
       Tl'uduq'ena (p.n.) hill west of north Babel River, 1.6, 65 lit. 'rear ledge'
       -tsiduq' (a.n.) O top of, head of, hilltop, upper area of landform cf. -tsit'uq' UI dighili tsiduq'
       k'etnu qilan there is a creek on top of the mountain; dghili tsiduq' ezdu he is sitting on a peak
       of the mountain
       vetsiduq' zdluyi (n.) NL Alaska spirea (Spiraea stevenii) lit. 'those which are on hill tops' P.R.
       Kari 1995:54-55

  dugh, dighu, duq' (a.n.) area above treeline

du⁶ /nearby/     /ⓐ::DIR-pf/
  du- (pf.) nearby, proximate occurs as a prefix in the nine directional roots dunit nearby at place
  upstream; dudut nearby at place downstream

du⁷ /get hurt/
   rev duh du' duh duh
   mom duh du duh duh
   neu du du duh duh
   d+n+D+du (conv-rev.) become hurt, injured dnasdu' he got hurt, injured; yednałdu' he hurt
  him; binuk'dnasdu she miscarried; biq' nuk'dnasdu she aborted; hutnaldu he hurt himself; yi
  k'u yach' qihdetulnił ch'u htitnuduhi ghuda O they too will fall down and get hurt for that
  reason; qeyeł k'nułduhi k'qeveq' qisen they had nothing to injure it (the bear) with
  egedu (p.adj.) IU ekdu O pain, ache; ouch ch'u qevechutl'a egedu ghu those with a stomach
  ache; ekgedu gheli it really hurts; ench'u egedu it doesn't hurt; nen shughu egedu shi'inł'an
  da you are the one that is hurting me; egedu eshlan I am hurt; shtsi egedu my head
  aches; vetsenteh egedu he has arthritis; lit. 'pain through his body'

    sukdu, -sukt'a (n.) O tsukdu U sutdu I story, legend besukt'a daghiłnes his story is long

    sukdu nuqelnishna (n.) storytellers

     q'esdu (archaic) QQ source (adv.) day before yesterday
  qendu [archaic] (adv.) in the past
  Grp. 2. ełdu
  ełdu# (v.pf.) for a long while very interesting 2-part disjunct prefix, cognate in Ahtna, rare in LT
  ełdu, łdu (a.d.s. nmom, nneu) for a long time an interesting compound eł+du cf. tledo ≡

  time; łduk'dnanldaq I slept a long time; ełduk'nidu he sat for a long time; chinah ch'a
  ełduqdulnił O he's been sick a long time; ełdushich'k'din'un O he lived, stayed alive a long
  time
  eldu (adv.) U to give up, surrender possibly built upon ełdu a long time "Bel' eldu yit'anni,"
  ggagga i'un qiyghiłt'eq' "Bel' has quit," and he threw the bear from there; "Debel' eldu
  yit'ankdi!" "His own Bel' has quit!"; SP-HN

(D)u *(D)aˑ /U-shaped/       /√::Vext/ an D+V-initial root
   mom dux dun duł du'
   neu dun du' duł du'
   per duł dun duł dush
   prog duł
   q+G+D+un (ext.) hole, trough, cavity, cave, space, U-shaped object, landform extends sdin'a
  baqidun there is a hole in the log; qanqisdun there are pl. holes, caves; shtl'useł'a baqidun I
  have a hole in my pants; biq' dudun it is hollow (log, etc.); Dzełken eghqidun there is a pass
  through the Alaska Range; niqidunt end of a ravine, enclosed landform; chaqenq'a
  qanqghisdunt quggił nghighun in a hole in the smoke house, she made some snares; P.
  Constantine 1980:44
     qiqudunt (a.n.) O qiqadunt U cave
     viqidin (n.) IU biquduni O kayak
     nutih biquduni (n.) O nutay biqiduni U nutih viqidin, nutih vaqilin I two-man kayak
     T'k'qeł'an Veghqidun (p.n.) on upper Swift R., 1.6 98 lit. 'U-shaped canyon where place
     animals gather'
     Dghili Yiqudun (p.n.) canyon on Upper Swift River on right side, 1.6 97 lit. 'U-shaped valley
     into mountain'
     qeghqidun (n.) chute, navigation route used in several Iliamna Lake names for boat chutes
     Chix Kaq' Qech' Qeghqidun (p.n.) 'Baidargi Chutes' off Pedro Mountain peninsula, 3.1 56
     lit. 'chute toward ochre mouth'
   tu#D+dun (mot.) water level rises, tide is rising esp. used for Cook Inlet tides nutugheduł the
  water level is rising, the tide is rising; nutu'idun the tide rose, it is high tide (on Inlet); nishqugh
  nintudushi it is mid-tide; q'antu'idun O q'anta'idun U it is slack tide at high
  water; naqantugheduł tide is coming back in; bantaydun U the tide has started to come
  in; nintu'idun hqugh margin of high tide; yus nutu'idun a lǫw minus tide
     banta'idun tena (n.) Nilas ice, that moves with tide lit. 'rising tide ice'
     vantudushi (n.) tidal grass lit. 'that which is inside tide'

  Ø+du° (stat.) sg. sits, stays k'uq'a hq'aghiduhni he should sit at the blind; niłq'azdu he sits

 dog; sheggunaq' diyeshdu I am sitting on my hand; gheshdut my place, where I stay; qena det
 zidu sit and be quiet!; dezdu he is sitting on it; ey ghu shan qeveł yighidu'i yi ki łu that was the
 one who had stayed with them that summer; niłq' chish ghalyuh ezdu he is sitting lotus-
 like; qizdu, qt'izdu he is sitting in an enclosure; hq'azdu he is sitting in a hole; skizdu he is
 sitting under a tree; idu' he is left sitting; ey'utna egh tgheshduł I will visit the
 neighbors; k'etnu qadut ezdu he lives near the river; ełduk'nidu he sat for a long
 time; tuvughhdi vegh ats'esdu we sit on the edge by the water; k'niquydu he is
 engaged; atgheshduł I will go night bear hunting; agheshdu I did go night bear hunting; yeqa
 zdu he lives on it (certain food), him (a person); uch'ak'iq'eshi ye yqa q'u tghiduł cutting off
 pieces you will live off it; k'eghu'inanshdu I live in dependence; kił gun k'niquydun
 ghun young man this was engaged in bride service (was living with wife's family); yeyqa q'u
 tghiduł it will sustain you to live on it
     k'eghu'itnasdunen (n.) OU k'eghu'itnasdun I k'eghunasdunen O commoner, slave, orphan
     qena ghedunen (n.) leader, advisor, chief, preacher

  ł+du° (stat.) keep O sitting zełdu I make him sit; dekut' yildu he had him sitting in his

 always he keeps him in his pocket; baqay ts'itayenq'e dahyełdu they sat her in the middle of the
 birchbark canoe; huł zeldu I keep it (puppy) with me; deghk'isen ki łu dazdliq' dezdu ha'

 child in her lap; begh hughdetgheldu O I will guard him; det'eh gheqadaldu he protects his

dleq' /laugh/    /√::Vn-anat/
  cont dleq' 〃
  mom dlih dleq' dleh dlih
  dur dlih dleq' dlih dlih
   cust dlih 〃
   prog dleh
   n#k'+Ø+dleq' UI (op.) to laugh this theme with n- distributive is not used in O, see ff.
  theme nk'ghidleq' he was laughing; nk'gheshdleq' I laughed; nk'eshdleq' he laughed at
  me; enk'ishdleq' I am really laughing (got giggles); nk'edleq' he is laughing; nk'idleq' you are
  laughing; nch'k'edleq' we (2) are laughing; nk'ehdleq' you (2) are laughing; nk'qedleq' they
  (2) are laughing
    i'eshdleq' I laughed; idleq' he let out a laugh; q'ak'idleq' he quit laughing; hunk'deldleq' he
  is pretending to laugh; k'ighiłdleq', k'idagheldleq' IU dgheldleq' O he tends to laugh
   ja#l+dliq' O-UI (op.) laugh
    jaldlih he is laughing; ya jaldlih he is making fun of him; k'usht'a jazeldligh I am not
  laughing; jaghełdleq' I made him laugh; jashghiłdliq' you made me laugh

dli° /cold/    /√::padj-temp/ The verb theme only appears in U. The term ezhi is commonly used in OI
  edli (p.adj.) U-IO it's cold
  dli (n.,i.) cold, coldness, chills note that the noun incorporate are used in all dialects dli
  shdełni I am shivering; shidlidelnish O chidlidelzex I I get the chills; shidlidulen I got the
  chills; chinudlidelzex I get chills over and over; dli dełdets I am shivering cold; qil bech' idli
  tałni it was bad, she was about to freeze to death; SPwolvr syats'ena shnudlihghełchezh I my
  jaw is trembling from the cold; qeshqa ghun sa gheli chidlidałyuggen U that rich man was the
  very first one to freeze to death
     dli ni (n.) T varied thrush lit. 'it says cold'
     edli'i (n.) U a cold spell, cold weather

   cust dlih "

    Ken yiq' ggagga I grizzly bear in the Alaska Range

    against mountain base (range)' one of few generated names with Dzełken

  names with dzeł, likely founding place name

ge¹:ezge, ge:ezhge, ge:edzege /tough, heroic/           /√::padj ↇ supp/ seems to be a unique
innovation, common Den. term, treated as three morphemes; not attested elsewhere
d.f.: ezge, ezhge, edzege
   ez, ezh, edze (c.) tough compound
   'ezhge, -ezhge'a (p.adj.) I ezge, -'ezge'a O edzege U competence, talent, bravery, fierce, heroic,
   industriousness ↇ qut'ana yich'a ezhge nlan ha' dyuq he became a better hero among the people
       ezhge'en (n.) I ezge'en O edzege'en U tough, talented person, industrious, heroic,
       skillful only found in Den. natukda'ina ch'u nachiyakda ina t'anch'q'u ezhge hghila out
       fathers and grandfather all were tough people; ezhge nlan ha' dyuq hero he was he
       became; łik'a ezge nelan it is a fierce dog; k'etnish deghu ezge when he drinks he gets
       dangerous; ey tl'egh guhdi qeshqa vey'a gun qeve'ezhge'e kiq'u yezdlan ha after this rich
       man's son became their skilled hunter; Ts'anhdghulyał gun Dena'ina ezhge'a gheli ghila this
       Ts'anhdghulyał was really a tough Dena'ina hero; łik'aha ezge nghila, denghiłjaq dogs were
       good workers, they were swift; ch'u ezgenen k'usht'a chishtułnil OII and good man won't kill
       me; ezget qayeh a rough town; dach' hyegh nuqulnix Ts'anhdghulyał qev'ezhge'a yeghila'
       htsast'a thus they used to tell the story of Ts'anhdghulyał, he was their great hero long ago
       ezhge'en (n.) IO capable person, hero

get¹, git *gəd, guˑd /rotten, smoke, afraid/   /√::Vneu fire/ three interesting groups
   dur get 〃
   neu get 〃
   cust git 〃
   tran git git geł git
   mom git git geł git
  Grp. 1. rotten
   G+ł+get (op.) rot, become rotten no transitional ghiłget it is rotten, falling apart; dghiłget it is
  rotten
     dghiłgedi (n.) UI rotten wood
     łech hnaghiłgedi (n.) OU łech qenghiłgedi I urine soap
     ve'iłgida (n.) I residue of membrane on skin
     k'iłgida (n.) L edible rennet inside stomach of moose
     iłgida (n.) lichens (various)
     ch'vala iłgida (n.) I spruce lichens (Peltigera aphthosa, Lobaria pulmonaria)
     ełnen iłgida (n.) I mushroom (various) eseni iłgida I cottonwood lichens; q'eytsayi
     iłgida I birch lichens
  Grp. 2. smokey
   d+G+D+get (stat.) be smokey dastget it is smoky; ditgit it got smokey; quk'detget smoke is
  going up and out; dnighetgit it was smoking; velida dnastget the stove is smoking; dnasget it
  is smoking; dnutget it will smoke
   causatives, keep O (smudge) burning k'dałget he is keeping a smudge burning; danłgit I made
  smoke; dghełget I kept smoke going; q'uyehdi vich'ek'nałget hluzhun jani hqugh we leave
  them in smoke for ten days; vet'uh tidighiłget you will keep smoke under it; qeyet'uh
  idełget they keep smoke under it
     dasgedi, -dasgeda (n.,i.) smoke bich'adastgetdini'u volcano is
     smoking; qudasgedighi'u smoke is going up; nuch'adasgegi'ilqet' he exhales smoke
     dasgedi qilt'ani (n.) N blue-gray color lit. 'resembles smoke'
     va'idastgedi (n.) smudge pot
     dasgedi yaghiluyi (n.) smoked food (fish, meat)
     dasgedi nlani k'tsen (n.) smoked meat
     dasgedi t'eł'ani (n.) active volcano lit. 'it makes smoke'
   k'+d+D+get (mot.) smoke, odor moves dak'dnatget it is smokey inside; yuyeh dak'ednatgit it
  got smokey inside
  Grp. 3. afraid
   (P+)ni#l+get IU-O (desc.) be afraid, scared (of P) nilget he is afraid; yenilget he is afraid of
  him; nizelgit I am scared; nighelgit I was scared; yeniłget he scared hier; qut'ana guna
  qevninlget du qevteh zidu? are you afraid of the people you are living amongst?; shtinułget he

Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                    192

     will scare me; nnizelgit I got scared of you; shniziłgit you scared me; yenilgit łu. nda'ich q'u

git, get /drip, swift, limp/ the ff themes seem related, are unique to Dena'ina and most common in
O
     mom git git get git
     dur get "
     per geł get geł
     cust git
     neu get "
     tran git git geł git
     tu#d+l+git (op.) water drips, leaks tudelgit water is dripping (faucet or house leaking); shji
    dulgit it dripped in my ear; naji dulgit it dripped on us (liquid); tudulgit it was
    leaking; tutdulgit it will leak; shji tudelget the house is dripping, leaking on me; nuqdulgeł a
    light rain is moving about; q'anłidnulget rain was sprinkling about; kadałtin ch'adulget blood is
    dripping out; kadałtin ch'adulget blood comes out; I tuhdghiłget O there is fog over water
       nuqdulgełi (n.) spring showers
     d+n+l+git (ono.) sound of rain on roof, in bucket tutnalgit it is the sound of rain on
    roof; hqatutnalgit it is the sound of rain on it
     k'+n+l+git (mot.) OU-I limp k'nalgit he arrived limping; nuk'nalgit he limped
    back; quk'nalget he limped up; senk'tnilgit he limped downward; k'itnghełget he limps
       k'ighentełgeden (n.) O lame person
     d+n+ł+get O-IU (dim.) animate is swift qiz'in dnaghiłget clams are fast, quick; detniłgit it got
    swift; denqenghiłget łu they were swift runners they say; ch'ughiłget he is slow (runner, walker)

gu *gaˑ /here proximate/         /√::DEM space/time/
d.f.: gu, gin
   gu (dem.) this, these, here, now, proximate guduh qghenashat'a k'eltem it is thundering close
   by; gu k'u O here too; gu q'u right here; jangu today; gudach' this way (pointing), this way
   to turn (as in dog call); q'udi gu right now; gughu way over to here; dunih guhch'en ezdu he
   lives downriver and on this side; guhdich'en k'etnuh nelgguk I ran to this side of the river
   gin (dem.) this thing here gindi chik'ish nu ch'ełchini we made this cap for you
       gunen (dem.) this person gundi she'u this is my wife
       guna (n.) these people, group, neighbors guna shdesnaqa gunahdi shingha'ina ch'u
       shuda'ina these people my parents, these and my older brothers and my older sisters; qut'ana
       guna qevninlgetdu qeuteh zidu are you afraid of the people you are living
       amongst; gunadi these ones

    dudlik I kettle-size birchbark basket; qałnigi gudlik U cast-iron pot, dutch oven; tahqegh'a

gujun    Russian chugún (-kal) cast iron pot; cast iron
  gujun (n.) IO jugunga O chugun OU gujunga, jugun Il cast iron pot, ceramic kettle chugun
  qeyłnihi qałnigi q'ent'ayi, yi shughu chugun qeyłnihi, tahdna'inaq' hdi they call that chugun, it
  is like rock, the one they call chugun, that is a Russian word; sukdi chaynik ghin yi k'u chugun
  chaynik, qałnigi q'u t'ent'a, gudlik ghudehdi k'a the old time tea kettle, is ch. ch., it is like rock,
  and those kettles were too; MO76
  Gujun (ps.n.) OU story figure in Kenai Lake area PK91:244-247, Gujun gets killed by a golden
  hatchet one of several Russian folktales in Dena'ina Gujun Ses Qilantnu łu ghidu, yet łu zuluda
  qił'un G. stayed at 'ridge is there stream' Upper Kenai River, there he found gold

guk /slide/
  prog        gex gish
  momI        gux guk gex gux
   momOU gush guk gesh gush
   G+l+guk (mot.) glide, slide, slip, ski on surface; multiple objects (snow, leaves) drift in air, swing
  in a swing ilguk he arrived sliding; nughelgesh he is sliding around; nughelguk he slid
  down; hetl n'at janulgesh he is gliding in a sled; shdundilges he slid down; łuh'ilguk he slid
  around; dunalguk he stumbled in; chitl talguk snow drifted; chitl' ghelges snow
  drifted; talgush it is drifting; janulgesh he is swinging (in a swing); utsilu
  idich'anulgesh O his tongue flits in and out
     vinuch'elgexi (n.) I binuch'elgeshi U U sleeping bag
   d+G+l+guk (suc.) become impaled (on elongated object) qanchi k'eha hnidulguk porcupine
  quills are stuck in it; hyegh k'daniłguk they shoved spear in him; shtut' k'dulguk I have
  sliver; baydulguk O it is stuck all the way through it; shkets' shinik'dulguk I got sth. (a
  splinter) in my finger; shnik'detulgesh I will get a splinter; heyi ni'u nik'dulguk the first of the
  month came; stick is embedded, as in marking old time calendar tl'aq'i jani
  niłkidulguk O solstice came; lit. 'night connected to day' k'itseŷi ghini łuq'u gełq'a
  nidalguk that fire poker pierced it's throat
   caus. to impale, spear O negh tidighełgex I will spear through you; shtuqesa skidghełguk I stuck
  my spear in a tree; deshk'diłgush stoke (stir) the fire; deshch'dghiłguk we stolked
  it; nik'dinłguk he stuck sth. up to a point; yegełq'a k'dghiłguk tets' ghini eła he speared it in her
  throat with the spear; q'aditin ghini nu'ilyeł ch'u yeghenk'dełges ch'u ey'u nqeyełt'eh he
  grabbed his spear and poked it through it and he threw it to the side; q'aditin ghini nuyilyał
  igheydełges he grabbed that spear and poked it through him; yutsen hyeghk'daniłguk O down
  below they shoved a spear through him; bet'e k'inłgush you spear, jab sth. (waves); said to
  middle man in canoe to balance the craft
   A Wassillie 1978:29-30 riddle: tuqesi skiyeh ch'dghiłgugi shugu I see someone threw a spear under a
  tree; (answ) ch'vala hdidghik'eli a spruce tree with a verticle crack along the trunk
   O+d+ł+guk U-OI (mot.) split O (wood) into thin pieces ch'andayełguk I split a board off

ggagh /croak (of raven)/        /√::Vono/ cf. gguq another raven call
   d+l+ggagh (op-ono.) be caw of raven delggagh it is cawing
    delgga, delggagh (n.) U raven
    ggahyi (n.) Raven clan; Insect Larvae Clan folk-etymologized as from the the Raven's cawing
    sound; however SP76-clan states that this is related to ggih insect cf. At. xwggaexyu snowbird
    clan ggayi is not associated with snowbird or rabbit in Dena'ina ggih bighiduyi ghini yi
    itun she found a bug staying inside (a cocoon); "shgguya gun ki ggahyi tulał. ggahyi tulał
    łu," duda yełni "my child here, she will become the Ggahyi Clan," the older sister says to
    her; SP76-clan

ggahunagh /Kokhanok p.n./          Aleut, similar to some Aleut names Qagaan tayaĝungin ‘people of the
east’ Qax̂un, people of the Rat Islands, qag-, qaga ‘outside, east’, Qagaanu-x̂ ‘Little Bay (Krenitzin
Islands)’,
   Ggahunagh (p.n.) Kokhanok village and site not analyzable to either Den. or Yupik expert
   speakers

  Ggamukshah (p.n.) McNeil Cove, Kamishak site, 4.1 10 from Nick Kolyaha, 1981, this is N.K.'s
  version "Kamishak"

gganałggi ∞      substrate candidate, no clear source, in Kodiak Alutiiq qanallqiq ‘largest type of sea snail’ but
not in Proto-Eskimo (AMB)



 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh

  gganałggi (n.) O niggiłgga Il qanałggi U gganałggiq I sea snail, limpet, dogwinkle, “beans”

ggas *ɢeˑth /king salmon/            /√::N!-bio/ Only in the ff.
  ggas ten'a (n.) L-OUI small king salmon lit. 'king salmon handle'

ggasila /Kasilof p.n./       Aleut or CY, Aleut qasi-lix ‘to be fishing, to fish for supply, to store fish,’ also cf. CY
kassigluq ‘confluence of rivers’ (Anna Berge)


  Ggasilatnu (p.n.) Kasilof River a blend and noted as substrate place name, SPA 2016:147,
  however name indicates an Eskimo or Aleut people occupied middle Kenai Peninsula at the time the
  name was blended with dnu AB: "Aleut qasi-lix ‘to be fishing, to fish for supply, to store fish,’ e.g.
  Qasilasi-x̂ name of cape on Amlia. This etymology is plausible, as the Kasilof River is a fishing site;
  the closest Yup’ik word, kassigluq, means ‘confluence of rivers’"

  down; nutu'ituggesh it will drip once; nutughiggech' it dripped (once); nutuggesh water is
  dripping; shtuk'denggech' water sprays on me; shtutneggech' it sprays on
  me; bak'tunashtggech' water was shaken
   ta#G+l+ggech' (n mom.) inanimate sinks into water tadnalggech' it (box) sunk; ta'ilggech' it

                                                   gh/h


                                    AAD1990: gh: 202-226, KAD2000: gh, 226-277, LTDD: gh
   Dene Lexware dictionary comparisons:
  MTDD: gh YMM92: gh/h: 219-240, w: 647-661
  absent in in Den *xwy¹ pruce root


➡gh to, by, at, in relation to see egh, ghe in relation to


gh¹ *ɢə, *ghə /progessive/      /ⓐ::vpf/
  gh- (v.pf.) progressive/future/gh-perfective mode gheyuł he is walking along
  t+gh (if.s.) future mode tgheshyuł I will walk

gh² /gh-qualifier/ an excellent comparative issue for Den, Ahtna, LT, MT, UK, and Koy Den. has
several strings with the gh- qualifier that appear to be conservative
   gh+ (v.pf.) gh-qualfier
   Grp. 1. thematic, gh+ assigned to themes
    O+gh+Ø+dleq' (conv.) ridicule, mock, make fun of P yeghazdleq' he mocked, laughed at
   him; see the general op. theme to laugh
    P+a#+gh+i+D+ghach (mot.) bite P a motion theme with an unusual PAS: a#gh+i
       i mom basic derivation is Ø mom. yaghidghach he bit it once; yaghudghesh he bit it
      rep.; ugha k'aghindghash go ahead and bite sth
   Grp. 2. t.f.s. strings with gh+
   O+gh+Ø (t.f.s.) (conv.) gathering, counting
    O+d+gh+Ø+lu° I-OU (conv.) to count, (archaically) to count one-hundred O dghayeshlun I
   counted them; k'dghayeshlun I made, counted 100
   Grp. theme group with gh+l+kit
    O+gh+l+kit
   nił+gh+l (t.f.s.) gathering
    hu#gh+l+lu (rev.) get ready, prepare oneself huhghalyu he got ready; huhghazelyu I got
   myself ready; hunuqeghtghelyał O I will prepare myself again; hunuqeghanlyu I prepared
   myself again
       nił+gh+l+ta I (mot.) birds gather into a flock nice use of gh-qualifier nuniłghaltan birds
      gathered in a flock; nuniłgheltax they are flocking up; nuniłtghultax they will flock up
   Grp. 3. a.d.s
   gh+ (a.d.s. z mom, z neu) gathering, congregating, interesting as the a gh-qualifier a.d.s range of
   meanings is not determined ghazhchet it shrank; ghedazhchet O it (board)
   shrunk; hdghazhełchin I corked it; Tsałt'eshi nuti ch'en hghaztun 'Black Stone Axe' points
   toward the ocean side; yeghu hghazdatl' they gathered by him; sha'idenghazdem L-O it is tight
   on me

gh³ /optative/      /ⓐ::vpf+/ Den. optative can be clarified by Ahtna or LT with overt gho, ghw
  gh- (v.pf.) optative mode, let S, S should gh(e) is the optative mode prefix, Cu- is optative marker,
  C+gh → Cu yulach he should cook it; łiq'a ghelach he should cook fish; ighestseł I should
  chop it once; yenuł'an he ought to look at it; ch'idułqet we should ask him
   perambulative optatives łuhgheshyux ni I I should go around; nuch'uyuła I you should not go
  around
  OPT-VERB ni (v.enc.) one should VERB, ought to VERB with optative mode tuyush ni let him
  go; ch'hduchit ni let's quit; ch'k'dałtetl'i ch'duchit ni let's quit shooting; ghutnu ni he should
  work; igheshzhi ni I should call his name; k'gheshtnun ni let me drink sth.
  OPT-VERB ilay (if.s.) IO OPT-VERB ilagi Il negative optative, don't, one should not
  V hghinax ilay I hghinash ilagi Il don't talk; vets'iłt'an gun chidghiłnah ilay don't kill his
  partner; iy ch'niyuda hghinax ilay when we get there don't talk; eqech' t'ghit'an ilay don't do
  that when you are there; ch'uqighił q'u ki dghił'an ilay ki di don't treat him badly again
     -ch'kdi (v.enc.) one should have VERB used with optative verbs k'ghilqat ch'kdi you should
     have eaten; yeh q'u ghidu ch'kdi you should have stayed there; yeh tgheshu ch'kdi I should
     have gone there; ghednu ch'kdi qit'a ideshni I know she should be working; nuhughiltah
     ch'kdi you should have bathed

gh⁴ /in relation to/   /√/pp/ an allomorph of egh, -ghe ..c P+gh, egh, ghe forms postpositions or
areal nouns P+gheni behind P, upstream of P; -ghdut downstream of P; P+egh+deq,
P+a+deq between P; -ghekenu at base of

h¹ /reversative/   /ⓐ::vsf1/
  h (v.sf1.) reversative aspect neyah it is growing; ggesht'et k'u dnuyiłtan he washed the dirt off
  of him; nuyiłtan she gave him a bath, washed him; nunłtah wash him
  STEM+h (a.d.s. persistive) repeated action nggun tih he did move hands up and down; q'es
  ghettiq he shook his head; q'uq'esgheltiq he was shaking his head/neck; indicated h~q
  alternation occurs in persistive stem sets
  n# (a.d.s. rev) down1 and back

h² /attached/ disjunct prefix only detected in Den, At. (x#) and Koy. (which has haa) that appears in
various themes; some of these can be viewed as tertriary themes, having one or two previous levels of
theme formation always cooccurs wth gh+ qualifier; it is not in other Dene langs.

  h# disjunct h, attached, tethered this occupies its own prefix slot

h³, q *x̯ʊ /area-time/     /ⓐ::PF-SF-ENC¹-space/time‴/ one of the most versatile affixes
  Grp. 1. h, q areal (qualifier) prefix
  h+, q+ (v.pf.) area, place, situation, state, area gender/qualifier common rule is q precedes V, h
  precedes C qenq'a qiz'un there is a house; qenq'a hghi'u there was a house; qenq'a qełchi he
  is building a a house; qenq'a ch'eqeghun we are building houses; qenq'a ch'ehghighun we built
  houses
  q, h (v.pf.) thematized in verb themes
  Grp. enclitic
  h (n.enc.) locative enclitic, at, to a general area suffixal form of qe area seems most common in O;
  21.6-7 qenq'ah tazu he went to the area; qenq'ah nuch'tujuł we will run to the house; qenq'ah
  ezdu he is staying at home; tuq'ih nu'iju he went three times; Ednah ghetnu he is working at
  Edna's; yuq'h yuk'qul' O there are no clouds in the sky; guduh nulnił O he is flying around
  here; tubughh tazu O he went to the beach; guduh qeghna k'eltem it is thundering near
  her; yudeqh tiyush walk up higher; Bench'enh tazu he went toward the lake
  h (sf.) general area suffix in directionals, interrogatives and demonstratives yudeh in an upward
  direction; yut'eh in a downriver direction; nutih two times guduh around
  here; ndahduh O where to, where at; tsah first, ahead

  ha', ha (s.cnj.) IO qa OU since, when, while but, although, as most common in I where final

  naqadiłghesh turn over the knife; qeyeł tułghesh U CHK beluga SP
  whaling; łqi'ich'denghiłghach we hid it (a box); k'izhagi yelaq'a ghiłghach he handed him the

  together; nił'eyeshdghach I closed my teeth, jaws; nił'eshdghach he closed his jaws
   P+a#+gh+i+D+ghach (mot.) bite P a motion theme with an unusual string P+a#gh+i
      i mom basic derivation is Ø mom. yaghidghach he bit it once; yaghudghesh he bit it
     rep.; ugha k'aghindghash go ahead and bite sth; yaghadghach he is biting
     it; k'aghidghach he bit sth.; vayghidghach it bit him; vaghindeghash he is biting
     it; yatighudeghesh he will; k'aghideghach he bit it; yaghidghash he is biting
     it; shaghidghach he bit me; shaghidghach he bit me (a little); hvayghidghach he bit
     them; vatighigheshdghesh I baghitagheshdeghesh O I will bite it; k'usht'a
     ezghitegheshdeghesh O I won't bite it; k'atighudghesh he will bite

ghel, gheli /good/        /d√::adj/ possible derived root; especially distinctive of Dena'ina and Ahtna

  gheli a very long time ago; nen gheli di niqu t'deshni da ida you're really the one I've been

     berry'

  yagheli gheli (adv.) yalagheli U really good

    hey gek'a (n.) O hey gega U hey giga, k'inghildi NL hey gek'a Il low bush cranberry,
    mountain cranberry; lingonberry; in I winter stage of the low bush cranberry (Vaccinium vitis-
    idaea) P.R. Kari 1995:67-68 yithdi heygek'a naltun; yich'hdi dnudniltey ch'u eshdyesh
    łu then he tried lowbush cranberries; he got strength from them and he was able to
    survive; PK91:220

ghu¹ /expanse, according to‴/       /√ⓐ::AN¹/
d.f.: ghu, hu, ghuda, shighu
   Grp. 1. areal nouns, postpositions
   -ghu (a.n.) along expanse, length of P shghu gheyuł he's keeping pace with me; hghu hghazdatl'
   ha' they all gathered by them; yeghuk'dnałtun he measured the distance; qighu
   k'ighetnałtuh O he is measuring it; ch'vaq'da ghuniyu he walked the length of the hill; tuvugh
   ghu łuhenshyu I I walked along the beach; chu ghu nuhtelggex they keep going along the
   cache; yighu nusheł'idiltex he ran back and forth along length of it
       veghuk'teldexa (n.) I gun scope
       k'eghudenltuhi (n.) I k'eghutneltuhi OU ruler, tape measure
   niłghu (adv.) IO ułghu U level surface, evenly ełnen niłghu hnastun IO the land is flat,
   level; ułghu hdnaytun U the surface is level; ełnen niłghu t'ełuq I leveled the earth; qenq'a
   niłghu qiz'un the house is level; niłghu sht'a t'enlah make it level; qendugh niłghu t'qit'a the
   floor is level; tets' dakaq' ghu niłghu gheli ndunqeydał'u ha' they had the spears placed just
   evenly in the doorway
       ghuyi, ghuyiq' (a.n.) end, point, peninsula, end of point garandash ghuyiq' point of a pencil
   iqech' ghu (adv.) in the manner of iqech' ghu nch'u k'i tak'estsex he wasn't paddling like (he
   was) before; iqech' ghu t'qet'an hnuyu hva łitl'en qizdlan ha' thus they kept on doing that until
   it became spring on them
   P+ghu (p.p.) depending upon P yeghudiłt'a they use it; k'eghu'inanshdu I live in
   dependence; beghut'u deshni I am begging for it
       k'eghu'itnasdunen (n.) OU k'eghu'itnasdun I k'eghunasdunen O commoner, slave, orphan
       lit. 'one who stays dependently'
   -ghuda, hu (p.p.) IU qu O due to, because of O yi gini jija vetsi ghuda ve'izhi qilan this duck
   is named by its head; qut'ana chidełt'ix ha' tut'ił kadałtin ghin ghuda he kills people because of
   that blood; ełkuni ghuda k't'un nudnalyun because of rain plants grew; na'ełnen'a ghuda
   niłnuch'itudedeł because of our land, we will get back together; nabu qghinagi ghuda na'ełnen'a
   nalaq'a nuqdulnen because he spoke for us we got our land back; yin kił gunhdi ch'qidetnik.
   vetutda qeshqa nlani ghuda the young was lazy due to his father being a rich man; yinen ghuda
   q'angheshduk because of him I went; ełkuni ghuda gega txeł'ił because of rain I will pick

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                          249

  berries; yen ghuda q'angheshduk on account of him I went; dach' t'et'an ltah t'et'an
  qeyenuł'ani ghuda thus he was swaying in order for they see him (showing off); nił dnayi qilan
  ghuda niłch' nuqutdił they were related so they used to visit each other
     ch'aduch' huda (int.) O ch'adach' huda U nda'ich' huda I why, for what reason, what for,
     how come?
  -dughu (p.p.) sarcastically toward P with du verbal yedughu nidudin'un he said sth.
  sarcastically to him; yedughu qenash he is talking sarcastically to him; dughu nlan he is a
  wiseguy, smart alec; shdughu qinash you talk sarcastically to me; shdughu nidudi'ush you talk
  smartly to me
  P+ghu## (p.p.) fetching P (meat or fish at the killsite) k'ghu ch'tudał ni let's go get
  meat; qeyghu łuhnidatl ha' they went to the kill site; chu ghu nughinyuł go to cache (for fish)
  Grp. 2. strings
     k'eghuhten (a.n.) meat trail
  P+ghu (v.pf.) to, length of as disjunct prefix
     P+ghu# (a.d.s. nmom) giving to P yeghuyghaniłnik he gave it (a present) to
     him; yeghuyniqun she gave him a cup (of water); naghuk'niłkit they fed us
     P+i+ghu#d (a.d.s. Ømom) the length of P yighunudiltlet he jumped on the full length of
     him; shighunudiltlet he jumped as far as me
     P+ghu#gh+D (a.d.s. smom) part of P yeghuk'ghalqet' he ate part of it; yeghuk'ghastnun he
     drank part of it
     P+ghu#gh+ (a.d.s. smom) gathering, congregating only in the ff hghuhghazdatl' they all
     congregated; niłghutghutdeł they will congregate
  Grp. 3. other extensions
  -inughu (a.n.) O's happiness a compound -i inside, nu again and ghu² shinughu qilan I am
  happy; shinughu dyiluq I am happy about the way he did it; qvinughu ch't'inluq they are happy
  aobut what you did; shinughu sht'a nuhghesht'an I am happy to see you again
     shinugha (exc.) I shinugh'a O I am happy (for you)!
  ughu (adv.) -ughu particles Holton Lovick 2000 "-ughu particles (shughu and *dughu) differ from those
  of the evidential enclitics. These forms have a much freer syntactic distribution and are thus particles rather
  than clitics."


ghu³, ghin /there intermediate/         /√::DEM/ ghu, ghin alternations same as gu, gin here, this one
  ghu (dem.) there, in the distance cf. gu here, nearby ghu nugheyuł ch'q'u yeh dghiliq' he is
  walking around on that mountain there; ghu hqugh niyu he went as far as there; "yagheli łu ghu"
  yidni łu "so he is good there" she thought; shi du ghu? am I the one?; nen du ghu? are you the
  one?; yin du ghu is he the one; shi shughu I am the one
     ghun, ghunen, ghundi (pro.) that person yegh nudyux deghk'isen ghun he keeps coming
     back to that woman
     ghuna (pro.) those persons
     ghini, ghin (pro.) that thing, those things (at a distance) ghini łik'a that dog; ghini itchitl' that
     stuff is rotten; ghindi shtets'a that one is my cane; yi ghin qa those (people) over there
     ghinikda, ghenikda (n.) O black bear (nickname) lit. 'that no good one'
  ghu, dghu (s.cnj.) at that time, when łik'a yitsa dghu k'net'ish dog barks when he
  steals; chiqul'ul deghu when they were playing; huqyudet'an bila eł k'edet'us dghu he is

  careful, when he saws; k'eluyesh aniyu deghu when he went to K'eluyesh; kił dun'iju deghu
  łik'a baghideghaji yitsa O when boy returned the dog that bit him barked; tułkun deghu gega

  when people are talking about me; iłkun ghu nuqitdatl' they came back when it started to rain
     shughu (adv.) in my opinion shughu ndalvay huhdenlyah lu in my opinion they raised geese

     dughu (adv.) in your opinion nda'ih du dughu nał k'qisil da?" in your opinion why is game

ghu⁴ *gha /fur/     /√::Nv-anat/
  -ghu (n.) hair, fur
      -tsighu (n.) UI head hair
      -tl'aghu (n.) pubic hair
      -nik' ghu (n.) nostril hair
      -ghulits'a (n.) guard hair
      hulqadi (n.) I hudahi U animal shedding hair
      dehtehtsighu (n.) U dehtsighu IU spruce lichen,"old man's beard" (Alectoria spp., Usnea
      spp.) lit. 'hair above'
      łighuk'bet' (n.) T horned grebe (Podiceps auritus) lit. 'dog hair stomach'
      k'eghu ch'da (n.) caribou robe, fur robe
      ghuliy (n.) U wealth, fur wealth, potlatch gifts a well known term in interior Alaska that refers
      to fur as a source of wealth
  nghuyi (n.) I animal, four-legged game animal lit. 'furred ones' only in I, no obvious cognates
  P-ghus# (a.d.s. smom) skinning P a compound of ghu¹ and -s skin the suffix yeghusezdlu she
  skinned them (pl. animals); yeghusendent'us he skinned it (e.g. rabbit); yeghusensla he skinned
  it (large animal); qeyghusezgits' they case skinned it; ggagga nih chihdełt'ix ghuhdi ey q'ut'un
  qilan ghu qeyghusełtax ha when they kill a bear, that morning there they would skin it and; AB20
   D+ghun (desc.) be hairy, fur-covered unproductive but in nom.
      dghuni seł (n.) UI fur pants
      nudeghin (n.) O nedghuna U nindghuna L nundghuna NIl trailing black currant and
      skunk currant (Ribes laxiflorum and R. glandulosum) lit. 'that which is hairy' nudghin beghu
      qdilan the black currant has hair
      łindghuna (n.) NL twisted stalk, watermelon berry (Streptopus amplexifolius) lit. 'furry dog'

    yeghighun he made pl. O; gech' eshghun I am making gloves

  cottonwood bark; k'eli dghighun he made songs; k'duzhizha niłk'uch' ghedghun all beaks are

     time a war party come; dghuni niłtu qyeł nihughelyak he prepared himself for war

     dedghuni (n.) U small hawks, American kestrel (Falco sparverius) lit. 'warrior, killer'
   P+ghe#O+d+n+ł+ghen (conv.) connect, join, tie, splice O to P interesting stem
  variation niłdanłghik I joined O together; niłghendghalghen pl. O are joined, attached
  together; qahtnalghik brush camp is erected

     niłdghulghigi (n.) I finger knuckles
     hnidghulghigi (n.) O pole frame
     benaqahtnalghik, veniqahdenghalghik (n.) tree rings
  niłdghulghigi (n.) O niłghunhdghulghuga U horsetail, jointed grass (Equisetum spp.) lit. 'those

  nzaq'ezdli bekiyditghiłdeł for those with a toothache, you heat jointed grass root and press it

hu² /reflexive/    /ⓐ::vpf+/ seems to be in several west Alaska langs.
  hu+ (v.pf.) self, reflexive the position of hu is interesting, may be the right-most disjunct prefix, we
  treat it as in dir. obj. slot

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                               252

    hu+D-effect (n.d.s.) relexive, by oneself nuhughelbaq' he made himself
    vomit; nuhughelvil he lowered himself by a rope, a spider lowered itself on
    web; chihulyuq he killed himself; hudnil'an he is looking at himself; hujagheldleq' O he
    laughed at himsel; hyunuhu'elyuq I repaid myself; huqeyudet'an UO he is careful with
    himself
    hu (n.d.s.) pretensive, pretending to VERB chihuk'delnah he is pretending he is
    sick; chihuk'el'ul I chihuk'del'ul O he is pretending to play; chiduhuk'del'ul he is
    pretending to joke, to tease; ch'u huk'del'egen ghun q'udi ch'aqi'ushen and the one who was
    pretending to shamanize, revealed his power; PK91: hunk'deldleq' he is pretending to
    laugh; prefix order here is disitnct
  hu#l+ (t.f.s.) move self, one's body only in a few motion themes
     nu#hu+t+l+ch'eŷ O (op-rep.) rest oneself IU -yix nuhutelch'ex he is
    resting; nuhutalch'eŷ he rested
     hu+k'+l+kit (mot.) go, wander by oneself nuhuk'ulkeł he is wandering
    around; q'anhuk'ulket he was wandering around
     hu+d+l+t'ak (mot.) move one's body quickly, fish jumps similar to l+ghel but a more vigorous
    movement hutdalt'eq' he jumped, moved quickly; nhudelt'eh he keeps on
    moving; quhuk'dult'eq' sth. (fish) jumped up; shtuhuydalt'eq' it escaped, ran off
  hu (pf.) self, reflexive pronoun used with postpositions shi yi shit'i hugh deshni t'i, Shem
  Pete that's me talking for myself, Shem Pete; huq' nudli he acts normal, acts like himself
  again; benan yes huq' nuk'eghnulkit he pulled skin back up on his own face
  hu (c.) self, person forms several adverbs
    husht'a (exc.) IO wait!
    husht'an (adv.) I in a little while
    huqedlan, huggedlan (adv.) IU slowly

    holes on it'

ghuk'¹ /fin/     /√::N!-bio-anat/ only in I, cognates uncertain, only in two compounds

jex¹:vejex *bə-dzəxʸ /caribou/           /√::N!-bio/ An ancient Dene disyllabic root; this be related to I

   ldexi caribou being shot with arrow with (its own) horn point; highly abstract riddle AW "I see it is
  inflicting wounds upon its own body"


jex² /rolling hills/   /√::Vext-geog ∀/
    common in I but not recognized in UO, a rare noun and verb theme not attested elsewhere
  neu jex 〃

  hills, twisting; eshjex qughashjex some rolling hills ascend
     eshjex (a.n.) I rolling hills, series of hills eshjex n'ez'u there is a series of rolling hills; place
     names with this theme, all in Lime area implied underlyng form: s+D+jex

     Nunk'dushjexa (p.n.) hills above mouth of Bonanza Creek; 2.1 74 lit. 'series of hills'

     are joined together lake' perhaps distinct verb theme
     Nudenghaljexi Vena (p.n.) lake east of Nqutl' Vena, 1.5, 35 lit. 'series of hills lake'

d.f.: prominent derived roots d√: kala, kaq', kaja
   Grp. 1. tail
   -ka (n.,i.,c.) tail, (of muskrat, beaver, bird) hukadghił'u k''un ghu it had his tail sticking out the
   den there
       -kats'ena (n.) tail bone
       ka t'u (n.) tail feather
       kaych'uk'a (n.) U old-style shirt with pointed tails in front and back lit. 'tail hanging'

       lit. 'the one at the base of the tail'

       tallow'

       itun so as they came to the saltwater she found a fishtail; “ghu shih shihdi shgguya ghunen
       Kali tulał” shu dghini łu “there me, my child of mine will become a F.” it seems she
       said; SP76-clan

       yudi ghelkala (n.) I surfbird
   kala q'edi (n.) U evening star lit. 'on the fish tail'

     -kalt'a (n.) fish tail, whale's tail contrasts with ka, kala kaja k'kalt'a detached fish tail; nini

  others seems to be a founding place name with archaic vowels

ki²:ski, sken *th-ki /area under tree/ An interesting compound in two groups of compounds;
cognates in Ahtna and Dena'ina have s cf. 'ethchena' ≡ LT

ku¹ *kaˑ /abdominal cavity‴/         /√::N!-anat/
d.f.: ku, kuy, d√ kun, kut'
   -ku (a.n.) abdominal cavity, inner body, womb, cavity with guts shku k'dulggat I have
   diarrhea; shku k'eltuł I have an upset stomach (diarrhea); kunuch'qenuljeq' ninuch' 'ni let's wet
   our insides, have a drink; q'in ghin vekuy nazdlu the eggs stay inside the stomach; vinazdli yan
   veku ch'anahk'enlax we just take the intestines out
       kuz'in (n.) IO kuy'in U heart
       -ku ch'adancheden (n.) U mother after deceased lit. 'camne from her womb'
       -kuqizdlu, -kuqeyagh'a (n.) insides, entrails, guts (in general)
       k'kudi, nakudi (n.) O diarrhea
   -kun (a.n.) abdomen
       k'kunyin (n.) IO k'kuyni T k'kunyi U stomach fat, fat around large intestine, suet
       niłkuhdighelts'ina (n.) family lit. 'those from the same womb'
       kununi, -kunun'a (n.) hunting ability, hunting luck
   -kut' (a.n.) insides (or carcass), pocket, within a garment shgech'a shvaldu'a kut' dghełdatl' I
   put my gloves in my coat pocket; k'kut' qeł'an she is cleaning the insides, guts (of fish, animal)
       vikut' quni (n.) OI kut'i qak'naquni U lined coat
       hkut' k'eł'anen, hkuk'eł'anen (n.) doctor, traditonal Den. doctor, medical practioner
       kut' hyil'uni (n.) IO t'ehyil'uni U pocket watch2
   P+kut'##, P+kut'e## (a.d.s. gh mom) into the clothing of P yeł kut'e k'ut'et U he put it inside
   his clothes
   ku# (i.) humbly, feebly, frailly, meekly hq'akunastan it was lying in hole, on its stomach; ADD
   ku (exc.) umpf, expression when lifting likely with this root
    l+kun (desc.) to be pregent ilkun she is pregnent; only in Rezanov 1805, but not used in modern
   Den.

kuh *koˑx ̣ /in the lee/    /√::PP/ absent in OU, common in NL dialects, note that main root du

  -kuh, -ghkuh (a.n.) LN downstream, in the lee of nakuh nihełdalen there is darkness down
  below us; AE "You are aware ahead of time on the East side that it is going to blow a North wind."
     -ghkuhch'en (a.n.) on the downriver side from one
     qeghkuht'an, kukuht'an (n.) NL Downriver clan

     snare creek' ‡ geodup paired with Caribou Snare Creek, U-trails
     Tuhditali Qeghkuh Ch'adaniłen (p.n.) creek that joins Shehtnu behind Pete Bobby's camp, 1.5,
     9 lit. 'stream downstream of timber extends'

   G+ł+kegh, kugh (dim.) be big, large, tall, high, great in quantity or volume
   neuter k'idiki tazkugh it is a little too big; shegh tazkuh it is too big for me; k'idiki
  dghiłkugh it is too big; nzhah daghiłkugh, nzhah daghiłkuh the snow is deep; dghiłkugh he is
  big; dehghiłkugh you are big; k'idiki gheli dech'ghiłkugh you are too big; chu dghiłkughi a
  big beaver; shquł dghiłkughi a big spoon; denqełkugh they are big; denhtułkugh they will be
  big; t'qidaghiłkugh tsat'uh łu it was a big cave; k'etnu t'qidaghiłkugh tuvugh ch'aniyu ha' he
  walked out on the wide river shore; q'uyehdi ch'vala daghiłkughi egh niyu then he came to a big
  spruce; qidaghiłkugh gheli nihqini'un they built a big house; ni ghini ghu dghiłkugh gheli
  ch'q'u ten gheli yan niqaz'u ha' ice really was all around the whole island; chik'ish dghiłkeghh
  zełchin I made the cap too big; deghełkegh I was big
   transitional de'ułkeh I t'ułkeh O he is getting big; de'iłkigh it got big; de'itinłkegh you are
  getting big; nzhah tidułkugh the snow will be deep; dediłkigh it got big; nzhah diłkugh the
  snow got deep; ch'q'aynigen ghun ki t'iłkugh the baby got big; vava ghini ndahqugh shuq'u
  t'iłkugh how big a pile is the dry fish I wonder
   comparative niłinlkegh they are the same size; bi'a benuyulkigh she got as big as his
  son; veyighiłkughen I he is getting as big him, as it grew; ggagga ghini duntda nnu'ilkugh;

k' *k' /indefinite/     /ⓐ::v.pf.+‴/
  k' (pf.) something's, indefinite possessive prefix and postpositional object k'tukda a father, the
  father
  k' (v.pf.) something, indefinite postpositional object
  ch'+ (v.pf.) something, indefinite object/subject, unspecified non-human k'e'uł he is chewing
  something
  k'+ (t.f.s.) thematized indefinite k'eldzes he is dancing
  k'ech' (c.) to, towards from k'e+ch' thus, in the manner of
  niłk'ech' (adv.) zigzag, crooked niłk'ech' łuhshełdaniłtuk' he ran zigzag; beq'es ghin niłk'ech'
  nunk'ighastken ka t'ent'a its neck is bent back and forth; k'duheł ghini niłk'ech' ghu da'u
  heydenghałgheł he was swinging the horn club back and forth; niłk'ech' t'anch'q'u
  shtuhhnaniłyit they all ran away zigzag; deggech' niłk'ech' ghu nushełdułtex up the hill zig-
  zagging he kept running
     -tsen niłk'ech' (n.) direction of the muscle fiber
     niłk'enteh (adv.) occasionally, now and then shanteh niłk'enteh Yaghenen łuq'u
     q'anhghutnish baydalgi n'at occasionally they they all went across to Kenai Peninsula in a
     bidargi in the summer; P. Constantine 1980:16

k'eq' /collared pika/    /√::N-bio,Vono/ the pika are only in At. and Upper Inlet
  k'eq'a (n.) U pika, rock rabbit (Ochotona collaris)
  ono k'eq' 〃
  d+l+k'eq' delk'eq' there is the call of the rock rabbit

k'et' /stretch, tough/ potentially an ancient derived root, k'+t' indef.+beneath
  k'et'a, k'eda (adj.) tough,
  -k'et'a (n.) tendons
  ch'ik'eda (n.) NL medium hard, flexible wood
  -nk'et'a (n.) gristle
      -qank'et'a (n.) IO Achilles tendon K'uzhaghałen gun yeqank'et'a ghin
      yentsał K'uzhaghałen was chopping away on his Achilles tendon
      -qant'uk'et'a (n.) tendon in front of ankle
      -tsik'et'a (n.) U cowlick, crown of feathers
      q'ey k'eda, q'ey k'et'a (n.) thin willows used for lashing q'eyk'et'a jageyniłk'eł ch'u qeyeł
      niłnuk'uyesh they split this willow to tie things with
   neu k'et' 〃 k'it
   rev k'et k'eł k'at
   mom k'at k'at' k'eł
   prg k'eł
   trans k'at k'at' k'eł k'at
   G+l+k'et' (dim.) be tough, durable, sinewy, hard to chew or tear, to congeal dghelk'et' it is
  tough, e.g. meat; dnaghelk'et' liquid (blood) is thick, coagulated; t'qughelk'et' (house) is
  durable, solid; t'qughelk'eth t'qiluq he made the place (home) solid; t'ilk'at' it became tough,
  durable; gudałtin denilk'at blood coagulated; nkunun'a neł t'ilk'at da neł ch'duqitnitułt'agh if
  your game is hard for you to kill, you will have misfortune; udekena daghelk'et' its stem is
  flexible, sinewy, bendable; uyich' didilk'at' its breath became durable, animal resist dying; sheł
  t'ilk'at' it became hard with me, i.e. animal resisted me; deshishch'idnulk'et'i niłtu
  qghich'ex they lived for good health; lit. 'for durable breath they breathed' PK91:202-
  203 gudałtin dnaghelk'et' the blood is thick, coagualted, congealed; k'ta'a dnaghelk'et' the
  soup is congealed; denilk'at' it congealed; de'ulk'eł it is getting thick (as ice cream)
   G+Ø+k'et' (ext.) be stretched, taut; flexible object extends in a line, hangs; geographic feature
  extends in a line
     nik'et' it extends (rope, line); eygu yus nik'danik'et' gu yach' qenghunu out there it (reef)
  extends to that bend; tl'ił nanik'et' it (rope) stretched; nunanik'et' it stretches
  across; nuk'naniłk'et' he has sth. stretched across; hnanik'et' a line of bluffs, ridges
  extend; I nughik'et' it is hanging down (curtain, line); nuk'ghik'et' sth. is hanging
  down; vetsighu nundazk'et' her hair hangs down; nuk'ghik'at' sth. (fish fence) is in
  position; hkaynaghik'et' (boats) extend in a line along the river; yatniqazk'et' I yach'
  naqazk'et' O niqadayk'et' U it is inside out; yatniqazk'et'i sth. that is inside
  out; yatniqazk'et'i nughetggezh he put it on inside out; tl'ił ninik'et' the rope is

 stretched; nch'u tl'ił ni'ik'et the rope is not stretched; vech'annazk'et they (ropes) extend out

   moustache derivatives with heł darkness, dusk, via J. Tenenbaum veduvunu q'udishlahdi hełnik'et' ha'
  t'edyuq on his lip a moustache barely was starting to appear; lit. 'dusk strung across one's
  lip' veduvunu hełnik'et on his lip a moustache barely was starting to appear; lit. 'dusk is strung

   INC#O+G+ł+k'et' (mot.) move O using INC, go pulling rope batl'iłch'k'dnalk'et' we pull boat
  (upriver) with a rope; vaqilin at tuq'u tuq'is ghinidi qeyniqatl'ił'ełk'et' ha q'uyehdi
  chiqeyedeł'ish in bidargis they would surround a sea otter and they would kill it; V. Roehl 1980:13
   ta#d+n+D+k'et' (mot.) make a wake2 in water vatadnak'et' it is making a wake
   k'+i+n+gh+Ø+k'et' (stat.) to frown, have sour expression k'inghazk'et' k'ighnazk'et O he is
  frowning, has a pouty face, always cranky k'ighentuk'et O he is gonna frown
   hu+l+k'et' (op.) exercise (one's body) hulk'et' he is exercising, training; veł hudelk'et' he
  stretches himself with it, does chin up on it; hu'elk'et' I exercise
      qeyeł hudelk'edi (n.) U veq' nik'unuch'ghilk'edi I chinning game
   tl'ił#n+ł+k'et' (mot.) walk in single file tl'iłhniłk'et' they are in a line; ts'itl'iłch'k'nałk'et' we
  are walking in a line, single-file; ts'itl'iłch'nałk'et' we (pl.) are walking in a line, marching
  straight; I tl'iłhniłk'et' they are lined up even, strung out; batl'iłch'k'dnalk'et' we are pulling
  sth. (boat) with a line
   d#i+l+k'at' (tran.) be tough to kill, to resist dying neł t'ilk'at' da if it is hard for you to
  kill; uyich' didilk'at' it won't die

     K'eskaq' (p.n.) Port Graham perhaps with this root important Den. place name reported by

     Kodiak; V. Roehl 1980:13

 Grp. 1. postpositons etc.
 -k'u (p.p.) away from, off from, thwarting ch'u chik'enaq k'u niłhquldih they teach one another
 to prevent sickness
 k'u'i (n.) prevention, preventative, thwarting, curing
    qes k'u, qes k'u'i (n.) cures for a cold see P. Kalifornsky's elaborate essay, PK91:222-225
    kun k'u'i (n.) OI raincoat lit. 'one that keeps away rain'
    ts'ix k'u'i (n.) O ts'is k'u'i U nanldałi k'u'i I mosquito lotion lit. 'one that prevents
    mosquitos'
 -k'uch' (p.p.) opposite, away from, protecting from, distinct P qil'i k'uch' na'inten protect us
 from evil; qilch' na'engi'a nak'uch' inłkit take our sins from us; shlik'a shk'uch' ighetneq he
 took dog from me; dakaq'di hk'uch' opposite the door; hk'uch' qilchi it changed; hk'uch'
 quht'ana ezdlan he became a different person; hk'uch' geluch ghini ighetneq he brought that
 different shaped key; uk'uch' niłch'ey ch'en the downwind side; tagh'i eła qeyk'uch'
 hyiten they hold the oar away from it; Dutna guna qeyk'uch' hch'anaqehnalyit the Eskimos
 started running back away from him
    k'uch'na, k'uch'itna (n.) people of opposite clan
    niłk'uch' (adv.) various, different, assorted, opposite niłk'uch quht'ana qilan there are
    different (various) people; niłk'uch' k'et'un q'it'a'idini du? do you know different
    plants?; niłk'uch' k'eqilen there are a variety of things; niłk'uch' dazdlu they (words) sound
    different; niłk'uch' nqilt'an they look different; ggih gguya niłk'uch' t'ent'a there are all
    different kinds of little worms; tl'ił niłk'uch' hyanesh they are pulling a rope in opposite
    directions (tug-of-war)
    niłk'uch' denqidnilt'anch' (n.) multi-colored objects
    niłk'uch' vadentnexi (n.) NL niłk'uch' batnetneshi OUIl accordion lit. 'one that is pushed
    back and forth'
    -ghk'uch'en (p.p.) the opposite side from, the side against sheghk'uch'en on opposite side
    from me; niłeghk'uch'en ch'vala skiyeh ey hnastsut they sat down across from each other
    under the spruces; qaniłch'eyi beghk'uch'enh tanshu I went against the wind; qaniłch'eyi
    eghk'uch'enh batanshu I went against the wind
 Grp. 2. as disjunct prefix
 P+k'u# (v.pf.) from, off, away
    P+k'u# (a.d.s. n mom) away from, off from P hk'unineq it fell off; ggagga ghin hk'udalen
    ch'q'u veq'es k'idghetl' the bear fell off and broke his neck; hk'utadanilu wave withdrew
    from shore; niłk'uk'daniłen idi eła qunsha qeneh t'qighishin gheli she opened her eyes and
    then there was a really nice ground squirrel camp house
    K'unust'in (p.n.) Kijik Mountain, 2.3, 93; Bodenberg Butte, QQ lit. 'one that stands apart'
    tik'u (a.d.s. n mom) into the woods, back country tik'uniyu he went into the
    woods; tik'uqnidatl' they went to woods
 IU, nuk'u# O">nik'u# IU, nuk'u# O (a.d.s. n mom) away, off from shore ff are from O dora
 nuk'uniban he swan out to the dory; dorach' nuk'unibash swim out to the
 dory; nuk'uniyush go out to it (in water); nuk'uch'ibashigu don't swim out there; bech'
 nuk'utiyush go out from shore to it
 Grp. 3. derived roots and lexemes

  come k'usht'a ki nothing else

k'u², k'a, k'i *k'ɑ /also/    /√::ENC/
  k'u (enc.) O k'a U k'i I too, also appears after most word cat, but not after VERB shi k'i
  ench'u I shi k'u k'usht'a O shik'a nch'u U me neither; agreeing with someone who already
  said no shik'i I shi k'u O shi k'a U me too; nayi k'i I nayi k'a U nayi k'u O we(2)
  too; hin k'i I you (pl.) too; qudi k'i I they themselves too; yina k'i I them guys too; nen k'u
  nuntgheshtuh O I'll help you too; yadi kazhna k'u qenchi k'u lagu yadi ninya
  daqadilggeshi O also for either lynx or porcupine, the climbing animals; gu k'u O here
  too; ndahduh k'u shu qch'aninyu? O where might you have come from?; gin heyi ch'u ki heyi
  da k'u O this year and, in years ahead, more years
     A q'u k'i, B q'u k'i (cnj.) either A or B ADD CHK

k'un /food/    /d√::N!-food/ Den. does not use the typical verb 'to eat', *haˑ
  k'un, -k'una (n.) UIO -duk'una L food, feast uncommon, archaic łhk'unch' t'qeł'ih they are
  feasting, celebrating; PK91:210
     ghelguts'i k'una (n.) N freshwater herring, least cisco (Coregonus sardinella) lit. pike's food
     k'undet (n.) IO k'unde U famine, without food k'unde nihnidaq famines struck

k'us, k'es /fish bin/  /√::N!-tech/ cf. Ahtna c'osk'ae ≡
  k'usq'a (n.) I k'esq'a O fish bin, rock or willow fish bin, fish cutting hole cf. k't'usq'a U ≡ łiq'a
  k'usq'a hq'atugheshlu I put salmon in the fish bin

                                                      l/ł


   Dene Lexware dictionary comparisons:   AAD1990: l/ł: 261-284, KAD2000: l/ł (part) 374-426, LTDD: l/ł
  MTDD: l/ł; YMM92: l/ł: 357-395

l¹ *lə /l-classifier/     /ⓐ::vpf+-‴/
  Grp. 1. l-classifier v.pf. functions
  l- (v.pf.) classifier
      D-effect+ł (n.d.s.) passive lchin it was made
      nu#ł → l (n.d.s.) iterative nuynil'an he is loooking at her again; cf. yenił'an he is looking at
      her
      P+pp#(#)(d+)D+ł (n.d.s.) indirect reflexive nk'dal'un he is wearing sth. (a hat)
      l (t.f.s.) derived intransitive
       l+ta (mot.) move the body
      O+l (t.f.s.) derived transitive
       O+G+l+datl' eat plural objects cf. O+G+ł+datl' move, throw plural objects
      INC#l (t.f.s.) derived intransitive with incorporate
      INC#l (t.f.s.) derived transitive with incorporate as object
      INC#O+l (t.f.s.) derived transitive with incorporate of manner
      d+G+gh l (t.f.s.) (desc.) descriptive themes dghelnesh it is tastey
      hu+D-effect (n.d.s.) reflexive chihulyuq he killed himself; hudnil'an he is looking at himself
      nił+l (n.d.s.) reciprocal, each other nuniłhenaljeh they hit each other in the face again
      d+l (t.f.s.) move oneself
       d+l+'ats to step, take a step
      Ø+pp(#)#(d+)D-effect (n.d.s.) indirect reflexive
  d+l (t.f.s.) onomatopoetic, be noise, sound of
       d+l+lesh (op-ono.) be the sound of striking of sticks
  INC#d+l (t.f.s.) derived intransitive with incorporate of oral noise
       INC#d+l+yu° walk while INC nucheghdulyał he is waling and crying
  Grp. 3a. -l in verb formation
  ROOT+l (v.f.sf.) derived onomatopoetic roots
   a few ono. theme that may have derived roots
     d+l+zil (ono.) to ring, be hollow noise a verb from zi branch, zi+l tube k'dghełzil I rang
    sth; sergu k'delzil the church (bell) is ringing; hdelzil sound of hollow ice
   d+Ø+qul U-IO (op.) whine, meow, shrill cry (as fox, cat) dayqul he screamed
     P#d+Ø+qul (op.) to sing soprano k'dequl she is singing lead soprano (Ahtna-style)
    Grp. 3b Themes with potential derived roots ending in -l
     chi#l+'ul to play
     n+Ø+yul (conv.) to spend the night, days journey

ł¹ *ł /ł-classifier/    /ⓐ::AF-‴/ see also related root eł with P, comitative
  Grp. 1. ł-classifier in verbs
  ł+ (v.pf.) classifier
                                             O+ł+chi make sg. O; G+ł+kit food is in position
   appears thematically with no obvious function
  ł (t.f.s.) theme meaning contrast ł+tun enclosed object is in position; cf. Ø+tun elongated is in
  position
  O+ł+ (t.f.s.) derived transitive forms transitive themes from
  nouns tlegh oil; O+G+ł+tlegh anoint, smear O; xeł club; O+G+ł+ghel club O
  O+ł (t.f.s.) derived causative this is very common O+ł+chegh make O cry; cf. chegh he is
  crying O+G+ł+t'a roast, fry, bake O; cf zt'a it is roasted O+G+ł+'u keep O in position; cf. z'un
  it is in position
      P#ł (t.f.s.) derived indirect causative P#O+ł+qet' feed O to P; cf. k'elqat he is eating
      something
  d#gh+ł (t.f.s.) (neu.) dimensional theme formation unique to Dena'ina and
  Ahtna dghiłkugh I dghiłkegh UO
  INC#ł (t.f.s.) derived transitive with incorporate as object inc#G+ł+'u° keep inc body part
  extended
  inc#O+ł (t.f.s.) derived transitive with incorporate of manner INC#O+ł+datl' move pl. O in
  manner of INC
  INC#ł (t.f.s.) derived intransitive with incorporate of manner INC#ł+'u be in position in manner
  of INC
  Grp. 2. inflectional uses of ł
  ł (v.sf1.) suffix: progressive, future, perfective-negative
      gh+ROOT-ł (if.s.) progressive gheyuł he is walking along
      t+gh+ROOT+ł (if.s.) future tgheshyuł UI tgheshuł O I will walk
      i+ROOT+ł+ą́ (if.s.) perfective-negative nch'u iyul he did not arrive

la¹ /exist‴/
   neu lan (le') la' lał / lah
   rev lah lan łah lah
  tran lah lat lah lah
  cont la 〃
   Note that the inceptive stem is la' but the imperfect, optative, and future stems are łah.
  Grp. 1. the common verb theme with many derived nouns, adverbs
   COMP G+Ø+la (desc.) be COMP, is COMP, COMP exists; in s transitional become
  COMP nlan it is, he is
      with Ø-gender Dena'ina nlan he is a Dena'ina; Dena'ina ch'ilan we are Dena'ina; bida
     ełan UO vida eshlan I I am his friend kił ghun betlen yilan that man is his brother-in-
     law; qil eshlan I feel bad
  be cold I ezhi eshlan I am cold; ezhi nlan he is cold; ezhi zdlan he got cold; ezhi ch'ilan we
  are cold; ezhi inlan du? are you cold?; ezhi tayeshla' I am starting to get cold; ezhi gheshla' I
  was cold; ezhi tgheshlał I will be cold

 that fish trap; yuzhun qilan it is clear, blue sky; suy qilan there is sand; vejex qilan there are
 caribou; guhdi ch'tuduł ch'q'uhdi naqayeh qitulał we'll live here and this will be our village; iy
 gudi unhtsah Qizhjeh ht'ana qayeh qighilat here was the first village of the Kijik people; iqech'
 ghu t'qet'an hnuyu hva łitl'en qizdlan ha' thus they kept on until it became spring on them; hva
 shan nuhtasdla' it was starting to turn summer again for them; hendi eła dakaq' ghu qałnigi
 qighila ghu łu dakaq' htazdla' łu all of a sudden in the doorway rock where it had been doorway
 it became; qayeh qighila' a village had been there; lach'u beł qilan O q'ula'a veł qilan I he is

    Dena'ina ghistle' O he is not Dena'ina; ench'u ezhi ghi'eshlal I k'usht'a ezhi ghełal O I

     transitional ezhi zdlan he got cold; ezhi qizdlan area became cold; ezhi qtułah it will become
    cold; ezhi qelah the area is customarily cold; ni'i dghiliq' tuch'ezdatl' hq'u na'a ełkun
    yagheli nch'u na'a qełak well up the mountain we came up but on us it keeps raining good not
    on us there is never good weather; hnayeh ezdlan he got lonesome; łiq'a ezdlan ha' he
    became a salmon; qut'an nusdlan he became a person again; beł k'yizdlan it seemed
    different, it turned into sth. different; k'nusdlan he is ready; dakaq' htazdla it became a
    door; qil zdlan he died; used with persons, more polite than chidyuq eyu zdlan he became
    bashful; szaga qizdlan I I got exhausted; nch'u ki qil łak it does not get bad again; vadi
    ghini suy ezdlan the boat turned to sand; q'uyehdi Chulyin nuqesdlan łu hdnilen ha'
    shtuhdnalen then they became Ravens again and they took off and they flew away; shan
    nuqisdlan it became summer again; q'et'en ezdlan he got old; endahdi q'u enheya
    hdazdlan? how old are you?; 28 shheya hdazdlan I became 28 years old; nughedyuł ch'q'u
    vezaga qizdlan as he was walking back, he became tired; iqech' ghu t'qet'an hnuyu hva
    łitl'en qizdlan ha'. hva shan nuhtasdla' thus they kept on until spring it became for them,
    summer it was starting again for them; k'nuyeshdlan I am ready, prepared; ve'u nuqisdlan he
    got his wife back; begguya qizdlan O k'iydlan begguya she had a child; naqeli
    nuggagganusdlan in fall it became an animal again; q'et'en tułah he is going to get old; ten
    qil tułah the ice will get bad; yahets' hyanq'u t'qijuq chinaggi k'tułah du qit'anaghiyen he
    knew that just as it happened that a flu would come; heyi qełah ch'u ts'iłghetna tuda qelunna
    nudelyash every year twenty or thirty people would be born; ench'u ezhi yeshlagh I am never
    cold; eva eshlah I am cust. cold; ezhi qełah the area is cust. cold; ki k'usht'a beqenga
    nuqidlagh there was no more word of him; ench'u nuk'htusdlagh there won't be anymore of
    sth.; yagheli nugheshdlahi ghuda nch'u t'i'esht'e' shida I'll never get well
    again; shnudnuqidasdla' I to me traditions, history have returned; AE, high language nch'u
    ki qil łak it won't turn bad, e.g. some stored food; yagheli nutudlah he will get well again; ey
    gu q'u nheł yeshdu it da ki nch'u yagheli nha htułagh yida' yełni łu if I stay here with you
    guys the weather won't be good, she told him
 Grp. 2. derived lexemes, often analyzable

    got a cramp in the thigh
    vek'eshlaha (n.) I backbone hump

 k'elahi (n.) materials, building materials

    q'uł denlahi (n.) I whetstone rock
 Grp. 3. other lexemes, many reshaped but likely with la¹

    k'ta'a baqil'a (n.) O soup bowl

    k'uhda'i yes vanq'ashli (n.) I moose skin boat (smaller), canoe

    the ground cache'

    slowly; huqedlan ghinyuł you are walking slowly
