    k'ggun k'elit (n.) O bow guard
 Grp. 4. derived verb themes
  P#q+G+i+la+l U (u:tran-neg.) P is gone, absent, dead, disappears k'qilal it
 disappeared; k'qitustlagh it will disappear; nlugh k'a k'qistlak it hasn't yet disappeared; na'ił
 ta'iltlet ch'u naqistlak we went under water and we disappeared; nch'uk'a! nch'uk'a
 naqitustlagh shit'i! quht'ana k'ilan shit'i; du ch'u naqitustlagh? no! we won't disappear! there
 are lots of people. how could we disappear?; Susitnu gu k'qitustlagh gheli this Susitna will
 disappear; ełnen gini łuq'u qubeq'e łuq'u k'qitustlagh from this earth all their tracks will
 disappear
  P+e+yeni#d+Ø+la I (desc.) sg. be thinking of P veynik'deshlan I'm thinking of him
  P+(e)de G+D+la (desc.) to lack, to miss, be without P bedeyeshdlat I am lacking, missing it
 (rifle, knife); shch'da bede'eshdlan I am missing my blanket; yedesdlat he lacks it; qenayeh
 k'u vedeyeshdlat I miss him for company; ch'da dedlan he is lacking a
 blanket; bedech'esdlat we lack it
  ch'i#gh+gh+ł+le' I (desc-neg.) be rough, reckless ch'ighaghiłe' he is are
 rough; ch'inhghaghiłe' they are rough, they play roughly; ch'du'idghaghiłe he is
 grouchy; ch'ich'ghaghiłe'a don't be rough; q'ut'un gu ch'du'idghaghiłe this morning he is
 grouchy; veł ch'ighaghełe I harrass him
  P#q+Ø+la (rev.) P is born shqizdlan I was born; veqizdlan it was born, it became
 available; łik'aha qezdlan ch'u łik'aggwa qbegguya ezdlan they turned into dogs and the puppies
 had their babies; qevgguya nch'u hghilal their child never gave birth; t'esha k'i nch'u kisht'a
 vehghile' there are not many black ground squirrels born

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                  311

   P+#i+n+Ø+la L (u:con.) stalk game2 This theme is a thematized conative of the theme 'catch
  game' which does not occur in Denaina, cf. Koyukon yeldlaanh 'he caught it', ye'ooneegheelaa' 'he
  stalked it'. itinigheshla I'll hunt him, after spotting a caribou; ininla stalk it; yinighila he stalked
  it; yinala he is stalking it; itinighila du'? yełni łu will you hunt it? he said to him
  Grp. 2. various adverbs, enclitics
  OPT-VERB ilay (if.s.) IO OPT-VERB ilagi Il negative optative, don't, one should not
  V hghinax ilay I hghinash ilagi Il don't talk; vets'iłt'an gun chidghiłnah ilay don't kill his
  partner; iy ch'niyuda hghinax ilay when we get there don't talk; eqech' t'ghit'an ilay don't do
  that when you are there; ch'uqighił q'u ki dghił'an ilay ki di don't treat him badly again
  la'a (adv.) still, for a while la'a q'u k'deli he is still singing; la'a qut'ana qisen yet still there
  were no people there
  lach', lach'q'u (adv.) q'ula'a I lach'u O truly, it is true, certain q'ulach'q'u OU q'ula'a dini
  dit I lach'q'u dini dit? UO are you certain?; Q'u lach' dini dit? are you kidding?, is what you
  say true?; Balluta Evanoff 2005 q'ula'a nuqulnek I q'ulach'q'u nuqulnek OU he told it truly,
  he told the truth; q'ula t'dini da ha', nats'iłt'an ghun yagheli if you are telling the truth, our
  husband is okay; lach'u beł qilan O q'ula'a veł qilan I he is certain
  lach'q'u (adv.) always, constantly lach'q'u shełni I he always tells me; lach' du q'u k'ghetnuh
  dit should we always work, ^let's take a rest; q'ulach'(u) I always, constantly
        lach' gheli q'u (adv.) I completely, entirely, with all his might lach' gheli q'u
        vidadalven all the way up she filled it; lach' gheli q'u vidaz'unch' q'u ghizhel he hollered
        with all his might
  laghu (adv.) I a guess, surmise, it could be, similar to lay 'I wonder if' but laghu can be used with
  all pronouns dinchin laghu ench'u nghukiełkeł maybe you're hungry but I didn't feed you; shi
  laghu am I the one; eg shi laghu ekgedu ni'ił'an da am I the one that is hurting you; nen
  laghu are you the one
  lay, lau (enc.) I laga U lagi lay, lau OU I wonder, it seems similar to laghu, but laghu can be
  used with all pronouns lay seems to mean only speaker is expressing wonder nutgheshdyu
  lay I nutgheshju laga U nutgheshju lagu O I wonder if I should go back; nutudyu lay I
  wonder if he should go back alone; nli ghini hnalqeni lay I nli ghini hnalqeni lagu O nelni
  ghini hnalqeni laga U I wonder if that steambath is hot; ndanen q'u qenunes ghenachen be'u
  lagu kił gguya lagu O I wonder which one he loved more, his wife or the little boy; ezhi enlanen
  layen I ezhi enlanen lagu O I wonder if she is cold; nalqen nenlayen? is he
  hot?; hk'untgheshdyu lay? shall I go back?; łuhtsich'tghulkit lay I wonder if we should
  swim; taghetneq lay ideshni I taghetneq lagu ideshni O I thought that he was tired; "Debel'
  dniya ndakat ninu'iju t'ghit'a lagi." "The one saying his own Bel' has stopped at your door it
  seems."
  Grp. misc lexemes posssibly with la
  k'q'iliha (n.) L inner thigh meat, sirloin

lach¹ /cook by boiling/     /r√::Vconv-cook-temp/ cf ł-badr in LT, Koy.
  conc lach 〃       lish
  neu lach
   mom lash lach lesh lash
   O+G+Ø+lach (conv.) cook O, cook O by boil1ing k'ezhdlach he cooked sth., cooked by boiling
  (e.g. boiled some meat); shdlach it is cooked; chayi yishlach I made a tea party, cooked a
  feast; chayi zhdlach ^he made a tea party; lit. 'he boiled tea' giga neshlach I am cooking
  berries; hyu k'ezdlach they cooked sth. for him; k'dashdlach, huk'dashdlach he cooked for
  himself; k'ghinlesh you used to cook
    q'uyehdi hyak'dedlach then they could cook sth. in it; bak'yełach OU I cooked sth. in it (in a
  pot); yeghunzhdlach he cooked part of it; yik'ghilach she canned sth.; nik'nilach she finished
  cooking; k'ghinlesh you used to cook; q'azełach I cooked them all; q'ananshdlach O I
  unintentionally cooked them all; q'anitigheshdlesh I will unintentionally cook them all; elugh
  q'azhnashdlazh I havn't yet cooked them all unintentionally; łduk'nilach he cooked sth. for a
  long time; łduk'nełach O I cooked sth. for a long time; tiduzełach O I cooked on and on
  (without finishing); tidughełach I cooked it the wrong way; dek'ighilach I t'k'ighelach O she
  likes to cook; pot.
   with ti# vahtitudlesh it will boil away; vahtighedlach IO it boiled away; vahtidlash it is
  boiling away
     yuyeh k'edlesh (n.) indoor kitchen

     gulaja (n.) boil1ed water
     vak'edlaji, vak'edlesi (n.) cooking pot
     vuk'edlaji (n.) I veghu k'edlaji galeq I cookbook lit. 'guide to how to cook'
     dik'qelasht (n.) O cannery lit. 'where they cook sth. in containers'
   P+a+ti#q+i+Ø+lach IO (u:gh mom.) boil P away yahtighilach he boiled it
  away; vahtighedlach it boiled away; vahtidlash it is boiling away; vahnutidlesh it boils away
  now and then; vahtitudlesh it will boil away

lagh¹ *leˑx ̣ /swim underwater/        /√::Vmot-bio/
  mom lah lagh leh lih
  per leh lagh leh lah
  per leh 〃
   dist lah lagh lah lah
   IDF+Ø+lagh (mot.) IDF fish swims, pl. fish run, animal, fowl swims underwater, usually used for
  fish but can be used for people, beaver whales, muskrat, otter, swimming underwater, but not
  acquatic birds. It can be used for one or a small number. Note that l+ghuzh is used for large
  numbers of fish running. k'nilagh fish are running, the fish arrived; łiq'a n'u na'a k'nilagh in the
  month of June the fish came; nusdlaghi naqeliteh leh silver salmon run in fall; łiq'a qighishin
  nu'idlagh there was a good run of salmon; quyeshi hughilagh the beluga surfaced; tałin
  tanilagh the whale dove down underwater; nał izdlagh salmon run passed by us; shghena
  tazdlagh it swam near me; łiq'a nayesh idilagh the salmon ran passed by us; q'uyehdi huzhehi
  ghini deggech' huleh then those blackfish swim up and into it; hch'anilagh it swam out (as
  fish); yeghes ch'anilagh he swam out in front of him; yut'e' yi eł nik'un'idlagh he swam
  downstream with him; venut nuk'ididlih salmon go past him; niqana'idlagh he (it) swam (back)
  to the beach; nik'unshlagh I swam out; nagh t'k'ilagh he caught one! (in net); k'emeq'
  tuzdlagh first fish came to the fish pond from a lake or river; qughilagh it swam to the surface,
  e.g. whale, salmon, beaver; tanilagh it (usually beaver, person) swam down
  underwater; dighilagh it swam into it (fish trap); hulehga shegh k'niq' ghilagh whitefish swam
  through the snare on me; nugheleh it is swimming around; qunshi ghin baydalgi yesh begh
  quyeleh beluga comes up by him alongside the skin boat; tubugh hch'en tan'i bik'elehi
  qełchix on the beach side they made a fish trap that fish swim into
     shanlaghi (n.) summer fish run, main run

     chulan (n.) five cents see DTD 2013:324, PK91: 268-269 PK91:269 "A dried beaver castor was
     established as worth five cents, probably early in the fur trade era. The beaver castor would then be used
     as trap bait" PK "The Chinese laborers used to purchase chulan from the natives around the 1890's in the
     canneries. This is probably why chulan came to mean five cents." ch'qilu qbekets' ghu chulan ch'u
     hquldin qudiq' q'u with their five fingers and the beaver castor, they learned their own way (of
     counting money)

lu¹ la /hand, limb‴/     /√::N!-anat/
d.f.: lu, lugh, lughe, lunu, d√ -laq'a palm
   -lu (n.,c.) finger, hand, limb, branch, extremity the common noun for 'hand, arm' is -gguna, but lu
   is found in many compound nouns and incorporates that refer to hands, fingers, or branches. Also
   related are the areal nouns -lugh 'edge of blade', and -lugh 'holding on to O' as well as several
   nouns with -lugh.
       -lunu (a.n.) O edge uncommon once noted by E Baktuit
       k'elut'ich'a (n.) U peeled spruce bark
       k'elutuna (n.) inner bark
       k'eludiya (n.) U k'elutemisha L k'elubisha NIl spruce cone
       k'delu (n.) branch tips, tree top
       -lukiyiq' (n.) IO fingertips
       q'eylu (n.) willow branch
   ch'qilu (n.) ch'qiluh, ch'qiludi OI five probably referring to one hand; also puns on lu° plural
   oblects
       ch'qiluna (n.) five people
       ch'qilu hluzhun (n.) OI ch'qilda qelun U fifty; 10.00
       ch'qilu n'u (n.) U February lit. 'fifth month'
       ch'qilu dingi (n.) one dollar
   qeluzhun (n.) OI qelun U U ten lit. 'bare hands'
       -jeghlu (n.) earlobe
       luts'egh (n.) ring
       -luts'eq'a (n.) index finger
       -lukena (n.) wrist, upper finger joints
   lutdeq, -lutdegga (n.) I lukdeq OU thimble
       -luggezh (a.n.) between his fingers
       -luzheha (n.) IO -lujega IlT -lujek'a O fingers, claws
   luqet'anen (n.) I midwife
   lu (i.) hand, limb luqidnaghiłdenen he is handy; ludghaldatl' felt his way along (darkness) with
   his hands
   -lu (a.n.) OI sharp edge (of knife, axe blade) belu qul it is dull, has no edge; yelu niqiłchin he
   sharpened it, remade its edge
       -lugh (a.n.) within the hands niłlugh yiten they are both holding it (stick); yelugh

   mom     la     lu    lał    lix

   cont la         la' la'     la'
   pers lah 〃

   examples are presented 1) regular derivatives of main theme, 2) prod. sub-themes with extra
  thematic prefixes or incorporates and which have several derivatives, 3) uncategorized subthemes,
  4) other derivatives with incorporates, 5) other nominalizations
  Grp. 1. stative-neuter themes
   G+Ø+lu° (stat.) plural objects are in position; sg. rope-like object is in position gech' zdlu gloves
  are there; k'ezdlu he has some things; nazdlu n-objects are there; beads, sg. rope or pl. coiled
  ropes, spool of thread, fish eggs k'ełq'a k'eyeshlu I have traps at the kill site; naqeliteh
  ch'k'ezdlu in fall we set sth. (trap); k'ełq'a k'ezdlu he has traps at the kill site; geluch ghini
  kut'yilyu he has the keys in his pocket; tahvił tazdli nets are in the water; I loaned them out
  already; gega qu shegh nazdlu there are a few berries by me; gega eyghu k'i k'nazdlu there are
  a few berries over there too; chik'a dazdlu wood is there; t'ch'k'ghilu we used to have snares
  set; chashga dazdlu cups are there; chashga qidazdlu cups are in a hole in the
  ground; k'enchan ht'idazdlu, qidazdlu grass is in a hole; qałnigi dnazdlu rocks are on
  ground; dednazdlu there are objects up; on shelf, roof, cups on shelf yashik adazdlu stuff in a
  box
   with l+lyu° laq'a dazelyu I am holding a bunch of (pencils) in my hand; q'in laq'a nazelyu I am
  holding fish eggs in my hand; naq'ak'ezelyu I am wearing glasses; jeghk'enazelyu I am wearing
  earrings; suk'dazelyu I have snuff in my mouth; sunuk'dazelyu I have snuff in my mouth
  again; davak yidudazelyu I have tobacco, chaw, snuff in my mouth; gech' denlggeyi kut' nelyu
  ha' he has white mittens in his pocket

   I walk around

Russkaia Amerika v zapiskakh Kirila Khlebnikova, Novo-Arkhangel'sk. Nauka, 1985, p. 265."
  mandiga (n.) Il vandiga NL canned lard

markasha      not certain, Russian makársha Bistort (Polygonium viviparum) M. Bergelson "Russian for
'sourdock' is shchavel'
   kashi, markasha (n.) IU tash'i O sourdock, bistort (Rumex spp.; Polygonium viviparum) kashi
   is the common term, also reported as tash'i by FS possibly shaped by Dena'ina word from kash
   render; lack of correspondence for the U O dialects is curious; also given F. Sacaloff's specificity,
   these may be distinct plant names

  maryash (n.) O mayash U a card game

   tu#d+n+l+jem (op-ono.) be plunking sound of footsteps or rocks striking water tułchuda

n¹ *n /distributive/       /ⓐ::vpf#/
  n# (v.pf.) distributive important disjunct prefix
  momentaneous derivation + n# (a.d.s. dist) at separate intervals, places, distributive
  superaspect can be considered a superaspect, similar to Navajo da distributive a superaspect
  happens after a regular a.d.s has applied dink'taznel he poured sth. into several
  containers; bink'tgheshnel OU I will pour it into several containers; bink'ehnił you pl. fill
  them; ndanłbech O I stretched several (skins)
  typical distributive paradigm
        dist-pos     I                 P             F              O
  1sg van'eshnił ban(yey)zeshnel bantgheshneł bayngheshnił
  3sg yanyenił banyeznel               banyetuneł
  1p banch'enił banch'eznel            banch'tuneł
   S (is) pouring O into several containers
        dist-neg      with         nch'u           k'usht'a
  1sg banzeshnil ban'i'eshneł bantezgheshnel
  2sg banzinil        ban'inneł    bantezghinel    banch'eyenełigu
  2pl. banzehnil ban'i'ehneł bandzihnel
   S (is not) pouring O into several containers
  order of n nehvaya ghusench'ila ‡^ we are skinning pl. rabbits; vaq'anch'hdunił ni you should
  dump them (liquids) out; hduts'enesendenghalten I overflow ice formed into plural bluffs
  n# (if.s.) plural humans (dimensional themes), plural features (extension themes) ntiztun
  qinaghelt'a there are many trails; ntiqełtun they have plural trails; dench'iłdes O we are
  heavy; nehvaya ghusench'ila ‡^ we are skinning pl. rabbitsex denqełkugh; they are
  big; denhtułkugh they will be big; niłenqilkigh UO I niłenqilkugh they are as large as each
  other
       neu             I                             P                F
  3s daghiłtey         dedghiłtey~dighiłtey          detdułtey

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                            348

  1p dench'dałetey dench'dghiłtey                  dench'tudułtey
  2p dendazełtey dendiłtey                         dentdiłtey
   3p denhdałtey       denhdghiłtey~denhdighiłtey denhtdułtey
   plural (are) strong
  n#i+ (if.s.) I they, many, multiple, larger number seems to be like Koy. multiple k'tsen
  nch'ilqat we (many) are eating meat; k'tsen nch'ighelqet' we many were eating
  meat; t'anch'q'u nk'i'elqat everyone eat!; t'anch'q'u łiq'a n'i'elqat everyone eat
  fish; nyedivach I yedivash Il he is stretching many; nqyedivach they stretched, spread out
  many skins; nch'u desnqisen they (mult.) are not good people; nk'qilnix L they pl. are drinking
  n# (a.d.s. cont) keep on Ving wth gh perf., not common in Den. n'esh'u' I am carrying it (ball)
  and walking with it; nk'du'uł he is alive; lit. 'he is carrying time along' nk'de'u he is
  living; ts'iq'u nk'dghesh'uł I'm still alive; nk'dghi'u' he was living; neba UO he is swimming,
  keeps on swimming; n'eshba I keep on swimming; nya' he keeps on walking; ndesten I keep
  on carrying it (stick); ndiistaan, ndghastaan I kept on carrying it
  n# (t.f.s.) repeated actions nhqilyu they searched; nqeł'en he, I sense a spirit; nk'edleq' UI she
  is laughing; nk'ilges jerk your hook up and down
      P+n# (a.d.s. gh mom, s neu) wearing P, up and onto P (as when putting on a hat or
      coat) nk'dalkits he is wearing sth.; nunk'edelyash, nunk'elya he is getting
      dressed; nunditggash O nnuk'itggash I put it (coat) on

n² *n+ /n-qualifier/   /ⓐ::vpf+/
  n (c.) gender, n-connective in various nouns -enchix nose; -entuq' forehead; nkix I nkish
  UO rosehips; k'enli hail; unripe berrieslikely
     k'enteh (a.n.) jagged mountain peaks k'enteh łuhenshyu nudyi iqu I hunted sheep on this
     mountain peak
  n- (v.pf.) n-qualifier
     n+ (n.d.s.) gender, roundish, rope-like tl'ił naz'un a coiled roped is there
     n+ (t.f.s.) thematized gender
      O+n+ł+'an to look at O
     n+ (a.d.s. assume a position naztan he went to bed
     n+D (a.d.s. zmom) errative, do VERB by accident nuk'nalqet' he overate; yenalyeł he
     accidentally caught it; he grabbed on but couldn't let go; beghunayeljel U veghunazeljel
     I beghunanljel O I paddled up on it by accident; k'dnalyich' he choked on
     water; chiydnalyuq I chiynalyuq OU he killed it by accident; ch'u uł ch'nashju yutgge
     qughelggush nudutnik and he (the boy) walked into a bad situation with it where he couldn't
     climb up; shełtun k'inastnik he got credit in error (can't pay for it)
     n+ (t.f.s.) flying nudeł plural birds are flying
     O+n+Ø (t.f.s.) following O yenuyuł he is following her
     n+gh+l (t.f.s.) into a condition nghaltenh it froze solid

     arrived; ch'adaniłen current flows out

n'e, ni¹ *neɁ, nɁe /upstream, back/       /√::DIR‴ (supp.)/
    several broken or paired roots, one of the more flexible directional roots
  d.f.: n'e, n'ech', ni, nih, niq', nit, naq'
  -n'e (dir.) in the upriver direction with motion verbs yun'e yach' hnił'an he was looking upriver
  that way; yun'e dnanilu the ridge extends upriver; yun'e łuhenshyu I walked up the
  lake; ts'in'e heading straight upriver
     n'e (adv.) back n'e hyaniłchet they pushed their caps back; n'e qagh nqildatl' they moved
     back to the wall from the center
     tin'e (adv.) against the current tin'e tghigheshnex dzastets' ghutdghełt'ał I will go against the
     current so I will use a pole
  yun'eh, yun'e (a.n.) in upriver direction, a watershed can be used in place names
     Tutl'uh Yun'e (p.n.) Turnagain Arm lit. 'upstream of rear water'
     yun'eh k'eli (n.) NL mourning song
     yun'eht'an (n.) LN Upper Kuskokwim people
     yun'eh ch'agheyułi (n.) N wolverine lit. 'one that comes out from upriver'
  -n'ech' (adv.) OU toward, from an upriver direction yun'ech' yeh hch'ana'iju she came from
  there; not used as such in Lime Seska'a yun'ech' hghaztunt 'Big Ridge' extends
  upstream; dun'ech' zdu he is staying in the upstream direction
     dun'etna (n.) middle Susitna people (Kroto Creek area)
  -nit (dir.) at a specific place upriver, rear, in back (of boat) ch'ak'tniłtunt yunit valley is back
  there; ch'ak'tniłtunt dunit valley is back up (nearby); dunithch'en one on this upstream side (as
  two boats); dunidi the one in back; yunidi qenen dghiłkegh the hill upstream is big
     dunidi (n.) north wind1 OU; northeast wind N
     yunit (a.n.) IO k'enich'en U stern of boat k'idiki yunit vaqizdlu there's too much load in the
     stern
     yunidi ez'uni, yunidi yiz'uni (n.) rear gun site
     Hnidi (p.n.) Beluga Mountain lit. 'the one upstream' SPA:
  -nich' (dir.) in the direction upriver, behind
     yinich' (n.) U north wind lit. 'from upstream' yinich' iłch'ey the north wind is
     blowing; Tsałt'eshi yunich' hghaztun 'Black Stone Axe' (ridge) points upstream
     k'enich'di (n.) NL rudder
     venich'di (n.) N anchor

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                               350

  -ghnich'en (p.p.) upriver from P
     dunich'en (n.) L south wind
  -nih (dir.) general direction upriver yunih taznu he moved upriver; yunih yunch'dich'en
  ezdu he lives upriver and on other side; dunih nelgguk I ran upriver; dunih qinughudnik he
  went back upriver by boat
  Grp. 2 various lexemes
  niq' (n.) stream the reverse hydromymn is rare in Den.
  Kenaniq' (p.n.) Kuskokwim River lit. 'base, timbered stream' likely founding place name; one of a
  small number of reverse hydronymns SPA:6.61
  -iniq' (a.n.) IO -iniq'e U back (of the body), behind P nayiniq' qadach'daznex we heard sth.
  behind us; yiniq' gheli gu sht'a łu yiłdex he shot him in his back right there; ey ghu ga iden
  hyiniq' ga huhghinechen ki łu that same guy whose back you plucked is there; hulehga shegh
  k'niq' ghilagh whitefish swam through the opening on me
  P+ghenit (p.p.) in back of, behind P sheghnit eydu he lives in back of me; shi q'u yach' negheni
  tghełdeł I can put them away behind you
     k'eghenidi (n.) harpoon drag, harpoon float lit. 'something that is behind' qutsaghił'i k'edeyes
     bighejetl'i k'eghenidi t'ghit'a łu it is said the drag was an inflated seal skin
     nistl'u (n.) IO sternpiece in canoe venistl'u qełchi make a sternpiece for it

na¹:dena *dəneˑ, ne /person, our/      /√ⓐ::N-PRO-VPF-ENC¹‴/ classic Dene dual root-affix
  Grp. 1. nouns
  dena, -dna (n.) man, person
    Dena'ina (n.) Tanaina people
    k'den'a (n.) baby seal
    veghutna qilani, veghudena qilani (n.) I dry red or king salmon lit. 'that which is for the
    people'
  na (n.) person several compounds
  dnayi, nayi (n.) persons, people
    -eł dnayi (n.) clanspeople yina beł dnayi shina'i they are his relatives; -tukda eł
    dnay father's clansmen
    niłdnayi (n.) relatives nqitl' ch'u ts'enłt'ida niłdnayi qilan the salmonberry and the raspberry
    are relatives
    k'tsey dnay (n.) U white people
  na (n.enc.) human plural
    nidnayi (n.) I human beings as called by animals lit. 'hearsay people' incorrect as 'strangers' in
    DTD:82
    deghili dnayi (n.) mountain people in legends
    q'enay (n.) siblings, brothers, sisters
  ina, dna (n.sf.) pl. humans, pl. animals in storytime Dena'ina Dena'ina people; qeshqa
  ina chiefs; niłyida ina mutual friends; vadna who plural
  Grp. 2. pronouns, verb prefixes
    nana (pro.) O nayi I nayna U we

  na (pf.) 1st plural, our, someone, us naqenq'a our house; naq' as us, upon us

na⁵, nak *qə-neˑ /word/         /d√::N-speech/
    from q+na, see highly irregular (y)a¹ na⁵ sg. talks
  qena, hna (n.) word, language, speech dek'isen ghun hnat'u eydu U woman that in silence sat
     qena ghezdunen (n.) I war lieutenant lit. 'one who stay by words'
  qena (i.) talking
   qena#d+l+ghuzh (op-rep.) pl. talk, speak qenahdelghesh they are
  talking; nuhnahdelghesh they are talking again; nuhnach'dulghesh ni let's (bunch of us) talk it
  over
     qenadelgheshi (n.) IU biq' hna qilani O radio
  -qenaga (n.) IO hnaga U language, voice henyi hnaga your pl. language; niłq'u qenaga
  dazdlu the languages sound alike; qenaga k'gheł'ih I called it (e.g. square dance); gini di
  nunendalnent, hnaga shu q'udi beł nuhqelnegi idi this is when the customs became established;
  they were telling this in the language of this present time apparently; SP76-clan
     Dena'ina qenaga, Dena'ina hnaga (n.) Dena'ina language

nagh, nah, nuh *neˑx ̣ /eye/           /√::/N!-anat/ also see related root nu'u
d.f.: nagha, nah, nuh, na, naq'a
   -nagha (n.) IU eye cf. -eł tugh'a ≡O
       -nughuts'ena (n.) IO eyebrow ridge, bone
       -nugheyes (n.) IO -nahyes U eyelid
       tunagha (n.) OI tahnagha U bladderwort (Utricularia spp.) lit. 'water eye'
       tsanagha (n.) OSl sea anemone (Actiniaria) lit. 'rock eye'

  ni let's blow it (fire, campfire, candle) out; shegh nudnasdneq my fire went
  out; vinuk'dnudneh the fire is going out; vinuk'dnastneq the fire started to go out
     daz'i nutnełnehi (n.) fire extinguisher

nes¹ *ŋəth /ahead, toward fire/    /√::DIR‴/
d.f.: nes, dus, s, 'us, nse, ghenes, ts'enes, -useh, d√:ghes, yus,
      note radical allomorphs highly interesting for the derived lexemes and their meanings; nes
   appears to be a unifying concept in the nine-point directional system allomorphs are diverse, with
   the n- often missing
   -useh (dir.) general area ahead, at a distance yuseh nugheł'esh they are migrating way out
   there; duseh nugheł'esh they are migrating out there

  Grp. 2. other lexemes and extensions
  k'ghes (n.) O bone ↇ, razor clam shell mainly used in O, appears to be from ghe in relation to
  plus -s < nes into the fire, ahead, etc. interesting esoteric innovation shegh ghes egedu my bones
  ache, I have arthritis; ninya ghes qyegh hugheltsax they used to abstain from animal
  bones; k'ghes yeghuch'ełkedigu yentulnah qu O don't feed it the bones or he will choke
  qenunes (adv.) O hnes U the most not used in I dialects nutih hnunes hqugh two times as
  much; hnunes q'u beteya qusih more and more of his strength was gone; sez'a beł gheshdunen
  Chickalusion łuq'u hnunes t'k'ghiłih my uncle that I stayed with, Chickalusion, he gathered the
  most; ndanen q'u qnunes dghiłnez? which one is taller?; ndanen q'u qnunes ghenachen does
  he love the most?; yenunes dghelqeni k'tsen ghelqet' I ate the most delicious meat; shnunes q'u
  belik'a dnaghelt'a he has more dogs than me; shnunes dighichin he was hungrier than me

     -nest'a (n.) adult male
     chunest'a (n.) large beaver
     łinest'a (n.) male dog
  k'etnesa, k'tisnesa (n.) stomach lining likely this root
  ts'itnes (adv.) UO ts'ines I straight, neat, orderly, normal ts'idnes zisan stand straight
  up; duyeq ina ił ts'idnes nihdghichet they made agreements with the chiefs; ts'itnes
  niqdalnen UO ts'ines nihdalen I things have settled down, are back to normal; ts'itnes
  qizdlan it became neat, orderly; ts'itnes tinitun the trail is straight
  ts'enes (n.) frozen overflow, aufies

  rut; vatdulnix it will taste good; dghilingek'a bents'isa t'inaghelt'a ch'enłq'ena q'ent'a hq'u
  batnelnesh the blackberry has many seeds like the bunchberry but it is tastey; ch'enłq'ena
  bents'isa ghetnalggen, t'inaghelt'a ch'u batnelnesh hq'u hqul the bunchberry has numerous dry
  seeds and it has no real taste; nkish jitggwa q'u batnelnesh the rosehip is a little
  tasty; vadelnex it is tasty; gemfidi vadelnex the candy tastes good; hyadełnex hnuyu ey ghu
  k'dnal'ina ghuna qeł hghizhel they were tasting it while the hidden ones with them
  hollered; dnigi tsen q'u elnesh it tastes like moose; k'usht'a badilnesh it wasn't
  tasty; nu'ilnik it got tasty again; vadiłnex taste it; batniłnish taste it; yadełnex he is tasting
  it; yadghiłnik he tasted it; łiq'a qighishin vadelnix the fish tastes good; szaq' gu q'u
  vadełnik I can taste it in my mouth; a word you're trying to remember

ni³ /shake, vibrate/ Note that in I the perfective stem has the typical -n perfective suffix; however OU
treat this tlike ni° say, deem and n-perf suffix does not appear
   durUO ni 〃
   durI       nin ni' nił ni' nih
   mom nix nin nił ni
   d+G+D+ni (op.) be shaking, shiver, tremble, vibrate, be nervous, to dart, swim
  swiftly dednin I dedni OU he is shaking, is nervous; dayeshtnin I danshtni O I shivered
  (once); dudni OU he was shivering; yus hududnin I he darted up and out; neshech'
  yadadnin it darted out (fish struck by spear); yus hudidnix dart out fast!; yus hududnił he will
  dart out fast; neshech' yatidudnił he will dart out with him; ugha yinizen ch'u dednin he wants
  to go and he's nervous; aggeya dutnił the otter swims swiftly; nishdutnin it swiftly swam
  downriver; aggeya tent'uh nishdutnin ch'q'u yudut geqh tuni'u ghu hugheltlet the otter swiftly
  swam down under the ice and came out the airhole below; neshech' yadatnin he took off with
  spear (as stabbed fish); dayeshtnin I shivered; dli shdełni I am shivering from the cold; dli
  shdghiłni I was shivering from the cold
   d+D+nin (ono.) sound of roaring water, ice ten dastnini rumbling, creaking ice

   d+n+ł+ni (mot.) plural fly quickly hdnałni UO a flock of birds took off

 say'; nch'u dinil he didn't say anything; tał kiq'u dghinik next he started saying mattress
 again; sutdu łu ch'etni we are going to tell a story, say the story; ĥwi ĥwi ĥu ĥu dehni ch'q'u
 delghesh ha' you say hey hey ho ho and make noise; kadałtin veq' qilan' qetni łu qayeh ghu his

   k'uqu dni he is asking for sth., is bumming; hetdetni U huk'detni O he is groaning,
 moaning; hudedni he is expressing himself; nudini you say it again; buydini say it for
 him; k'usht'a ezdesne he does not say; beghut'u deshni I am begging for it
  with P+eł yighuda shugu shla gguya q'u k'elqatni neł deshnit that's the reason I'm telling you I

 told them; yada gu łdiniyi? what are you saying?; you didn't need to thank me operator ł

 used to call Qishvet'; nda'ich' du ghu "cup" ł dinih? how do you say"cup"?; ey ghu sheł dghini
 ghu nagh hnidatl' now he told me they've come to us; iqech' hghuda shughu qil łdgheshni
 da that is the reason I told him no good; nqayeh nunindyu it dahdi Ts'anhdghulyał ghun
 Jilughun chiydałyuq sheł dghini da when you go back to your village tell them Ts'anhdghulyał
 killed Jilughun; iqech' dnanluq, sheł dghinił you will say to me he did this to us; Qishvet' qeył
 dghinihen Qishvet' the one they used to call; dach' qeył dghinihen Dena'ina ezhge'a ghila'en
 yen k'i thus they used to say he also was a Dena'ina hero; nch'u yeł dinil ha' itdahdi qut'ana
 chidełt'ix ha tut'ił kadałtin ghin ghuda he didn't tell him or else he would kill people for that
 blood; iqech' dach' dnanluq' sheł dihnih' qełni łu "you are telling me that in that way he did that

 saying to me, she told him; q'uyehdi hch'ahniyu ey gu qeł dghini ghu and then they started

 saying?; eygu dach' sheł dini tell me this is right; qenq'a qeł deshnih I call it a house; chashga
 ł deshnih I call it a cup; qeł dghini "ey gu tiqin vejex chidghiłt'ik'" he told them"here wolf
 killed some caribou"; nada'ich' dughu (__) ł diniht? how do you say (__)?
  complex quotes when quote follows nt'i hghuda t'enł dgheshnich', ghu t'ent'ach' ninł'an well
 that's the way I was telling you, you see the way it is; ch'q'u qenq'eya qeshax ch'q'u ghu
 t'qełdghinich' t'ehyiluq ha' so they whittled alders and and he did it as he had told them; q'ut'un

  Grp. 4. other lexemes
  nih, nihdi (adv.) etcetera, and so forth, anything,variously, whatever lit. 'it is cust. said' nda'ich'
  nihq'u some way, anyway; ndaha nih q'u some place, wherever; nih dna all people, and the
  others; yeh nih any place, anywhere; yada qunsha nih k'i iqu nuqudeł they going for ground
  squirrels, or whatever, etc.; ghu łuhshełdałtuk' hnuyu ts'iłten nihdi k'i qeyqełdih when he
  started running around they used to teach him bow and arrow etc.; ntutda nih q'u t'inluggi da k'i
  yagheli yida you did whatever your father did and it's all right; ghun k'i nch'u nih t'desne' that
  one never says anything at all; ughasht'a nich' duqidinik nihq'u anything makes you
  angry; nch'u nih t'qinil da hnuch'dultlet if nothing else happens we will fail; k'iqu qul'an
  ghuhdi ggagga nihdi yeghedishla nihdi tets' eł t'qeyeghił'an they would hunt brown bear and
  black bear, etc. using a spear; Dutna guna nch'u nih nda t'ehyilil the Eskimos did not do
  anything to him; ki yadi nih q'u k'usht'a qghiłnesh they didn't find it anything else; Dzełken
  nihdi Huch'altnu nihdi qunsha iqu shtunuhtededił naqeliteh in the Alaska Range and at Swift
  River, etc. they would hunt squirrels in the fall; k'qat nihdi t'anch'q'u yihghilu ha' the provisions
  and things were all inside it
  OPT-VERB ni (v.enc.) one should VERB, ought to VERB with optative mode tuyush ni let him
  go; tubughh teshush tahbił nughełtl'i ni I am going to the beach to mend the net; henyi hnaga
  dunuch'duldih ni hegh tughanshnik I came to you so that we can relearn your
  language; ch'k'echegi hghełdihni hegh tughanshnik I came to teach you writing; yighuda
  shugu shla gguya q'u k'elqat ni neł deshnit that's the reason I'm telling you I want a little to
  eat; yaghudeghesh ni yitni he intends to bite it; nutgheshna ni nesen I want to move
  back; tik'uyiniłtan beqghełdih ni he took him to the woods in order to teach it him; quldih ni
  benudeshtnesh I can't teach him; ch'at'ghilah ni? what could you do?; dghelqes ni t't'an he is
  ready to cough; yeh tgheshu ni t'esht'an I am ready to go there

➡niqa, naqa circling, around see qa7

nich *niˑchʳ /middle/       /√::AN/
d.f.: nich, nich', nish
   -nich (a.n.) middle, center
       -tanich (a.n.) I -tunich UO small of the back, midriff
       nishqugh (adv.) IU nich'qugh O halfway nich'qugh ezdu he lives at half-way
       point; nich'qugh nitinitunt ezdu he lives half-way down the trail; nich'qugh niyilqet' he ate
       half of a fish; n'uyi nishqugh hdedghidlut' (there is a) half moon; lit. 'the moon cracked in the
       middle'
       ch'ishanich (n.) midsummer
       ch'iteshnich (adv.) midnight ch'iteshnich shegh niyu he came to me at midnight
       ch'ijanich (n.) midday
       ch'iheynich (n.) L January; midwinter
   nich'q'a (n.) O spear thrower, atlatl possibly with this root
       nich k'eltin'a (n.) O lower mid fin on fish

nich' *niˑchʳ' /swell/      /√::V-anat/
  tran nich' nich' nesh
   P+tu#l+nich' to swell, have a blister betu'iłnich' he got a blister; sheq' tu'ighiłnich' I had a
  blister; t'anch'gheli q'u tu'ilnich' he got really swollen

  nidili (n.) OU week nutiha nidili da in two weeks

    qaduk'dazhnex he heard sth.; qaduk'dghinik he listened to sth.; qadanshnesh O I heard

  eya! qadak'daznex hnuyu ndunutsidalen oh no! he heard it when he pulled his head back

 hnaniłyit they heard him and willow brush among they ran; chiqel'ul ch'łuq'u gu sht'a qegh
 k'dazdli qadaqeydaznex nt'i they were playing and there just near them he started to sing, they
 heard him; ghu veł tuk'utił ch'q'u qadaydenex idi eła k'ijeghi nch'u veł duhyidilt'an as he was
 drifting down he was listening to it and it didn't sound like an owl to him; k'elqat sht'a idi łu q'u
 qadak'daznexiq' qent'a he was eating just when it seemed that he heard sth.; ch'vala veł
 dnasdghetl'i qadadayeshnex I heard a tree fall; ey ghu htl'eghhdi nch'u qenaga
 qanatdezgheshdnik after this I'll never hear his voice again; qenaga qandadgheshdnik I listened
 again to the voice, the language; qenage qanandgheshdnik) vach'ala eł qadaqvedgheshnik
 “Giga uqu huł ch'tultał” she heard her aunt say,"we can take her along to pick berries"; HD-
 bpick ch'u k'jegha k'u yi k'u qughusht'a qahdunesh ni and ears are good for hearing; "un"
 shqetnih, ch'u qaduhqebedeshnish "come" they said to me, and I would listen to
 them; k'daltetl'i uhu qaduk'itneshnesh I listen for the shot
  nuhu qaduk'itntgheshnesh I will be expecting to hear from you again; qut'ana qada'idenix idi
 gheli łu he really heard people; qadak'denex; 'eya!' qadak'daznex hnuyu ndunutsidalen he
 listened; 'oh no!' he heard it when he pulled his head back in; qadak'denex idi eł k'delin qilan iq'
 qetni łu she was listening and then it sounded like someone singing
  qa+da#d+D+nik (mot.) be a sound, noise
    qadahdednex it is noisy, there is a noise, a voice; qatadetnex there is a noise of rushing
 water; h'ini q'u qaduk'deshtnesh it sounds scary; k'idiki qaqdetnesh it is too noisy; k'idiki
 gheli ten adahdetnex the ice is making a loud sound
     causative: make noise qadahdełnex you are making noise; nen k'idiki nen shughu
     qadahdiłnex you, you're making too much noise; kazhna gun nch'u qahdełnik lynx doesn't

 village around it anything if tell us you will say that's how we'll know it's you; q'ut'un q'u nach'

  plucked; yiniq' gheli gu sht'a łu yiłdex he shot him in his back right there; qenq'a qiniq' behind

     ts'iłten yiniq' nazk'edi (n.) L sinew backed bow

  paddle so the water won't bump against it; yada kadałtin nlani ch'ayiłchet ch'q'u yenish

nu¹ /iterative/
  nu#D (v.pf.) iterative, again, repeated action
     nu#D (n.d.s.) iterative, again, back, redoing often triggers D effect with intransitive verbs; it is
     often used with verbs that refer to habitual activities such as eating, dressing, making
     fires nuyiłchin she repaired it, she made it over; nuk'elqat she is eating
     again; nuhdiłq'un she made fire again; nu'idyu she came back; negguya nutudlał he will be
     your (step) child
     nu+cha'# (a.d.s. s mom) removing, taking off shvaldu'a nuch'ayeshtggezh I took off my
     coat; this may imply iterative can also be in adv. prefix psoision
  Grp. 2. other iterative lexemes
  qeghnu (adv.) after, when (past), the other side of ggagga k'ghi'an qeghnu dunqidejaq' when
  they saw a bear, they ran back; iłyuts qeghnu nuqtastnu they went back when it started to
  snow; łik'a k'enet'ish qeghnu yethdi yitsax whenever a dog steals, it barks; qenen
  qeghenu other side of the hill; tułkun qeghenu gega tgheł'ił after it rains I will pick berries
     niłnunu (adv.) taking turns hnuyu niłnunu dehi nuquyił they take turns walking to the cache
  qenghunu (a.n.) bend, point on stream hyach'en yeh qenghunu tuvugh ch'a'ilgguk on the other
  side around the point on the beach he came out; qayeh gu yach' qenghunu dach'
  niqatdghi'ihnex you guys go around the point to the village; k'inazdli ghini niqa'idghazdlen
  qenghunu sht'a qinehggesh you will pull the guts where the creek flows around the bend

nu² /perambulative¹/ see two other entries, łuh I q'an OUIl
  nu# (v.pf.) perambulative
  nu#gh (a.d.s. prog) perambulative, around and about, in various directions, in no fixed
  direction the imperfective (progressive) is shared throughout all dialects nunudeł birds are flying
  around; nuch'udeł we are walking about

nu³ /downward/      /ⓐ::vpf#/
  nu# (v.pf.) down ward
                   1

    nu# (a.d.s. ghmom) descending, going down1 nughiyu he came down; nughilen current is
    flowing down; nutighitun trail descands

Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                               385

     Nulchina (n.) Sky clan lit. 'made descending' yet gheli qaqichuq' gheli Yet Nulchina nuqejuq
     tqejuq there they arrived and the Nulchina were made again, it happened; SP76-clan
     nu# (a.d.s. nmom, nneu) crossing nuniban it swam across

nu⁴ /two/     /√/c-num/ in compounds of number two, see tih two
  nutiha, nutih (c.) two
    nutna (n.) two people

nu⁵ (na) /win/
  conc nash nun nał nish
   q+n+ł+nu O-IU (conv.) win at gambling łuq'u yegh qenełnash everything he was winning from
  him; negh nuqnanlnun I won it back for you; nuqentghilnash you will win back; k'usht'a
  na'eł qentułnał he won't win from us; qubeduhtnayełnun I talked my way out of it, I won
  verbally; duqdnałnun he won a point (in an argument); el'egen Gashaq ghe hnałnun da
  qeynizen, ndahduh qayeh łuq'u qitułkeł they knew if the Evil Shaman had won over Gashaq, he
  would have taken over the whole country; PK91:24 k'ch'eltanen ghun el'egen'a nuqnalnun the
  believer won back from the shaman; unhtsah beł chitghil'ul na qentułnash ch'u nenk'u begh
  nuqentghiłnash the first time you play with him he'll win from you and you too will win from
  him; hninłnunda nghu ditagheshtił if you win I'll give it (spear) to you

nu⁶ /reluctance/ only used in I
  enu (exc.) I I don't want to enu tsa'i I don't want to
    enuyi (exc.) I let it be enuyi yach' tiłt'uh leave it be! throw it away!

nu⁷ naˑ /salt, mineral lick/    /√::C!-lith/ root status is moot
  nu (c.) mineral lick, salty soil likely a compound, but
  nuq' (n.) salt lick, mineral lick, game area lit. 'on salty soil'
    nuti (n.) salt, salt water
    Nuti (p.n.) Pacific Ocean, Cook Inlet
    Nuti Vunu (p.n.) Cook Inlet littoral lit. 'saltwater shore'
    nuquq' (a.n.) sandbar (on bank), dry riverbar, bare ground

nu⁸:Dnu *D-noɁ /stream/           /d√::N!-geo/ the distinctive Den. stream term, always with D/t before
the stem; related to nu°¹ considered to be a derived root likely based upon 'nomadize'; all Den. stream
names use the form Dnu
   -tnu (a.n.) river, stream in stream name compounds
   k'etnu, -k'etnu'a (n.) stream, river
      k'etnu gguya (n.) I k'etnu ggwa O k'etnu gga U creek
      k'etnu' dakaq' (n.) river mouth

nu°¹ *naˑʷ /move‴/       /√::NVop/
  k'enu (n.) sign, premonition, omen, luck k'enu el'an he has premonition of sth. (to hunt); k'nu
  niłtu qyighitih they keep it for luck; k'enu qubideł'ishi they would have lucky signs
     k'eghun nu (n.) U red moon lit. 'sign of war'

 Grp. 1. to move, shake
  G+gh+Ø+nu° (op.) move, tremble, shake, vibrate ełnen ghenu the earth is quaking; ełnen
 ghghinu the earth was quaking, there was an earthquake; k'deyes ggwa kut' idalkizi ghinhdi
 ghedetaznu O the little skin he had put in his pocket started to move; ch'bala
 ghedenu O ch'vala dghenu IU tree is shaking; veq'es vedghinux move it aside; nch'u
 tghesdnu he didn't move; nutezgheshtnu HDQ niłhu'idghidnu' ex ni they were all on top of
 one another; q'aghtaznu' O he started to move, began working; ch'vala tudghenuyi spruce is
 shaking; niłquq' qidghitnu they were crowding one another; ch'łuq'u dakaq' qech'
 niłhu'idghidnu łu vejex ghin and they moved on top of one another towards the door, the caribou
  causatives nughtanłnu O nutezgheshtnu HDQ I I started engine; lit. 'I made it work' tali nał
 tuk'ghghiłnu' the waves were pitching us; nał tuk'ghełnu waves pitched us; nughtanłnu O I
 started the engine; nuq'aghtałnu' he started the engine; yeł q'aghtastnu he started
 it; yeghdełnu O yedghełnu IU he is making it move (tree); ch'ghełnuyigu O don't move it
    k'deyes ghedetanu skin started moving; ughasht'a qił q'atastnu they started to work
 early; nch'u k'i tghesdnuk he didn't even move; k'usht'a q'azghanshtnuk' O I have not quit
 working; ch'at'ent'a dughu Betiydalts'iyi łdi qech' elugh k'a bech'daghiłnugh? what is that
 Betiydalts'iyi that we haven't touched yet?; begudałtina nghenu'i O his bloodstream circulation
     ghenuy (n.) caribou U, grizzly bear, brown bear I, wolf L lit. 'one that moves'
     badlidnałnun (n.) U person who is always cold
  ?? qanishnush Sl it is windy
  k'+gh+d+nu (mot.) time elapses, passes nik'ghintnu after awhile; sukdu nutiha tsiq'
 nik'qeghdatnu the story lasted two hours; ch'qilu ni'i nik'ghanshtnu I worked for five
 months; ndahq'u nik'ghatnu? how long did he work, how long does it go?; nutiha tsiq'
 nik'qatnu it lasted two hours; nutiha tsiq' nik'ghanshtnu I work for two house, I spent two
 hours; ełduduk'qidetutnuł it will last a long time; ełduduk'qeghdatnu it lasted a long
 time; ndahqagh q'u nik'qghitutnuł? O how long will it last?; ndahqugh nik'ghanhtnu as you
 continue; nik'qghadnu they worked up to a point
     Qenchix Ch'denghanuxa (p.n.) hill on southeast shore of Telaquana Lake, 1.5 157
     lit. someone's nose touches a place
 Grp. 2. work
  gh+D+nu° (op.) work
    ghetnu he's working; ghudnu' he was working; k'egh gheshdnu I am working on
 sth; ghgheshtnu O ghighestnu I ghagheshtnu U I worked; ghetutnu O tghutnu UI he'll
 work; gheshtnu I am working; ghigheshtnu IlI ghagheshtnu U ghgheshtnu O I
 worked; tghigheshtnu IlI tagheshtnu U ghetgheshtnu O I will work; nch'u
 tezghighestnu I nch'u eztghigheshtnu Il nch'uk'a eytagheshtnu U k'usht'a ezghetgheshtnu
 O I won't work; shu ghetghitnu O you will work for me; shu ghitnu you work for me; shu
 ghintnu you worked for me; shu qghutnu they worked for me; niłbu qghetutnu O they'll work
 for one another; vu gheshdnu I am working for him; ench'u zgheshdnu I am not

  working; ghidnu dida? are you working?; gheshtnuh I work now and then; k'eyes gheghetnu

    q'aghadnu' he finished, quit working; q'adudghatnu' IU q'adughdatnu O he finished
  talking; en'ugha q'azghayeshtnuk I I have not quit working yet; q'aghanshtnu I finished
  working; Dena'ina qenaga yadi niłtu beł q'ach'eghtastnuye di? O why did we ever start to work
  on the D. language?
    yenuvun ghetnu he works for money; janq'u k'kin'i ghin gheghetnu still he works on that
  head; ełkunda k'usht'a ezghetgheshtne if it rains I won't work; k'eyes ghe ghetnu ta'iltsiggi
  nułtu he's working on skins for smoke tanning; ughasht'ayi qeyeł q'aghtastnu O they started
  working early; jan ghu vadi yeghednu yeghednu that day he worked on the skin

  qeghednu q'ich'idya gun eła Ch'iduchuq'a he kept working on the doorway with pika; dakaq'
  qegh nutghasdnu he started working on the door again; qinaghelt'ach' ch'tghudnu we have lots
  of work; vava ch'tghudnu let's work on dry fish; sukdu egh ch'ghednu ni let's work on the
  story; uch'en gheshdnu I am working outdoors; shqenq'a tghigheshdnu' I am going to work in
  my house; shqenq'a qegh tghigheshdnu' I am going to work on my house; ench'u
  tezghigheshdnu I am not going to work; ush egh yu ghednu he is working on snowshoes for him
    yeghełnu he makes him work; nughtanłnu O I made it work, I started it
  (engine); huk'ghelnu he pretends to work; huk'ghulnu he was pretending to work

     veł k'ghetnuhi, veł k'ghednuyi (n.) tools
     veł k'ghetnuyi vazdli (n.) tool box
     ghednu'en (n.) worker, employee
     henu, -henu'a (n.) work, job henu ghetgheshuł I am going to work; henu nuteshjuł I am
     going back to work; henu tuyuł he will go to work; nda'et henu tazu? when did he go to
     work?; ch'u ghedisla ghini yech' hnu nutasdyu and he went back to work on that black bear
     henu qeshqa (n.) foreman
     hna henugh (n.) language work suk' ina qit'ahqitni hna hnugh łuq'u huqidutench' they
     should be aware of the old people and retain all of their language work; PK91:6

  gatherer
     k'enuy'a ggwa (n.) O k'enuy'a gga U small beaver
     nughuniy, nughun'i' (n.) O nughenuy U nugheniy I freshly killed game or fish nughun'i
     łuq'a fresh fish; nugheniy nlan ghu qeyelqatch' k'i yagheli the fresh (bear) meat there is good
     to eat also; AB20
  elnu (n.) slave, drudge, lazy person, good for nothing, laziness based upon passive-causative of
  theme not in Den. to capture cf. At. elnaa slave, drudge elnu nlan you are lazy, a lazy one; elnu
  shegh lughaldatl' laziness got hold of me; elnu ak'nitighildeh you will sleep til you are
  lazy; elnu ak'nitanldaq I got lazy from oversleeping; obviously related to nu² but vowel is non-
  ablauting

nu°², (na-) /to nomadize/
  mom nax nu nał        na nish

  cont na 〃
  per nał nak nash
   cust nish nik nish
   prog nał
   Ø+nu° (mot.) travel, move nomadically, move with one's household ts'ił ghenałna ndahduh
  qiz'in qilanh hdaqilchet one group went wherever clams were; łuq'u niłch'ahdalggezh nutih
  qunałh all they divided up two parties; hk'unutasdnu they left for home; nunch'tułnał we will
  let you go back; ench'u qeveł diset ndaha iqil ch'tuna qiyenizex it was not far for them to
  wherever they wanted to move; ndaha yi'uh nugheł'eshi ch'u shagela dnaghelt'a yeh
  niqenax they would move to wherever there was more abundant game and trout; huch'ghinu we
  made winter camp (not village); tinu'idnu she had her period, she moved out; tinuneshdnu I am
  menstruating; q'uyehdi htaznu then they left; ey ghu nutih niłdatun idi eła nuhtasdnu there
  two days later then they started back; tiquna hvidesh ni I want them to move out; ey ghu
  nuhtednaxi eł nuhtutnał with their packed up things, they're going to move; ugha qevch' ch'tuna
  ni now let's go to them; tinhnax you pl. move out; tich'una ni let's move
  out; ch'entastnu O we came unsuccessfully; k'usht'a tesnak' O he is not going; k'usht'a
  ezteshnak' O I'm not moving; nduch'una ni let's move in; yeł dunastnu he unexpectedly
  moved in with him; Pedro Bay ch'tuna ni let's go to Pedro Bay; nishch'tuna ni let's go
  downstream (to fish camp); nishch'tuna ni let's move downstream; t'anch'gheli q'u
  ch'tunał we're all moving (entire household); tich'tunał we're moving out of the old house; eygu
  nduch'tunał we're going to move in here; Pedro Bay ch'tunał we will go to Pedro Bay (by sled,
  boat, snowmobile); Pedro Bay tgheshnał I will go to Pedro Bay; nishch'tunał we are going to
  move down; huch'tunał we are going out to spring camp; endaha tinax dit? where are you
  traveling to?; hek'untgheshnał I am going to leave you guys (leaving town); ench'u
  tezgheshnal I am not going to go on a long trip; nuch'tasdnu tsadi I wish we could go
  back; hch'aqninu they moved out; quznu he moved up; qutunał he will move
  up; nuch'tetnash da when we are about to go back; tihqiniłnu he made them move out; ndat
  q'u beqenq'ah nin'idnu? when did he move back to his house?; nintgheshtnał I'll move
  back; nuhtałnu they sent him back; k'usht'a nuzteshnak' I will never move
  around; nishhghinu they moved down to fish camp; nishnuqetnax every summer they move
  down; nishtinax move downstream!; nishtuna ni let's move downstream; q'angheshtnak I was
  moving around
     ghenałna (n.) band of people, the nomadic band gu łuq'u ts'iłch' ghenałna ch'ilan we are all
     one family, nomadic band here; PK91:274 ghenałna kiłqa k'isna quch'enaqa ts'iłq'u shani
     niłtu quqayeh ghu nuqidedatl' the whole family men women their children returned together to
     their village for summer
     ghenałi (n.) male moose, bear with family
     benen tich'enashi (n.) E March lit. 'month we go out'

nun¹, yun *ŋaˑnɁ /across/          /√::DIR/
d.f.: nun, nut, r√: yin, yun
      This root is unstable in Dena'ina, varying in shape
   -nun (dir.) across ts'inun IU ts'itnun O straight across; ts'itnun nuniłch'ey it's blowing
   straight across; ts'itnun dedaztun it's (log) laying across and on top; ts'itnun nuniyush go
   straight across; ts'inun yach'a straight across that way; yunun yeł hk'utsighalkit N yinch'en
   yeł hk'utsighalkit L he swam out across with him; yinch'en qiz'un it is cross river; no yunun for
   L yunch'en N across the river; yunch'et eydu U he lives across there; sheghnuch'et U across
   from me

nun³ /hunting ability/   /r√::N!-sign/ mainly in U; cf At. ca#n+l+ni° have hunting luck, ability
  kununi, -kunun'a (n.) U hunting ability, hunting luck bekunun'a qghila he had hunting
  ability; nkunun'a neł t'ilk'at da neł ch'duqitnitułt'agh if your hunting ability gets tough you will
  have misfortune; bekunun'a bełentnesh beq'uhditu'uł take care of the game or you will have no
  luck hunting; ey'uh nugheł'eshi ggagga heyeł dghinihi yi chich'eł'ishi bekununa dghila łu The
  game animal they called"ggagga", brown bear, that we kill (harvest) was his specialty it seems

   (IDF+)d+Ø+nut' (suc.) IDF (light) shines, twinkles, flashes; lightning strikes detenet it is

  sparkling; daznet it sparkled; qen hdenet izhi itułch'ex stars are twinkling, a cold wind will

  us; bedlitnghinut' he has frostbite; bedlitnitunut' he will have frostbite

nut' /materials/    /√::N!-tech/ not used this way in other Alaska languages, a possible derived root,
likely with nu
             Q

   -nut'a, nut' (n.) building materials, craft supplies
      k'enut'a (n.) gambling score sticks

q¹ *q /third person pl/      /ⓐ::vpf+/
  q-, h- (v.pf.) they, third person pl. subject only used for humans, and dogs qechegh they
  are; hdelqes they are coughing
  qeve (pf.) I qu OU their, them, third person plural object/possessive prefix qevlik'a I qulik'a
  UO their dog
  qeve (v.pf.) them, third person pl. direct object qevenetl-'anh I qu OU I see them
  qeve, qev, qu- (v.pf.) them, third person pl. postpositional object qevq'eza alongside of
  them; qeveł deshni I quł deshni UO I say to them
  qev-, qu- (v.pf.) them, third person pl. direct object used with 1st or 2nd person
  subject qevch'enił'anh we are looking at them
  deh- (pf.) IO hde- U their own dehqayeh IO hdeqayeh U their own villages
  qudi (pro.) they themselves cf. udi himself, herself qudi qev'idi it's their own stuff; qudi ki
  t'anch'q'u yuyeh łhdnaldaq hqidni they (themselves) thought that they all were sleeping
  inside; łiq'a iqu htel'ih qudi qevnułtu I they fish for themselves

q², h *x̯ʊ /area/      /ⓐ::vpf+,ENC‴/ one of the most versatile affixes
d.f.: q, h, qech', qegh
   q, h (pf.) area, place, in a general area, areal object/possessive pronoun hnen U qenen
   IO hillside
   q, h- (v.pf.) area, place, situation, state, area gender/qualifier ch'ehnił'an we are looking at the
   area, place; hneł'an qenq'a qiz'un there is a house; they are making places, houses; hdeghut he
   is dusting a place
   h (sf.) area can be used as directional suffix, also in numerals and adverbs yutsih area distantly
   downland; nutih two places, ways
       kih (adv.) later on, in the future gini łuq'a kih niłtu this fish is for later on
   unhsah, unhzah (adv.) O sa, unhyaqah U first
   q+ (n.d.s.) areal gender/direct object qiłchin he built it, made a structure
   q+ (t.f.s.) thematized gender or areal object/subject
        q+ł+qa (op.) shovel an area (snow, loose sand), dig in O with shovel, hooves hghiłqa he
       shoveled; ch'hghiłqan we shovelled; huhdghiłqan he broke up the soil; shtuqiniłqan he
       shoveled dirt away; q+Ø+daq FIX CHK pl. P are angry, mean, ill-
       tempered; qeveyihghidaq they got angry, mad
   qi# (a.d.s. smom, sneu) inside qunsha de'un qidalts'i the ground squirrels are sitiing inside their
   dens
   qe, qi (c.) area
  Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh

qa¹ *qeˑ /foot/       /√::NV-anat/ two interesting derived verb roots

   Grp. 1. anatomical
   -qa (n.,i.,c.) IU -qatl'na O foot, hind legs of animals the O term is distinctive and unusual; -tl'na
   is some type of reanalysis sukhdi yi qighisen, ch'u q'anqutdił quqa eł there were none of these
   in the old days, and the people travelled on foot; P. Constantine 1980:16 niłdeqa k'ghał'un he
   has his feet crossed in front; niłnuqa'ighel'u he was sitting crosslegged; yeqałqesh OU he
   kicked it (once)
       ts'ełq'i shqa (n.) IU ts'ełq'i shqatl'na O one foot-length

       -qanghuna (n.) toes

qat qaˑd /buy, ask/       /√::Vop/
  dur qat 〃 qit
  rep qet 〃
  rep² qet qat qet qet
  sem qet 〃
   mom qat qat qeł qat
   suc qet 〃
   O+i+Ø+qat (op.) buy, pay for O, obtain O mainly with money, by trade; hire O q'u igheshqat I
  just bought it; k'ighiqat he bought sth.; k'ighiqat he bought sth. with money; niłk'qiqet they
  buy from each other; shu inqat buy it for me; hvu k'inqat buy sth. for them!; shu
  k'ighiqadilay don't buy anything for me; kliva uqat ideshni I want him to buy bread; veł
  k'idigheshqadi shegh k'isen I have nothing to buy sth. with; itigheshqat I will buy
  it; ch'ituqat we will buy it; k'i'eshqet I buy sth. now and then; hvu k'i'eshqet I buy for
  them; t'anch' gheli q'u yighiqat he bought up everything; udi nułtu itułqeł he'll buy it for
  himself HDQ; k'idaghiłqat he likes to buy things; pot. k'idaghelqat it is affordable; pot.
     k'iqeden (n.) a buyer
  lahgagh itqedi (n.) store-bought items lahgagh itqedi k'chizhiga store-bought spare ribs
  huqadi (n.) O belongings, one's outfit lit. 'one bought for oneself'
   P#O+i+Ø+qat (op.) buy O from P shk'inqat buy sth. from me; nk'igheshqat I bought sth. from
  you; nch'u shk'iqeł he didn't buy anything from me; k'kidza sh'ituqat he will buy fur from
  me; łiq'a n'igheshqat ni let me buy a fish from you
   O+i+d+ł+qat (op-rev.) ask O k'idghiłqat IU k'idghiłqet O he asked
  someone; yidghiłqat IU yidghiłqet O he asked him; yegh k'idełqet he asks someone about
  it; shitdaziłqat you started to ask me; q'u idghełqat I asked him; nini q'eya idghełqat I asked
  for the porcupine hipbone; for luck in hunting idi eła ey gu t'etni łu ts'iłt'an ghun 'ugha yach'en
  gu dalts'ina guna hvidełqet' and then there one man said, 'go ahead to the people staying on the
  other side and you guys ask them; shu idiłqet ask him for me; itdghełqet I will ask
  him; ch'itdułqet we will ask him; "yagheli, ch'itdułqeden yin," heyłni łu "good, we'll ask him,"
  they told him; M. Chickalusion, 1980 k'idaghiłqat, k'idaghiłqet, k'idełqet he asks lots of
  questions, he is inquisitive; pot. t'k'idaghiłqat it is questionable; pot.
   O+i+d+l+qet (op-rev.) ask O permission, compel, press O by asking, beg from O ch'aqini'ush
  ch'u qughesht'a nunidghelqet QQ slips O you are obtaining power so I should ask you
  thoroughly

qats' *qʷeˑtth' /enclosed, elongated object falls/   /√::V-classif-fall/
   mom qas qats' qes qis
   per qes 〃
   prog qes
   dist qas qats' qas qas
   G+ł+qats' (mot.) enclosed object moves independently, falls mishuk niłqats' the sack
  arrived; hk'uniłqats' it fell off; k'qan ch'diłqats' the beaver house fell down over the
  bank; shegh shtuniłqats' I lost it, forgot it (bag); k'izhagi tughiłqats' the knife fell in the
  water; yashik tudenghiłqats' the box fell in the water
  qaniłqats'i (n.) U common eider (Somateria mollissima) lit. 'one that bites' SP's translation
   O+G+ł+qats' (mot.) drop enclosed O nuyghiłqats' he dropped it (bag
   tu#d+ł+qats' (mot.) water moves QQ onto ice venq' hutudghiłqats' overflow came up on lake
  (ice)
   G+Ø+qats' (mot.) loaded boat moves; long object glides in air badi ka gheli hkayeghiqats' a
  really big umiak came upriver; bulhut yach' gheqes boat is going that way; nughiłent heł qetnih
  na'eł ch'aghiqes he came out down where they falls was; ch'u nundudqes and it (arrow) was
  falling back down; beq'aditin'a ghin yudeq ghu duqes his spear was flying up above; suk'ves
  yinudutqasa L it glides inside our stomach when eating it (wild celery); song by Emma
  Bobby qubeł tak'tayqats' hqitni they're starting to sink they thought; yudeq ch'bala adinqats' it
  (arrow) hit on the tree; shghulugh nuk'dutqats' it (an arrow) fell back to me
     Vandazqats' Vena (p.n.) lake west of Telaquana outlet, 1.5, 135 lit. 'someone tipped over in
     canoe lake'
   INC#Ø+qats' (mot.) INC falls nuk't'unghiqats', k't'una nughiqats' leaves
  fell; nuk't'untuqes leaves will fall; nuk't'unqas leaves are falling; betl'a k'utqats' UO she is
  a barren woman; lit. 'sth. dropped from her crotch'
     betl'a nk'utqats'i (n.) UO barren female animal
   h#gh+Ø+qats' (mot.) clock hand moves, attached elongated object moves, stars, constellations
  move hlun ch'ahk'anqats' it is 10 minutes past the hour; ch'qilu ch'ahghinqats' UO it is five
  minutes past the hour; ch'qilu eł k'idghiqats', ch'qilu ahdghazqats' I it is five minutes before the
  hour; nihghaniqats' I nihghinqats' UO it stopped (clock); ^he died of old age; udiyeh q'u
  nihghaniqats' qeył dghinih he died of old age by himself they said of him; nutiha beq'di
  ch'hk'ughqes it approaching 12 o'clock
     naq'ech' niqahdghuqesi (n.) I naqaghuqesi O big dipper (constellation) lit. 'it revolves
     around above us' yi k'i sen desa naqahk'dghuqesi łdi ghuda iqu that star is a clock that
     points/turns for that pupose; HD-9/24/19

qen¹ *qʊnɁ /fire/       /√::N-fire, Vstat-temp/
  qen (n.) fire daz'i is the common noun for fire in D.
  qen (i.,c.) fire yuyqux qen denix the northern lights are making noise of fire; HD jeghch'en qen
  hdalq'en: fire is burning at its (sun's) ears,; sundogs on both sides of the sun yaqendaniyel he lit
  the fire; qeyeqendiyeł they lit a fire (match); vanaqench'tiduyeł we will light a
  fire; nuqendełdał, qenldał plural lightning is striking, flashing
  INC

    nugheyuł he is going about visiting

  hot; hnalqen it (area) is warm, hot; na'a qenelquh we will get all the summer heat; hwa
  qenlquh whole area (State of AK) gets warm, they tend to have warm weather; nalqeni
  nunlya get dressed warmly; hnighelqun it was warm; dayin nilqin steel is red hot; neli qałnigi
  dnizełqin I warmed up the steambath rocks; k'idiki tinighelqił I will get too hot; k'idiki
  tinighilqił you will get too hot; qenq'a qinulqin the house got warm inside; beggats'a ghin

  our relations the will take (marry) us; SP76-clan

qen⁷ *qəŋ /request shaman/        /√::Vop/
   ser dur qen 〃
   cust quh ~ qih 〃
   qen (op.) pray for, make shamanistic wish, make offering to O (shaman or
  spirit) duk'idudqen I duk'dudqen UO he prayed; duk'ida'indqen I duk'dindqen UO say
  your prayers!; shegh duk'idutqen he prayed for me; Naq'eltani du'ida'intqen pray to God
    yit k'u yadi k't'un ey beyich' qilani duk'qidedquh ch'qyan they always prayed to plants and to
  all living things; PK91:16 qeyuhul'ih dghu duqyedetquh "k'usht'a nhu'izdeyeshdle'" O they
  would hunt it when they used to pray"I am not equal to you"; a prayer said when hunting brown
  bear ahtlut ghini nuti n'at hut'qijuq da, ahtlut du'itdihtqen; qenutnutuh if they happen upon
  killer whales in salt water you should pray; they will help you; gunen tsukdu nuheyghiłnigi
  ghudu shughu beq'e ch'etniyi ch'u ahtlut duqutdutqih nuquyuł dghu they told this story so that
  they would pray to the wolf and the killer whale when out hunting

qet'¹ *qət' /slip; eat ↇ/   /√::Vdim;V op‴/ Dena'ina theme to eat is unique
   dur   qat qet' qat qat / qit
   neu   qet' 〃
   mom qat qet' qeł qat / qit
   prog qeł
   rev qet qet' qet qet
   trans qat qat' qeł qat
  Grp. 1. slip, slide
  qet' (n.) cavity, socket, small cylindrical hole deqet' dghinł'u you keep it there in the socket place
  they had for paddles; e.g. paddle rest, oarlock va qet' nuytihyel ha you guys will put them (bow,
  spear) in it (under straps, in the socket)
   G+l+qet' (mot.) slide, slip on surface, abrupt movement takes place talqet' it, he
  slipped; tughelqet' it slid into the water; ch'q'u qałnigi ghini vechutl'a gheydnalqet' and the
  rock slid through his stomach; ch'adnalqet' the door was opened; qdilqet' ch'u qa'ilts'et he
  slipped and bumped against it; bank'dalqet' it (bucket) tipped over; nudalqet' it (sled, boat)
  capsized
   caus. to slide O ch'ak'dnaniłqet' he opened sth. (door); nushdghiłqet you might tip me
  over; qank'naniłqat close the door; qank'dnanłqet' I shut the door; qak'denaniłqet' I he shut
  sth. (the door); hq' yetałqet' he slid it on the surface; yetsi k'naniłqet' ha' he twisted her head
  off; yeduzhizha ghini yeł tałqet' he twisted his beak off; ye'u nuk'ghiłqet' he erased, rubbed,
  scraped sth. off of it; be'u nuk'iłqat erase it; dach' ghu ztan vechutl'aq' duydnalen ch'q'u
  qałnigi ghini vechutl'a gheydnalqet' it fell on him the way he was lying on his stomach and the
  rock went through his stomach
     niqaghelqełi (n.) revolver lit. 'that which slides in a circle'
     veq' tełqeła (n.) I beł k'tełqeł'a O gun hammer lit. 'on it sth. slides'
     niłq'ahghulqełi (n.) the atlas, the 1st vertebra lit. 'one that rotates within'
     tudzulqet'i (n.) U concretions, rock shaped like objects dzul uncertain, but seems to be qet'
     slide
   INC#G+l+qet' (mot.) INC slides, falls hqa'idghilqet' I qadilqet' O he slipped, lost his
  footing; qahuch'denghilqet' the tree roots overturned; ve'u nuyestelqet' skin fell off of
  it; nuch'adasgedi'ilqet' he exhaled smoke; hutsisnułqeł it makes my hair fall
  out; hutsitnutqeł her hair will fall out; yagheli igi hutsinghilqet ni it is good if your hair falls
  out; qutsighelqet' he went bald
   shed hair hulqat it is shedding hair, fur; hughelqet' it shed its hair; shlik'a hulqat my dog is
  shedding hair
     hutsighelqet'en (n.) bald-headed person
   INC#O+G+ł+qet' (mot.) INC causes O to slide, move abruptly ch'ałch'eydnanłqet' the wind
  opened door
   kah#n+l+qet' (mot.) water spills, dumps out from a container

    qeych' yideh ghu qeyeł qakahqeydnaniłqet' on top of him there on him they turned it upside

   O+G+l+qet' ↇ (op-rev.) eat O ↇ note that the durative stem variation is irregular k'elqat he is

  sht'a I and they all eat, they all started eating here
     veq' ch'k'elqidi (n.) birchbark tablecloth, tablecloth lit. 'we eat on it'

     ch'elqadi (n.) food

  resupplied me with food
     qada (exc.) honey, sweetie, dear said to one's spouse
   P#O+G+ł+qet' (op-rev.) feed O to P shyełqat he is feeding it to me; shk'ghiłqet' he fed me

qey, qux *qɑy /throw, strike elongated object; animate moves/          /√::class-throw (SgPl)/ fast-speed
paired with ghwł (normal speed)
   mom qux qey qex qux
   mom² qux quk' qex qux
   sem qex 〃 qish
   cons qey 〃
   dur qey 〃
   O+G+ł+qey (suc.) cut, chop, blaze, hew O with axe, knife; spear, harpoon O
   spearing yiłqex he speared it once; yetałqey UO yetałquk' I he threw a spear at
  it; qey'idelqey U he speared it; yak'dalqey he speared sth; tets'a ghini ełneq' qeydałqex he
  stuck that spear in the ground
   hew O yeghiłqey he hewed it; tghiłqey you will hew it; qaniłqey he blazed it (tree) with an
  axe; yahghiłqey he hewed the inside of an object (dish); yedghałqey he hewed it into a
  shape; yadak'niłqey he hewed the insides of it (log) (for dish, boat, etc.)
   propping, bracing with pole vik'nazełqey I propped sth.; yink'dałqey he put up pl. braces,
  props; qak'niłqey he put a brace up against a place; nuk'ilqey it is spread apart with a
  stick; veniłtudeq k'ilqeyi its got a spreader from side to side; k'inun'i k'ełqey he is spreading
  sth. with a spreader stick; yek'iłqex he put sth. (willow) on it (fish for drying); ch'enlahi
  qeydnałqesh they bounced the gambling sticks; detets'a qeydełqey he is bouncing his cane
  against it; qetnitułqesh O he will bounce it
     qeyeł k'tnełqeyi (n.) IU pool
     niłq'ach' beq'un hnalqeyi (n.) double-ended spear with sharpened bone heads
     nuk'ilqeyi (n.) fish spreader stick
     vadandalqeyi, vadanq'dalqeyi (n.) interlocked split cottonwood log roof
     vada'ilqeyi, badadalqeyi (n.) dugout canoe
     qannalqeyi (n.) blazes on tree
     tuqesi (n.) IO tiqesi U fish spear, beaver spear, harpoon
     telqexi (n.) I throwing spear

   P+qa#ł+qey (suc.) kick P most common in U yeqałqesh OU he kicked it
  (once); veqanazełqesh I stubbed my toe on it
   P#k'+l+qey U (mot.) P hobbles, limps shq'ank'elqey I was hobbling around; shnuk'ulqesh I
  am hobbling around
   P+INC#ł+qey (suc.) strike P with INC body part yets'iłqex he poked him with his index
  finger; yeqanghunghiłqey he kicked it rep with his toe; yetl'ułqex he sat on it hard with his butt
   INC#ł+qey (suc.) strike, bump, stub INC body part, move INC body part
  quickly nutl'ughiłqey he fell down on his butt; hunutsaghelqey he stuck his head back
  out; hghedzzełqex I I dislocated my shoulder; knitsighiłqey he stuck his head in the snow
     qidułqeya (n.) O łidułqeya, tsidułqeya U shrew lit. 'bumps its snout, head'
   D+qey (mot.) jerk, leap, move with force, bump into tastqey he jerked, twitched; shtałqey he
  startled me; ni'idqey he came to a sudden stop; nik'u'idqey it jumped up (caribou when
  startled); niłnutsastqex rocks shifted close together; niłch'nunasdeqex we bumped into each
  other
   n+D+qey (mot.) stumble, stagger hch'anatqey he stumbled out; nunghintqex you are walking
  off-balance; uq hyanq'u qubeq'e nuyestqeyi clouds only their tracks will cover; dnayeshtqey I
  stumbled

qil /bad ↇ/     /d√::p.adj./ This distinctive Dena'ina root and meaning, based on one or more negative
derivatives of 'be' la¹, Den. qilal 'it is not'; cf. Ahtna kole 'nothing', kulaele 'it is not ...' see also qul
O nothing; a Den. innovationↇ, no cognates are found, related to qil nothing
  qil (p.adj.) bad, no good, terrible sheł qil qilan I feel bad, terrible; lit. 'badness is with me' sheł
  qil gheli it's really bad for me; qil qizdlan the weather turned bad; ten qil rotten ice; qich'a
  qil it's worse; iqech' dach' hghuda shughu qil neł dgheshni da this is the reason I told you that it
  is bad; qil! nach' gheli q'u qudeł no good! they're coming right toward us; qil zdlan IO qil
  eydlan U he died; qil ghanichet he took his/her life, he killed him/her

qu² *qaˑ /surface/   /√ⓐ::AN¹‴/
    we place here qu, hu upward, to surface from below
  d.f.: qu, quq', qugh, qagh, quh; d√ quq', qul
  Grp. 1. surface, area of

 -quq' (a.n.) on surface of, area of resource see DTD:136 ełnen quq' on the earth; nudalyi quq'
 nunshyu I crossed on the foot bridge surface; dghili quq' nzhah qilan there is snow on the
 mountain; miłni quq' nutuyutił he is drifting on water
   tiquq', tiduquq' (a.n.) on the surface of water
   nuquq' (a.n.) I stream bed, gravel bar (on side of river)
   qałnigi quq' (a.n.) rocky area, rocky bar
   łach quq' (n.) silty area, fine soil flats
   k'echan quq' (n.) grass flats, grassy area

   tuquq' (a.n.) tundra, open landscape with scattered trees

 qu, quh (c.) surface
   -quq'a (a.n.) O -qaq'a UI wound lit. 'surface cavity'
   Qanquhtnu (p.n.) McKinley Creek, 1.2, 45 lit. 'lodge surface creek'
   k'qushiya (n.) I k'qushya OU marmot, small marmot (Marmota caligata) likely with qu² ‡
   loaned to ⇨ Aleut, qusx̂i-x̂ ‘marmot’ Alutiiq qusriq (K, Ch quirriq (AMB)
   k'qushya (n.) UO young marmot
 Grp. 2. disjunct verb prefix qu, hu # v.pf.
 qu# (v.pf.) OU hu I upward, ascending to surface
 qu#, hu# (a.d.s. gh mom, gh neu) up, ascending, upward qughilagh it swam to the surface, e.g.
 whale, salmon, beaver; Tubughnenq' qech' tabagh quyushi qughidał beluga come up toward the
 beach at Tyonek; nuq' hughi'u a sand bar is sticking up; hughi'ats he is holding his foot up
  beluga terms sharing prefix qu²
   quyushi (n.) U quyeshi NL qunchi Sl qunshi OIl qubishi U beluga whale (Delphinaterus
   leucas) lit. one that comes upward', 'one that swims up the prefix qu upward is common to all
   terms; the term in U is most transparent, based on yu° sg. goes; note the OIl form is reanalyzed
   and blurs the stem; a second term in U means 'one that swims up' see Shem Pete's classic
   account of beluga hunting, SPA:78-79
 A Wassillie 1978:37-38 riddle: niłyida ina huniłghulkitna shugu two buddies surfacing; quyeshi
 hughilaghi łiq'a veq' nanq' nałtani a beluga swimming up with a dead salmon upon its face; A
 Evan's answer: "at Tulggezh Big River a seal came out with a salmon on its face, as it dove out from
 saltwater into clear water."
 ts'i+qu# (a.d.s. zneu) straight surface ts'iqutaz'u it is straight grained
    ts'iqutaz'iy (n.) straight grain

 qu (v.enc.) due to, because ggugguyni deltetl'a ch'dełtetl'igu tułkun qu qdghinih don't step on
 puffballs because it will rain, they used to say; ch'k'idełyisigu qa'itułch'ex qu don't whistle or it
 will blow; ełkuni yiq' nuch'uyułigu nuk'itułtleh qu don't walk in the rain because you'll get wet
    qushi (adv.) evidently qushi t'dini' evidently that's why you're saying that; qushi
    ch'q'aynigen hvegh qisen; qevgguya nch'u hghilal evidently there was no child with them;
    their child never gave birth; yagheli qushi nach' qadahudulnik eyghu dghini ghu yeh
    hninł'an evidently he must be OK as he let us know; go look where he spoke
 Grp. 4. temporal extensions
 qugh, hqugh (adv.) IO qagh U up to, as far as, as much as tuq'i jani hqugh for three days; gu
 hqugh this much; gu hqugh yan nitnatqa the tracks go only to here; nch'u va k'ighuldixch'
 hqugh nishełdiłtex yudeq he ran up to where the arrows couldn't reach him; chulyin duzhizha
 gini tsel tghitdzih hqugh a Raven's beak big enough for you to wipe your ass with; k'etnu hqugh
 niniyu he went as far as the river; ey hqugh łu ghu t'itułne' apparently it will go this
 fast; shqenq'a hqugh daghiset from here to my house (distance); nenla hqugh daghiset from
 here to your steambath
    ndahqugh (int.) IO ndahqagh U how, how much, how far ndahqugh q'u tghiduł? how long
    will you stay?; ndahqugh q'u dnikuh? how much does it cost?; bench' ndahqugh daghiseł
    dit? how far is it to the lake?; tets' ndahqugh q'u daztun? how much does the cane
    cost?; ndahqagh ghetutnu? U how long will he work?; ndahqugh q'u gheshtnu
    t'a'ininzin? how long do you want to work?; ndahqagh q'u nik'qghitutnuł? how long will it
    last?; ndahqagh daghiłnazi di? how long is it?; ndahqagh dagheldzeli di? how thick is
    it?; ndahqagh shdu'itnaghiłt'eyi di? how hard is it?; seł ndahqagh ezdluyi di? how much
    are the boots?
    duzhqugh (adv.) I how much, how duzhqugh shuq'u t'itułne' shuq'u' I wonder how fast it
    will go
    qughesht'a (adv.) O well, nicely, excellently, thoroughly qughesht'a nik'dalkit he put up
    food well; ch'aqini'ush ch'u qughesht'a nunidghelqet you are obtaining power so I should ask
    you thoroughly; q'u' ndeshni daq'u, qughesht'a ch'u shegh nitghilgheł when I say 'enough' to
    you, you will give me a chance
 qu, qun (s.cnj.) UO-I when, while, until, during yideghu yetih qun betukda tuyuł they hiold it
 there until the father returns; inten qun na nutgheshjuł keep it until I come back to you; shlik'a
 inten ghetgheshtnu qu keep my dog while I work; shbalt'u ighiten q'angheshtbash qu he kept
 my coat while I swam; dnigi gu niłdeq niyu qeghnadi delghesh qun a moose walked by here
 while we talked; tułkun qun gega tgheł'ił I'll pick berries until it rains; ch'u qeliq' itqun
 Ch'aghełnikt niqenish k'qezdulghani and during spring to Pt. Possession they would move to put
 up fish; qyuqu teyish qun hbeł shtunendnelnish when they went after it they got disoriented

qu³ /they, their/ based upon qe+ve
  qev-, qebe, qu (pf.) their qevlik'a I qulik'a U qebelik'a O their dog

qul¹, quł /Ω/      /u√:N/ several nouns best pulled together here, analyses moot, qutl' chop, shquł
spoon
     el quł (n.) I eł qula U eł quł O whole spruce branch
  qala (ins.n.) NL k'qula, k'qila N split cottonwood logs for roofing, trough gouged out of
  cottonwood log noted as a roof logs, purlins also cf. qa⁵-qala UO dirt pile in front of bear den
     K'qula Qelchixitnu (p.n.) second creek into Chulitna River, 2.4, 13 lit. 'creek made like a trough
     of cottonwood'
  yuyqul (n.) U beluga spearing tree (on Inlet tide flats) rare verb theme that is is absent in
  Dena'ina; cf. Ahtna kal gaping, open (garment) SP "The Tyonek people would embed and fasten a spruce
  tree upside down into the mudflats at low tide. At high tide a hunter would sit up in the hollowed out root
  structure and would spear beluga as they swam by." q'uyethdi bantadusht hnuq'u yuyqul deduhna
  ghuna nik'uhdel'ush then when the tide comes in tree they they take out in boat those who sit on
  the spearing tree

qul² /nothing/       /d√::padj-NUM/ a derived root, common in O, from negative of la exist; see also la
and zen
  -qul (p.adj.) O -qisen, -qisil I qilal U nothing, zero, absent, gone
   O examples, other examples see qisen < zen, qilal < la¹ bequl he is gone; k'qul there is nothing, it is
  absent; bak'qul it is empty inside; naqul there is no one there; taqul' water is gone; yuh kił
  qul' the boy is not inside; begguya beyich' qul q'u nudultan her baby was born dead
  -qil, -qul (c.) zero, none, absent compound stem in numbers
     łtaqul'i (n.) O łtaqil'i UI eight lit. 'two are gone'
     łtaqulna (n.) O łtaqilna IU eight people
     łtaqulh (n.) O łtaqilh I łtaqildi U eight times
     łtaqilh hluzhun (n.) I łtaqil'i qelun U łtaqul hluzhun O eighty

qun¹, qin² tV-qaˑn-i /Ω-wolf/      /u√::N!-bio/ no clear meaning for widely distributed lexeme
  tiqundi (n.) U tiqin I tiqun O wolf (Canis lupus) uncommon in O
     nuti' at htiqina (n.) killer whale (Orcinus orca) lit. 'saltwater wolf' Kela Nuch'iłtan story by
     Antone Evan in Tenenbaum 2006:176-210 "nuti at qech' q'udi gu datghilcheł ch'q'u nuti' at
     htiqina tghilał;" "when you move to salt water you will become the saltwater wolf"; iqech'
     t'edyuq hdi nuti'at htinqin hdi zdlan so then he turned into the saltwater wolf; Tenenbaum
     2006:210
     tiqin qaggena (n.) I wolf claw amulet (to be a good hunter)
     tiqundi gga (n.) U tiqindi gguya I coyote (Canis latrans) lit. 'little wolf'

qun² /to chisel/   /d√::Vsuc/ likely with qu³ surface
   dur qun 〃 / quh
   O+n+ł+qun (conv.) to chisel, plane grooves, holes in O (snowshoe frame) yenghiłqun he was
  chiseling it; dnaghilggen idiłti dnghiłqun when they (wood runners) dry then you will chisel
  it; S. Californsky 1980:40
     nulqun (n.) socket for sled stanchion that crosspiece fits into
     ush nulqun (n.) chiseled holes on snowshoes for rawhide to go through
     beł qelquni (n.) IO beł k'tnelquni IU chisel for snowshoes, sled

qun³ /sew/
  mom qush qun quł qish
  rep qux qun qux qux
  dist qun 〃
   rev quh qun quh quh
   cust qik'
   q'an#O+d+ł+qun (conv-(rep).) sew O q'ank'dałqun I q'unk'dałqun UO she was sewing
  sth; yiq' q'uniydałqun she sewed pl. things on inside of it; q'ank'daghiłqun she has sewing
  ability; pot. q'annk'tdghełqun I will sew several things
     veł q'ank'dalquxi (n.) I uł q'unk'dalqushi O thread, sewing machine
     k'enudalquxi (n.) patch (e.g. on jeans)
   POSSIBLE THEME cf. w ff niłkinyednałqun she sewed them end to end; nunk'ehdełqun they
  are sewing pieces (e.g. for boat)
   O+G+Ø+qun, O+G+Ø+qun (mot.) sew, mend O yenazqun I she sewed it, made it by
  sewing; nuk'ghazqun she mended sth.; tuyghazqun she sewed trim on it, decorated
  it; niłtuyiniqun she sewed them (pieces) together; yik'ghiqun he sewed sth. on the
  inside; q'ach'idequn we completed sewing it; niłkinyednazqun she sewed them end to
  end; nił'anyizqun she sewed them together; yink'ezqun he sew pl. O on inside; jan
  dek'enghiqun she sewed all day; shunkda ghunhdi baydalgi qeyeghayqun U my mother sewed
  up the skin boat; undat beq'estsiq' quqeyedghuqish they sew up the last
  seam; yak'denghazqun I yak'ighdnazqun O she sewed sth. on the inside of it (lining); ndahda

qunsha ∞ /ground squirrel/       ∞substrate /∞√::N!-bio/ unanalyzable, only in Dena'ina and Upper
Kuskokwim konsa; likely substrate word

  uncertain; qunsha iqu ch'el'an we are hunting mountain squirrel

    poles

    ts'ełq'i qunsha (n.) UO one hundred
