qutl'¹ *qaˑ-tl' /chop (carefully)/   /d√::Vsuc/ from qu³ surface, important contrast with tsatl' chop
   mom quł qutl' qeł quł / qił
   cons qetl' 〃
   ono qetl' 〃
   sem qeł 〃
   dur quł qutl quł quł
   dist quł qutl' quł quł
   O+G+ł+qutl' (suc.) chop O with axe (lightly) contrasts with tsatl' note: knock, chop O with blunt
  edge, insect *stings O most common in UO
     yidełquł he makes small chips while chopping; yeghiłqutl' he was chopping it; qghiłqetl' he
  chopped the area; qełqetl' he is, I am chopping the place repeatedly; yeghiłqetl' he chopped it a
  series of times; shiłqeł O it (mosquito) stung me; yech'ak'dinłqutl' he chopped it
  out; vich'adaniłqutl I chopped out the inside of it; yeq' daniłqutl' he chopped it
  off; bek'enłqutl' I chopped it in two; benidulqutl' it (axe) is embedded in it; hnidalqutl' it is
  chopped, embedded into it; anitułqeł he'll begin to chop; tihuk'diłqutl' he chopped and missed
  it; chik'denłghash gheneshyu ch'q'u qatni'ełqutl' I came to a burl and I chopped it off; ch'q'u
  q'uyehdi vantudushi eł tałqutl' ha' and then wide grass he chopped it down; k'eł tałqutl' he
  chopped sth. down; yada eł q'u k'eł tułqeł? what will he chop anything down with?; beł
  ntałqutl O k'eł ndzazełqutl' I he chopped several trees down; veq'ak'ni'ełqutl' I felled a tree
  on him; vank'nazełqutl' I chopped many on to it; q'ak'ni'elqutl' I felled a tree on
  myself; shq'ak'niłqutl' you felled a tree on me; quydghiłqutl' he chopped it all
  up; huhdghiłqutl' he cleared the area by chopping; tsak'dulqutl' he chopped sth. tree down into
  a gulley
   tsatl' vs. qutl' k'atnitsay ch'ak'danełqutl' I knocked off (with blunt edge) the punk; k'atnitsay
  ch'ak'danestsatl' I chopped off (with blade edge) the punk
      qudulqutl'i (n.) chopped wood
      hnik'ełqełi (n.) T walrus lit. 'one that chops'
      tsik'tnełqetl'a (n.) U woodpecker lit. 'one that chops with its head'
      basqetl'a (n.) U white fungus, punk on birch
      k'da iłquła (n.) I gnat lit. 'horn chopper'
  ełquł (n.) O elquł I ełqula U whole spruce branch seems to be from this root
      elquł hetl (n.) I ełquł hetl O forked branch dragging sled, travios

q', q'e¹ *q' /on/           /√ⓐ::PP¹‴/ can have range of meanings we treat 'tracks' as a reanalyzed root
d.f.: q', q'e, q'ech', q'et, q'egh
   Grp. 1. postposition examples
   P+q' (p.p.) IO -q'e U on, upon, after, behind P ch'valaq' OI ch'balaq'e U on the tree, like a
   tree; qenq'aq' ezdu it is stting on house; deghiliq' nitgheshnał I will move to the
   mountains; dghiliq' ghiłyuts it snowed in/on the mountains; Dena'inaq' duhdelden du? does he
   know the Dena'ina language?; deghiliq' hdeqidatl' they went down the mountain; betsiluq'
   duyiłdatl' he put them on his tongue; benq' nelgguk I ran on the lake; dghiliq' qughiyu he
   appeared on the mountain; yashikq' dezdu he is sitting up on a box; niłq' hudultan they were
   lying on top of each other (dead fish); niłq' duzełdatl' I put (blankets) on top each
   other; vanq'ashliq' nuk'ighaztuk' he drew a diagram of like a canoe; tiqinq' dadyuq łu he
   howled like a wolf it seems; Dena'inaq' qenax he talks (like a) Dena'ina; k'iztin ghinlikelyi
   łiq'aq' ch'ełchix we make that bone like a fish; saldatinaq' qanunk'ghilu they dressed him up
   like a soldier; k'enuy'a qan q'et'ent'ay yech' łu qighelgguk she went in what seemed to be a
   beaver lodge; shi shq' deli he sings like me; q'udi qeyan ezdu shishq' he (his father) lives alone,
   like I do; ggaggashlaq' ch'it'a nuch'nulnił tsadi we wish we could fly like birds; el'ek'inaq'
   qinunk'ghilu they dressed him like the shamans; qenchiq' nugheyuł he walks like a
   porcupine; yeq' dudeli he is imitating him
   -q'ech' (p.p.) over, above, upon naq'ech' dnalnen it flew over us; hał qech'q'ech' qilan pack is
   above them, over them; veq'ech' nichił duch'dełgget they we put birch bark over it; sheł
   ch'indaqna shq'ech' ch'anqidatl' yiqech' hghuda gu zeshdu my brothers back me up that is the
   reason why here I am now; lit. 'came out above me' hq'ech' qenink'danshel I set crosses over the
   place (the graves)
   Grp. 2. tracks, footprints interesting for dialect variants
   -q' (a.n.) IO -q'e U tracks, footprints
   note dialects with k'-
    k'eq' (n.) NL k'e'eq' Il k'eq'e U k'beq' O k'weq' Sl foot print, animal track k'eq'
    qilan IO uq'e k'ilan U there are tracks; qyebeq' hq'aghidatl' they came to his tracks
    qiz'in beq' (n.) clam “eye,” clam track
    K'beq' (p.n.) K'beq' site on upper Kenai River a name coined by PK
    yuq' niyunen beq' (n.) O Milky Way lit. 'tracks of one who walked in the sky'
    veq' ch'etniyi (n.) wolf lit. 'its tracks someone says' nickname for wolf, often used instead of
    tiqin (Canis lupus)
 Grp. 3. compounds numerals
 q'e (c.) on, like compounds
 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                420

     -q'enay (n.) U siblings
     k'eq'di, -k'eq'da (n.) I shirt, blouse
     -q'edi (n.) covering, tarp outer covering, e.g. tarp
  veq'di, veq'na (n.enc.) plus, in addition used in numerals
     ts'ełq'i veq'di (n.) IO ts'ełq'uq'edi U eleven things
     ts'ełq'i veq'na (n.) IO ts'iłq'uq'etna U eleven people
     ts'iłghetna ch'qilu veq'di (n.) twenty-five lit. 'twenty plus five'
  q'egh (c.) derived form
     -q'eghdeq (a.n.) between P, above
  Grp. 4. noun formation, verb formation, various lexemes
  q', q'e gl on, upon, at (n.f.sf.)
     -lukiq'e (a.n.) U -lukiyiq' IO fingertips
     -t'uyeq'e (a.n.) U inner chest
     -t'uq,-duq' (a.n.) on top of flat surface, e.g. on flat mtn.
     quq' (a.n.) on surface of, area of resource see DTD:136 ełnen quq' on the earth
  Grp. 5. q' verb-forming suffix
  q' (v.f.sf.) verb formation suffix Den. has a few examples of this, may be several patterns
   nu#D+vaq' (op.) vomit unique to Den. from va-q', grey nughetvaq' he vomited
   O+ł+qaq' (mot.) to track, trail O foot+q' k'eniłqaq' he arrived tracking sth.
      d+l+zaq' (op-ono.) be hoarse, raspy-voiced with za¹ throat delzaq' IO delyaq' U he is
     hoarse, there is a hoarse, grunting sound

q'a¹ q'eˑ /cavity/    /√::AN¹‴/
d.f.: q'a, q'at, q'adi, q'a'i, q'ayi, q'ach'; d√: q'at' fornicate
   Grp. 1. areal nouns

       k'q'a (n.) U five dollars

       Taq'ahts'a (p.n.) hill north of 'Gusty's Lake', 1.3, 5 lit. 'timbered lowlands+ ?'
   q'a (i.,c.) cavity, tracks łuhq'ani'uh, łuhq'aniyu tracks go all over
       tasaq'a (n.) NO tatsiq'a Il fish hole in ice
       -gełq'a (a.n.) cricoid cartilage, cavity of front of neck

       tikunq'a (a.n.) IO area of tundra puddles lit. 'rainwater cavity'

 Grp. 2. aq'a
 -'aq'a (p.p.) O -aq'a IU place, in one's absence, in one's place, after, cavity of
 -aq'a (p.p.) cavity k'uhda'i ghita' aq'a moose's bed; yach' qitihdeh deghu qech' ch'u
 sh'aq'ach' da yeh da'ilnesh ch'u iti'ihjesh before you fall down go there in my absence and you
 will save yourselves; k'chan hyan q'u h'aq'a nudnulyah after you only grass will grow; nił'aq'a
 chiqet'ish ts'u łuq'u Susitnu k'qilal one after another they are dying off and all Susitna is gone
    ts'ijanich haq'a (n.) U afternoon
 P+aq'a## ni# (a.d.s. nmom) finishing, removing, killing off P nhaq'a nik'tghełt'eh I'll club all of

    niłq'analyuyi (n.) I temporary snowshoes without webbing
    niłq'ahghulyut (a.n.) log notches
 Grp. 3. q'ach'
 P+q'ach' (a.n.) toward P (direction), from P (a direction), surrounding P chuł q'ach' vechutl'a
 qilan, ezhi'i q'ach' viniq' its stomach is towards the Chinook wind its back is toward the north
 wind; regarding birch growth ezhi'i q'ach' from the north; chuł q'ach' from the south (I), from
 the east (UO); lit. 'in direction of Chinook wind' naq'ach' around us; shq'ach' around
 me; q'uyehdi k'eyes dghel'ich'i qeyq'ach' hnazhyuch' then they tied the soft skin around him
    niłq'a, niłq'ach' (adv.) both sides, back and forth, double ended Yaghenench' łuq'u niłq'ach'
    ełnenh hqugh łuq'u q'anqudił They would walk back and forth all over the Kenai
    Peninsula; P. Constantine 1980:16

 Grp. 3. disjunct prefix, strings
 q'a# (v.pf.) cavity
    P+q'a (a.d.s. gh mom) into cavity of P, trading for P hq'aghiyu he went into a hole,
    cavity; q'aghesh'un I traded for it; q'udi ki ghu tghesht'iłch' ghu hq'ayenidghelen I figured
    out now the way I will do it; lit. 'my thoughts fell into a cavity'
    dken q'adiłtini (n.) curved snowshoe knife
    ts'en q'adiłtini (n.) bone-handled crooked knife
    P+q'a# (a.n.) n mom) tracking P yeq'aniyu he came upon his track; beq'a tazu O he started
    to track it
    P+q'a (a.d.s. s mom) completing P yeq'ahtazuk O he finished the set of
    dances; q'azhełchin I completed making it; q'ak'yeshlach I cooked it all
    P+aq'a ni# (a.d.s. n mom) removing, clearing out, killing, annihilating dnaghuna t'anch'q'u
    k'duheł ghini eła qaq'a nik'niłt'eq' all the people all horn club with he clubbed each and every
    one of them; yaq'a nik'nitsatl' he chopped, cleared them all with an axe
    P+q'a#D (a.d.s. n mom) quitting, stopping P note the D-classifier q'ak'neshdlach I finished,
    stopped cooking; q'aghatnu he quit qorking
    VERB q'a t'-t'an (coll.) trying to VERB tak'ghestsexni q'a t'an he is trying to
    row; k'dgheshlini q'a t'an he is trying to sing; k'dgheshlini q'a t'ghesht'an I tried to
    sing; huhgheshlani q'a t'esht'an I am trying to dig a hole; tgheshyuni q'a t'et'an he is trying
    to go; ghełts'ah q'a t'eshghił'an he tried to kick me; ighełjeh q'a t'eshghił'an he tried to
    punch me; nqełna gheshla q'a t'esht'an shit I'm trying to mix with you people (to be
    friends); hyiniq' ghu łu qeyjek ch'aghelggux q'a iqech' t'qeył'an they were scratching on his
    back so he would come out that's what they tried to do

q'ey *q'ʊy /pelvis/      /√::N!-anat/
  -q'eya (n.) pelvis, crotch
     qanchi q'eya (n.) OU nini q'eya I porcupine pelvis for divination in hunting AE "You put
    caribou hair in the socket and let it dream overnight. You scold it if it doesn't sit up. then you put it on the
    floor to punish it." nini q'eya idghełqat I asked for the porcupine hipbone
    chu q'eya (n.) I beaver hipbone toss

q'i /call of the pika/   /√/voc/ cf. U k'eq'a, Ahtna k'e'i
  q'is (voc.) I call of the collared pika
     q'ich'idya, q'inch'idya (n.) I collared pika and names for it are only in Ahtna pika pika is only
     in the Alaska Range, CHK UK, mammal like a bat that lives in the mountains named for its call,
     q'i PT: "little mammal the size of a muskrat, which are not killed and lives amongst rocks, boulders at the
     head of Lake Clark. It has big ears, light brown in color, and has a little bark "q'i, q'i, q'i", from which he
     got his name. There is a story about it."

q'u² /now/
  q'u (c.) now, emphatic Common in adverbials which specify a point in time or emphasis

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                              435

 q'u (adv.) now, stop, yet, enough, soon commony used with the interrogatives q'u tsa'i let's go
 now; q'u tsa'i ninki you go now; q'uqet' q'u tsa let it go for a while, it's OK for now; (e.g. if you
 owed me money, it's OK if you don't return it now) yada q'u? what now?; yada q'u
 t'enł'an? what are you doing?; ndan'i q'u shagela nlani? which one is the trout?; ndaha q'u
 zidu? where are you staying?; q'u teyush he is just going; q'u tulał it will be soon; q'u du? are
 you ready?; q'u lach' duq'u? is that so?; q'u shihqugh! good enough!; q'u du galeq nagh
 hniludu? did they bring mail to us yet?; yada ki q'u? what else?; ki yada q'u? what next?; gu
 q'u right here; q'u qgheshnak I already spoke; q'u teyux he is going just now; dets'iłtena
 ghini ey q'u yedghichet right then he dropped his bow; yadi q'u chinnah? how are you
 sick?; yin q'u tazyu he just left; (emphasizing he) heyi q'u nik'danlkit I put up food for
 winter; (emphasizing winter) nhin q'u chik'a t'ił'ih ch'ehdi k'eldunteh you guys get wood
 °; emphasizing you pl. q'u lach'q'u that's true; q'usht'a wait!
    q'udi (n.) now q'udi qeyan ezdu he lives alone now
    q'udi gu, q'udikdi (adv.) right now
    q'udi qeyagh'a (n.) IO q'udi hyagh'a IU q'udidi U new, a new object q'udiqeya'a coffee
    zełchin I made 'fresh' coffee
    q'udiden (n.) young person q'udiden eshlan I am young
    q'udi jani (adv.) today
    q'utsa'i (exc.) that's enough q'utsa'i ch'ek'etsałi we are done chopping now
    q'udishla (adv.) just as, right then lit. 'little now' q'ut'un q'udishla hdi itałqun idi eła tiqin
    t'etni łu in the morning just as soon as it got light when wolf howled
 q'uch'a (exc.) q'ushida I that's all, goodbye
    q'uha (exc.) stop, enough (said by song leader)
 hq'u (cnj.) but, despite, instead k'usht'a qdit'al hq'u quht'ana ht'ighiyu but not long after a man
 came inside; k'di'un hq'u ezhi qilan the sun is shining but it is cold; nughiłghatl' hq'u
 htl'uynushju it got dark but he caught up with them; shqenaga k'usht'a begh q'inizdilnik' hq'u
 un'i ada ndgheshni ni O you don't understand my language, but come let me talk love to
 you; from a Kenai love song k'usht'a k'dital hq'u quht'ana ht'ingheghu but not long after, a
 man came in
    ch'q'u (cnj.) and O mainly uses ch'u k'dałtetl' ch'q'u chiydałyuq he shot and he killed it
    q'uyehdi, q'uyethdi, q'uyehdi (cnj.) and next, and then
  Emma Alexie, 1980 Lime Village Reader q'uyehdi yesvits', three consec. sentences then I rolled it
 up; q'uyehdi nudazełchel ch'u k'igheszegh then I moistened it and scraped it; q'uyehdi
 k'izhełch'eh then I folded it into a basket
    q'ua (exc.) goodbye this is one of the only words in Dena'ina with a diphthong
 ts'iq'u (adv.) I janq'u UO still ts'iq'u veyich' qilan I janq'u veyich' qilan OU he is still alive
    ki q'u, q'u kiq'u (adv.) again kiq'u yinałjeh he hit her again; q'u kiq'u sheł dghini he told
    me again; ki q'u it da next time
    guq'u (adv.) right here szaq' guq'u vadełnik I can taste it in my mouth; a word you're trying
    to remember
 q'u ki tsa (adv.) once again, one more time q'u ki tsa shegh zitan lay by me again
    q'ushla, q'ushlaq'u (adv.) almost, nearly q'ushlahq'u qinenałqesh he almost got hit; q'ushla
    hk'udaninłt'eq' you almost knocked it down; q'ushlahq'u chijuq he nearly got
    killed; q'ushlahq'u dnasdu he nearly got hurt; q'ushla hq'u shitnilt'a it is nearly enough

Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                  436

  q'u gheli (adv.) really, intensely q'u gheli chegh he really is crying
  q'u idak'i (adv.) that's ok, all right answer to 'let's go' dak'i q'u kech' q'u yagheliyi that's ok, let
  it go, its good; nghalten dak'i q'u kech'q'u yagheliyi that's ok if it freezes
      hentq'u (adv.) suddenly
      huggetlanhq'u (adv.) slowly
      niłghenaq'u (adv.) at regular intervals
      łuq'u (adv.) UO t'anch'q'u I everyone, everything vetsen vesdesix idahdi łuq'u
      nqiduk'dulzex' dive in then and help yourselves to everything; ADD
  q'uch'a (exc.) that's all, the last sh'undaten q'uch'a kił yen k'u shch'ayghalkit even my very last
  boy he from me took
      daq'u (adv.) let it be, leave it alone, no more, death daq'u qizdlan ^he died suddenly;
      lit. 'suddenly he became no more' t'anch'q'u daq'u qezdlan they all perished; daq'u nułtu
      shugu ch'edyuq that was for life or death it is we did it
      q'ut'un (n.) morning

q'u³, (q'un) q'en *q'aˑ, *q'ɑnɁ /burn/       /√::V-fire (supp-pair)/
   mom q'ush q'un q'uł q'ish q'un
   neuO q'en 〃 q'uh
   neuIU q'en q'un q'en q'en
   cont q'en q'un q'en q'en
   dist q'en q'un q'en q'en
   (i+)d+G+Ø+q'un (mot.) ignite, catch on fire, fire burns, moves the i- prefix is
  significant idiq'un it ignited, caught on fire; nuyditq'un it started burning again; note D
  passive bahqeghtniq'un it (food) scorched inside it; hch'ak'dinq'un it burned out from a
  place; yusdi yaniłnik yets'ena ghini unk'dayq'un niqanaynilu ch'u U he put him in fire
  (cremated him) and he wrapped up his burned bones
  and; veghuk'dghazq'un I beghunk'eghdazq'un O it burned partially; nidaniq'un it burned up
  to a point; nhdghiq'un I nhdghiq'en, qendazq'un O nhdayq'un U there was a forest
  fire; nduq'uł it is burning along, the fire is moving; nhdeq'en the forest fire is moving, is
  burning; ses bek'dinq'un part of the ridge got burned; tsenhdiq'un it burned down toward
  water; bahk'tneq'un sth. burned to the pan; bek'dinq'un it burned in
  two; diqanhdgheq'en' trees are catching fire, it is burning upward; ndahdi jani dazli bet'uh
  idiłq'uh how many days do you keep fire under it; jegh enhendalq'un ears are burning, ^there are
  sun dogs; diqanhdgheq'en the fire burn up (climbing a tree); vihu'idghiq'un it burned up
     dghelay ghedinq'uni (n.) U active volcano lit. 'burning mountain'
   (nu#)O+(i+)d+G+ł+q'un (mot.) to build a fire idiziłq'un it started to burn; idiłq'un he built a

  rabotnik (n.) O worker

rama     Russian ráma frame
  rama (n.) O frame

rasdobga       Russian rastópka kindling
  rasdobga, rasboga, brasdolga (n.) O kindling

ray    Russian by FS ray paradise
  ray (n.) O paradise +FS

Rayzistvu       Russian Rozhdestvó Christmas
  Rayzistvu (n.) O Rayzesbu U Ruzestwa I Christmas

razboynik +2019          Russian
  razboynik (n.) O robber, thief

ribedishgi      Russian rebyatíshki kids, children
  ribedishgi (n.) O kids, children

richnay      English-Russian blend KLA: "probably from English rich plus Russian adjective ending -
nyy"
  richnay (n.) O rich man

ridut    Russian redút fort, redoubt
  ridut (n.) O redoubt, fort; Russian-Alutiiq name for Kenai

ris   Russian ris or English rice
  ris (n.) O rice

rok    Russian by FS rog horn, antler
  rok (n.) O horn, antler +FS

rubashonga         Russian rubashónka shirt
  rubashonga (n.) O shirt +FS

  tununuttech' he put his head in hole again; sheł hch'a'idetich' it charged out at me; el vegh
  dadetech' spruce needle poked through it; deggenshtech' he jumped up angry; q'u dnulzex idiq'
  dyuq idi eła deggenshtech' then it looked like he would fall over and then he jumped up
  angry; kninghettech' his head poked through the snow; kninunettesh he (drunk) tries to get up
  and keeps nosing over into the snow
  tech'a (adj.) stuffed
     q'intech'a (n.) dry fish with roe PT "Fall fish that you leave the eggs inside, cut the back, filet it
    without taking the guts out, good to eat after fish dries, eggs sour."

    sharp. It is lashed solid to the shaft. The point goes into the animal up to the hooks; this keeps the point
    from going in too far so you can keep jabbing. The Only the point penetrates the animal."

  nił#d+n+Ø+tun I q+d+Ø+tun O (u:nmom.) spend the night, camp, stay overnight an
 interesting innovation, cf. the common Dene then used in OU, n+Ø+yul ≡ niłdanitun he camped
 overnight, spent the night; ts'ił niłhdanitun they stayed a night; qedintun O they stay one
 night; niłdanshtun I did stay one night; nt'i niłch'tidutił layda well I wonder if we will stay
 overnight; gudih niłtidigheshtił I'll stay overnight around here; nł niłtidigheshtił I'll stay
 overnight with you; ench'u niłch'tidustil we (2) won't camp; ench'u niłtidzigheshtil I won't

 nutih niłdatun idi eła nuhtasdnu there two days later then they started back; tik'niłtun q'u
 niłch'detixi dghayi do not camp in a game trail; guhdi nutih niłdetił to here would be two days
 travel; idi eła ki q'u niłhdatix and then again they were going to stay overnight; tuvugh qeghena
 nch'u niłhdestik they never camp near the beach; qeył niłdetix they stay overnight with
 him; ts'ił niłdetix one overnight journey; niłhdetix ch'łuq'u nini qa they stay overnight and
 subsisting on porcupine; tut niłdanitun he stayed overnight three days; ey gu q'udi gu hluzhunh
 ninłhdatun da k'ishi niqu łuĥtudeł here now in ten more days maybe they'll come looking for you
  hetlG+Ø+tun (mot.) handle INC elongated object nuhetlghetił OU he is driving a sled

   O+G+Ø+tutl' (suc.) kick, step on, shove O with sole of foot yeghitetl' he kicked it
  rep; yizteł he kicked it once; gu yinitutl' he kicked it to here; qitetl' he's stamping his
  foot; qiyeshteł I stomped once; ch'iqizteł we stomped once; yetetl' he is stomps it (with sole)
     hk'uynitutl' he kicked it off; ht'iyghitutl' he kicked it into place; tiynitutl' he kicked it
  outside; tuyghitutl' he kicked it into the water; q'ak'itutl' he quit kicking; niłnuqidetetl' they're
  kicking it again; nughedeteł he goes around kicking; q'anghedeteł he was going around
  kicking; k'nanshtetl' I kicked sth. entirely; łik'a Peter uyizteł the dog kicked Peter; Peter
  uyizteł łik'a yitsa the dog that kicked Peter is barking; Peter yizteł łik'a yitsa the dog that Peter
  kicked is barking; negh kaq' itigheshteł ye gu t'int'a da I'll kick your ribs in while you are (lying)
  there; ush nuhutnultutl' his showshoe (frame) came apart apart while walking
     nitagheshtił I'll kick you; nch'ituteł we'll kick you; itagheshteł I'll kick it once; yutetl' ni let
  him kick it; nu'eshtetl' I'm kicking it again; nanu'ehtetl' you kick us; niłnuqidetetl' they kick
  each other
   Ø+tutl' (mot.) step, stomp, tramp with feet nugheteł he is kicking his foot around; yeq'
  tihnaztutl' he stomped it in (into the snow); tughitutl' he stepped in water; yegguna eł
  qanitutl' he stepped on his hand; Yet yelugh yeł qiteł ch'u That bear stepped out over it (the dirt
  pile) and; yet'e'itutl' he caught my foot on it; tiqentuteł IO he'll tramp out; nzhah ni'eshteł I
  step through the snow; hnihghitutl' hq'adulen he fell in where someone stepped; ki łu destla
  nunestutl' it is said again, he put his boots on; qeghnitutl' he stepped through (snow, ice); yeł
  qanitutl' he stepped on it; ban'eshtuł I am stepping through it (snow); vanshtutl' I put my feet
  against it; bet'e'eshtutl' I I caught my foot on it; vinłi q'a qech' hnihghitutl'
  hq'adulen someone stepped toward the water hole and fell in; nugheteł he's going about
  kicking; nuhudetetl' he's kicking himself again; nuhuqedetetl' they are kicking themselves
  again; ush ideghitetl' he flexed snowshoe by stepping on it rep; bededngheshtutl' I misstepped,
  stumbled; ht'ich'anitutl' he stepped out from a small space; qughitutl' he stuck his foor up and
  out; łuq'u qach'etełigu nink'dintun qu don't step on fish when you're
  menstruating; kighitutl' he stepped on point/tip of nail; nzhah hnighitutl' he stepped in
  snow; łuq'u yeq' tihnaztutl' he stomped it into the snow; nuqyulbeq'i yeqanitutl' he stepped in
  their vomit; tughitutl' he stepped in water; ey łu q'u z'un ki łu ey ghu yeh tihnaztutl' ghu there
  it was there where underneath he stomped on it as; tutugheshtutl' I stuck my foot in water; ush eł
  qenaztutl' he's trampling down snow with snowshoes

  d+l+tutl' (mot.) take a step, stride, place one's foot yinudultutl' he put his boots, socks
 on; yinudultutl' he stepped into it (e.g., his boots); qch'adaltutl' he took a step
 out; nik'udiltutl' he raised his foot; vanq'ashli adghinltutl' veghkenk'tidighiłtesh you might

 table); tudultutl' he put his feet in water; qyighitih qughusht'a qahdultiłni they keep it for good
 footing (sheep's foot callus); nik'udiltutl' he lifted up his foot; yekidultutl' he set his foot on tip
 of it (e.g., nail); shqendun seł yich'adaneltutl' sestla yinudgheltutl' I took off my slippers and
 put on my winter boots; sestla yich'adaneltutl' ch'u q'uyehdi qendun seł yidgheltutl' I took off
 my winter boots and put on my slippers; seł yinuch'daltutl'i he can barely put on his shoes; ve'eq'
 ghu ch'u nutih delteł qeyegh nuqulnik ghu his tracks were [like two steps, they would say about

 nearby, very nearby; ts'ił sht'a k'ghelqet I ate just once; chiluq sht'a t'ełuq I did just exactly

 sht'a' idiq' ch'it'a gu there's no way we'll make it here; more emphatic than nch'u no
   qint'ah qu (adv.) trying to this is the 2nd person customary of t'qit'a qint'ah q'u ugha ghilan
   da try your best to be quick; shzhala ghun vegh shełtun k'eghesnix ideshni hq'usht'a qint'ah
   q'u itdghełqet I want to borrow from my uncle but I will try to ask him

  k'ech' n+Ø+t'a (desc.) be mean, of a bad disposition k'ech' nat'a he's mean; k'ech' na'esht'a I
 am mean; k'ech' ch'nat'a we are mean; k'ech' hnat'a they are mean; buk'ech' hnat'eye ghi'an
 gheli łu He had seen a really mean one; Buk'ech' hnat'ey, wolverine q'e nełchish q'e nudeghun
 łu t'ghit'a ch'u they are mean and are made like a wolverine
  P+uch' k'+n+Ø+t'a (desc.) hate P buch' k'nat'a he hates him; shuch' k'enat'a he hates
 me; buch' k'ena'esht'a I hate it
  "> na'eł htidułt'a L it will warm up for us
  ch'u#G+Ø+t'e (desc-neg.) be bad
  P-pp# ch'u#Ø+t'a (trans-neg.) be tired of P, be tired of waiting for P yeł ch'ust'at he got tired of
 it; prob a neg construction ch'uch'est'at we got tired of it; vghuch'uyisht'at I got tired waiting
 for it; veghuch'uyisht'at I got tired waiting for him
  ch'u#G+gh+ł+t'e° (desc-neg.) (animate) be bad, ugly, poor, marginal ch'ughiłt'e' he is
 ugly; ch'uqughiłt'e it is a poor area; ch'u'iłt'at he got ugly, got drunk; ch'udaghiłt'e he has a
 bum voice; ch'uniłt'at he scowled; ch'unhqiniłt'e' they scowled; beł ch'uydiłt'at it is odd to
 him; ch'ughiłt'e he is homely, ugly; veqenaga ch'udaghiłt'e' he has a bum (ugly)
 voice; ch'uqughiłt'e' the area is poor, ugly; gheltsana ghunahdi ch'unhqiniłt'e' gheli they
 looked really mean (were scowling); ch'ununqinilt'ak gheli hnuq'u yusdi idayq'in ghin
 ideggit he really scowled again and poked the fire; ch'u'izełt'at I got ugly; ch'u'izełt'at I got
 ugly again; ch'u'itighełt'agh I'll get ugly; na'a ch'duqidiłt'e it got bad weather on us, we have
 bad weather; ch'unu'ilt'at he got ugly again, id. he got drunk again; ch'ushiłt'at he made me
 ugly; ch'ushitułt'aqh he will make me ugly; huch'uhqenelt'e they are sloppy, careless with
 themselves; bech'uydiłt'at it's odd
     Ch'dutu'itnaghiłt'e (p.n.) rapids 1 mile above Lime Village, 1.4, 75 lit. 'argumentative water'
  ch'+du#d+ł+t'e (desc-neg.) ch'dudiłt'e' he is crippled, handicapped
  ch'u+yni#n+l+t'e (desc-neg.) be hexed begh ch'uynighelt'e he hexed him; ch'uyni'ult'e he
 was hexed; begh ch'uynich'ult'a we wish bad luck for him; qeyeqech' ch'uynitult'ih U they
 will make bad medicine toward him; qeyech' nutult'agh they will make bad medicine toward him
  ch'+du#i+d+n+gh+ł+t'e (desc-neg.) be difficult, frustrating, troublesome ch'duqidniłt'at things
 went wrong; shich'duqitnaghiłt'e it's hard for me; ch'duhuqidnitułt'ał it'll cause him trouble,
 bad luck; ch'dutu'idnaghiłt'e' the water is dangerous; nkunun'a neł t'ilk'at da neł
 ch'duqitnitułt'agh if your game is hard for you to kill you will have misfortune; beł
 ch'duhuqidnitułt'ał k'u it will give him trouble, bad luck too; ch'duqitnaghiłt'e things are hard,
 difficult; shu ch'duqitnaghiłt'e things are hard, difficult for me; ch'udu'itnaghiłt'e it's no good,
 it's bad; qenaga henu ch'du'itnałt'e language work is hard; k'eldin'i k'eqenaga'
 ch'dun'itnaghiłt'e some words are difficult; ch'dutu'itnaghiłt'e' the water is rough, difficult,
 dangerous; veł ch'duqiniłt'at things became difficlut for him, he got bad luck; veł
 ch'duhuqidnitułt'agh it will cause him trouble; shi shghuda bekenu ch'duqidnitułt'agh on

  account of me his offspring will have trouble; shu ch'duqidaghiłt'e' it's frustrating for me, I get

  Grp. 3a

     łinest'a (n.) male dog

t', t'e *t' /pocket/     /ⓐ-√::n.f.sf./ very interesting word formation
  -t' (n.f.sf.) behind flat surface, pocket forms numerous area nouns
      yut' (a.n.) surface of sky, clouds
      -kut' (a.n.) pocket, within garment
      k'enut' (n.) fish meat possibly a compound
  k't'e (n.) U pocket
      -tut' (a.n.) IO -tut'e U beneath flat surfaces tał tut' ezdlu IO tał tut'e eydlu U they're
      under the mat
  t'e, t'a (n.sf.) under surface
      -vit'a (n.) sleeve
      nuht'e (n.) O blind (archaic)
  t'e (c.) under, within
      vet'ełeq' (a.n.) I crack in boards, logs
  P+t'#, P+t'e (a.d.s. ghmom, ghneu) under a surface, behind a layer va ht'eydiyił put it behind it
  (rib in canoe); vaht'eydghi'u it's sticking out behind it (boat rib); ht'ediyił put it (stick) behind
  strapping (e.g., on a wall); t'dughel'u he had his nose under his coat arm; beqa t'eghiłts'etl' he
  has a blister on his foot; bet'eghiłts'etl' he got a blister
      niłt'ehghilghełi (n.) doorlatch, lock
  P+t'egh# (a.d.s. n mom) snagging on P ht'eghneshnik I snagged the boat; ht'eghneshyu I got
  snagged, tangled; ht'eghnel'ets O I stumbled and got snagged on sth.
  Grp. 2. derived verb roots
  t' (v.f.sf.) PP, N → VERB ROOT
   O+i+Ø+q'at' (op.) to fornicate see q'a¹ cavity +t'
   qut' (mot.) surface, trap collapses qu surface + t'
   O+ł+qet' (suc.) slap, pat O with hand from qu surface + t'

  enclosure binghełt'ech' I stuffed it into it (e.g. into a sack); yadadnaniłt'ech' he filled it (boat,

   G+n+D+t'ech' (ext.) be crowded, stuffed, clogged, full, stacked nduqdnat'ech' that are crowded
  inside; vidahnat'ech' chudyi ch'adnat'ech' there is a lots of eaten beaver food coming
  out; tudnudet'ech' the water is clogged (with plants); hq' nidet'ech' things are scattered
  out; hq'nit'ech' pl. are scattered, jammed, stuffed, clogged, crowded; vidahnat'ech' U it is
  stuffed full, something is full; ts'ix ghin bayeghnasht'ech' those mosqitoes covered
  him; k'enchan tudnudet'ech' it (propeller) is clogged with grass; niłtunadet'ech' things are
  mixed up; hvaya andaz'i łu gu dutudnadet'ech' rabbit hair was all over here; hniqadnast'ech'
  hutiqutnik where the feathers were jammed around it, he found the water

  his head in the doorway from there; niłduvun denghałt'esh L he closed his lips together; nił'ił

     hnina gguya vidahdnat'ech'i (n.) I stuffed doll

t'em /jammed/
   mom t'ish t'em t'eł t'ish
   neu t'em 〃
   G+n+l+t'em (stat.) liquid, vapor, sticky object is clogged, conjested, viscous, packed, jammed,
  bloated theme is similar to G+n+D+t'ech', 'multiple objects are jammed', but this applies to liquid
  objects, not multiple solid objects vechutl'a nalt'em his stomach is bloated; yuq' hnalt'em the
  sky is cloudy; venik' tuk'nalt'em IU his nostrils are plugged up; badak'nalt'em it is stuffed
  full; yuq' hdnalt'em it is cloudy; hq'andnult'em it (door) is stuck closed; venik'
  tuk'nalt'em his nose is plugged; shchutl'a nalt'em my stomach is bloated; yuq' hnalt'em it's
  cloudy, solid overcast; yuq' hnighełt'im it was cloudy; shyit'ugh nik'nałt'em my chest is full (as
  when one has a chest cold); hq'adak'nalt'em outhouse hole is full; bidatanalt'em it is full of
  air; badak'nalt'em container is full of meat; venik' tuk'nalt'em his nostrils are
  plugged; nuhtnult'em I it's foggy, it is a thick soupy fog; it's full of snow O PK dukaq'di
  hq'antnult'em the door is jammed; dukaq'di hq'andenghinłt'em you jammed the
  door; budak'nalt'em it's full (container of mud, meat, fish, shit); badak'nanłt'em I filled it
  hq'andnełt'il (n.) squishy O
   d+n+Ø+t'em (stat.) be stretched tight, inflated denghazt'em I it is tight; dninłt'em inflate it,
  blow up it tight
   "> vek'ilt'em it is severed, cut by chopping with blunt instrument
   k'+l+t'em U-OI (mot.) limp, hop, pole vault rare, a loan from Ahtna nuk'telt'ish he
  limps; q'ank'elt'em he was limping; yunun nuk'nelt'em he vaulted across; k'tult'em he
  limped

t'eq' (t'uh) /move sg. animate, elongated quickly; sg. flies/   /√::Vclassif.-throw/ sg. fly merged
here, cf. AAD:343
   mom t'uh t'eq'   t'eh t'ih
   suc t'eq' "
   sem t'eh "       t'ih
   prog t'eh "      t'ih
   per t'uh t'eh    t'eh
   per t'uł t'uk    t'ush
   cont t'eq' "
   dist t'eq' 〃
   sem t'eh 〃 t'ih
  Grp. 1. sg. animate, elongated themes
   l+t'eq' (mot.) sg. animate, or stick-like object falls
   O+ł+t'eq' (mot.) throw, move animate, or stick-like O quickly, move stick-like, animate O
  vigorously
    he fell in the water; nuyghiłt'eq' he threw it (fish) down; tiyniłt'eq' he threw him out; łiq'a
  nik'u'iłt'eq' he tossed the fish up; shu qutałt'eq' he threw the fish at me; nuyghiłt'eq' he threw
  him down on ground; nuntghełt'eh, yełni łu I'll throw you down' he told him; nik'u'i'ełt'eq' I
  tossed it (baby) up in the air; nuydghiłt'eq' he threw it (the broom) down; ndaha q'u ighitsatl'
  nuydghiłt'eq' he had chopped it and felled it somewhere; chench' yih ch'iyiłt'eq' she threw him
  down the bank; tuveyghiłt'eq' he pulled him down in the water; nch'u qeveł venish tuytułt'eh
  ghuda so that it wouldn't bump the ice that way; q'ushla hk'udaninłt'eq' you almost knocked it
  away; hk'uyniłt'eq' yeł gheltlet diy'a eł tu'iltlet it pulled him back away and jumped on him and
  he (that man) jumped right in with his son

    ch'tełt'ehay (n.) feather with needle point; game played with feather with needle in end JT "The
    feather is sprung or shot from a piece of wood like an arrow tied to finger with piece of thread. In fall time,
    try to hit sparks by the campfire. A person who can hit these has the qualifications of a good hunter. This is
    used to kill mice and shrews."
  O+ł+t'eq' (suc.) club O forcefully, strike O vigorously with stick-like object; hit with stick, club O,

     yus Tika'a łu beł tuhqdghizet charcoal, smoke, and black ash went up into the sky and then

  fact neuter in aspect) This theme is common in O, is rare in U with t'ich'. No transitional is found,

t'i::nt'i
      nt'i, nt'ey (int.) IU ndi O where is NOUN; well, how's it going unlike other interog. pronouns,
      nt'i does not take -en and -na suffixes used for emphasis, really nt'i ndaja UI ndi ndaja?
      O where's your sister?; veyes nt'i veghuch'diłt'a we really use the skin (in many ways); nt'i
      dnigi, ndaha q'u idu? U where is the moose, where did it stay?; ndi dugul ghinqdi? O where is
      that axe; ndish shbalt'u qdi? O where is my coat?
      ndi (exc.) I get out of the way nt'i qek'di'un gheli q'ut'un ch'ahnidaq idi eła the sun was really
      shining in the morning when they woke up; tiyeniłti yeghin k'undet jyuq ch'q'u k'elqat ch'q'u
      nt'i the animals they let out them starving it was and they were eating and really well

t'i     /√::enc/
      shughu VERB+t'i (coll.) this is why VERB_t'i, that is when VERB_t'i suffixed to a verb phrase
        ghu iti'ihjesh dach' huda shughu q'udi qech' qeshnash t'i then you can save yourselves this is
      why now I tell you this; shghudghinni ni ghuda shughu vegh duneshu t'i you should advise me,
      that is why to you I came into; negh nink'dighelyani yighuda shughu negh neshu t'i let me leave
      word with you about why I came to you; yeghuda shughu k'begh desdgheltlet t'i that is why
      jumped in the fire for them; qeył ghetnuyi niłk'uch' yethdi shughu nak'qizdlan t'i at that time
      we also got different kinds of tools; yethdi shughu ghiłyuts t'i and that's when it snowed; yethdi
      shughu ghiłyis t'i and that's when it cust. snows

t'u³ *t'aˑ /under, beneath/        /√ⓐ::AN¹‴/
d.f.: t'u, t'uch', t'uh, t'ugh, t'udi, t'uda, t'uq', t'ut'
   Grp. 1. postpositions areal nouns
   P+t'u, t'uda (p.p.) I by means of, with the support of, assistance of P gguna t'uda nuyułtał he's
   leading him by the hand; sdulchik t'uda nugheshyuł I'm walking by holding on to chair; k't'uda
   nugheyuł he's walking hanging on to sth.; detets'a t'uda nugheyuł he's walking around by a
   cane; yet'uda gga'ilchet he supported him getting up; deqa t'uda nughik'et' he hangs by his feet
       chuggesh t'u zt'a'i (n.) meat cooked on a spit or roasting stick
       vet'u nuk'detggeni (n.) clothespin
       t'uq'e ch'elchini (n.) IO t'uk'inalkeni OU butcher knife with curved blade lit. 'curved upper
       side'
   -t'ugh, -t'uh (a.n.) under, beneath; one's location, place, home sht'uh my area (in house where
   one sleeps); qenq'a t'uh under the house; ves t'ugh beneath the bank, at the base of the
   bank; ndahdu k'qezdetulgha łuq'a uhuqel'ih yeh qut'uh qelah there their camp would be
   wherever they put up they would go to put up fish; łiq'a ghini ves t'ugh ht'ighilagh the salmon
   swam under the bank; shyida t'ugh tu'iłch'ik O I have heartburn, a burning pain inside my chest
       ełnen t'uh (a.n.) underground cache, cellar
       yeht'uh (a.n.) ceiling
       tsinht'ugh (a.n.) cave shelter (dugout or excavated)
       unht'uhdi (n.) underwear lit. 'furthest one below'
       -yit'ugh (n.) UI -yidat'ugh O -t'uyeq'e U diaphragm, inner chest
       niłt'uhdi (n.) U dry king salmon cut in two large pieces
       quggesh it'udi (n.) OU tava it'udi I bird lice, swan lice lit. 'beneath swan'
       Tsat'ukeght (p.n.) village site at Susitna Station lit. 'below big rock'
       Ht'u Bena (p.n.) Chelatna Lake (upper Yentna area) lake is in two colors, black/brown
       lit. 'below lake' (lake is below mountains)
       T'u'itnu (p.n.) Carpenter Creek (Matanuska Valley) lit. 'beneath creek'
   P+t'ut' (p.p.) within, under P
       vet'ut' k'ezdluyi (n.) L clothespin
   Grp. 2. t'uq' upon flat surface
   -t'uq,-duq' (a.n.) upper surface -t'uq and *-duq' are interchangeable for many speakers
   -tsit'uq' (a.n.) IU betsiduq' O top of his head
           -qant'uq' (a.n.) top of the foot, toe plate on boot
       -enlut'uq' (a.n.) back of the hand
   Grp. 3. as disjunct prefix, in strings
   t'u# (v.pf.) under

t'un¹ *t'aˑnɁ /leaf, thin/        /√::N-bioVdim/
  -t'una (n.) leaf, petal
  k't'un (n.) small plants, broad-leaf trees and bushes, young stage of woody plant
      k'et'un buts'a (n.) U aspen (Populus tremuloides) lit. 'leaf-round'
      kit'un, -kit'una (n.) U hat ↇ lit. 'tip leaf' noticeable U innovation
      ch'iht'un (n.) UI false elder, Pacific red elder (Sambucus racemosa)
      k't'un, davak k't'una (n.) leaf tobacco
      k't'un ggigga (n.) OU k't'un ggih'a IO leaf or plant bugs, leaf beetles (Miridae;
      Chrysomelidae)
      nuk't'undelayi (n.) autumn wind that scatters the leaves

  to swallow it; binik'etnaltlet it (straw) got clogged
   q+d+n+ł+tlet (mot.) I-OU storm moves hdnałtlet a storm arrived; nach' hdnułtleł I heavy
  storm is coming our way; na'a hdnałtlet snow storm came; hdenłtlet it (flurries) went; nlugha
  na'a hdnałtlet the snowstorm has not come to us yet
   shegh#d+l+tlet (ext.) I-OU be covered with frost, frost extends vesheghdultlet it is
  frosted; dutusheghdaltlet it frosted among the trees; qashegh'idghaltlet it frosted over a surface
  (door); ełnenq' hsheghdultlet frost settled on the ground; nasheghnultlet we were frosted (faces,
  whiskers); vesheghnultlet he was frosted; qeghsheghdaltlet frost coming through a
  crack; hsheghdultlet it is frosted (area,pants, coat, etc); venaq'ach'elyi vesheghdultlet his
  glasses frosted up; dutusheghdaltlet trees frosted upward; from steam and North wind over water
  Grp. 2. to burn, ignite quickly
   (i+)d+i+l+tlet (mot.) fire burns, ignites quickly
   with l hdiltlet a fire started; hdaltlet there is a forest fire; bich'aqeghdaltlet O it burned over
  (the timber); niłch'adaltlet it burned in half; nuqdultlet it burned and fell down; bek'daltlet it
  burned in two; diqadghiltlet one tree caught a fire
   causatives, light, ignite O; strike O (a match) yediłtlet he built a roaring fire, he struck a
  match; yuydiłtlet he built a fire for her; nudiłtlut light it; qahqidenghaniłtlet they burned it
  down to the ground; yuyeh hdalts'i t'anch'gheli qahqidenghaniłtlet inside they were staying, all
  of them they burned it down to the ground; beq'aqdetanłtlet O I burned it all up; nuydizełtlet I
  light it cust.; niłnuqentdułdełi hdiłtlet lightning storm started a fire; nuytidighełtleł I will light
  it; naneq nuydinłtlut light the lamp; huqeydiltlet they built themselves a fire; ch'u giga iqu
  tayu hnuyu huqeydiltlet they built a fire for themselves and went for berries; huydi'eltlet I built
  myself a fire; huydinltlut build yourself a fire; yeł hu'idiltlet he built himself a fire with
  it; qya'unuqyaghdełtlet they burn them off; vich'ak'idghaltlet it burned out (in the stove); no
  more wood in the stove to burn sitll coals in there; vich'ak'idenghaltlet it inadvertently burned out
  (in the stove)

tl' *tl' /verb-forming sf./
  tl' (v.f.sf.)
   O+Ø+tsatl' chop O sharply, forcefully from tsa rock +tl'
   O+d+ł+qutl' chop O bluntly, carefully from qu surface +tl'
   U">k'+n+l+q'utl' U (mot.) go being in pain may be q'u edged object +tl'

  -tl'a (n.) crotch, between the legs
      -tl'aghu (n.) pubic hair
      betl'ak'ghinigi (n.) U large male marten lit. 'the one with sth. hanging in crotch'
  tl'ach' (adv.) crescent shaped, crescent moon tl'ach' ilchi OI crescent moon formed; tl'ach'
  naz'un his head is tilted; tl'ach' elchin he's bending over; tl'ach' elch'etl' it's bent into a
  crescent
      tl'analyi (n.) L pink plumes, bistort (Polygonum bistorta) lit. 'that which lays in a crescent
      shape'

tl'aq' /night/     /r√::N!-time/   /Nav.: tl'ee'/ unusual distribution, one of the only Alaska Dene
cognates with Navajo tl'ee' ≡
  tl'a (c.) night
  tl'aq', tl'aq'i (n.) OIl night occasionally used in U cf. ≡ tets IU k'eghun tl'aq'i half the
  night; yagheli tl'aq'i qighila' it was nice last night
     tl'aq' teh (adv.) OIl night time tl'aq' teh ezhi it's cold at night
     tl'aq'i jani (n.) OIl a day and night tl'aq'i jani niłkidulguk O solstice came; lit. 'night
     connected to day'
     tl'adu (n.) OIl yesterday cf. q'ayt, q'ayet IU
     tl'aq'da (n.) OIl tonight (future)
     niłqun tl'aq'da (n.) OIl tomorrow night
     tl'aq' t'etniya (n.) U owl (nickname) lit. 'the one that calls at night' note here that U uses the
     OIl term

  tuntl'agi (n.) O-UI rip tide, brown bear nickname ch'u ggagga ghin łitl'en teh ghu qunsha
  anqelah dghu ezgayi yiq' t'ent'ayi qul, tuntl'agi qyełnih there is nothing as dangerous as a brown
  bear digging up ground squirrels in the springtime; they call him “riptide,”; PK "brown bear in spring;
  said to mean ‘ impassible'

     tl'ał, tl'atl' (n.) "black paint" as obtained by Osgood p. 214

     for tl'ał flint

tl'ech' *tl'əts' /blue-black/       /√::V-color/ main term for black in I, which does not use t'ich' main

    neu tl'ech' "
    tran tl'ich' "

   for this root
      dashtl'ech' I-OU ditl'ich' Su it is black; heyteh vinłni nitl'ech' in winter the water gets
   dark; didetl'ich' I it turned black; OU it is blue, it turned blue; tudnidetl'ich', tudnitl'ich' I it
   became black on the water; tudnuttl'esh a black object was moving on the water; shegh
   dnidetl'ech' I'm bruised; shluggena dashtl'ech' my fingernails are
   black; baqighenashdetl'esh O there are clouds on the mountain tops; k'usht'a

   has black hair; kisht'a ench'u chidnitl'ech' his hair is not too black (i.e. brown hair)
       didetl'ich'i (n.) OU blue

    hu#G+D (conv.) become bruised hudnayeshtl'ech' I'm bruised, hurt myself (black
   blue); hutnashttl'ech' OU hutnashdetl'ech' I he is bruised; hudnazhittl'ech' you have
   bruises; shchish hudnashtl'ech' I bruised my knee; qunutl'ech' O she's
   tatooed; shiqudetl'ech', shiqedetl'ech' I was disgusted

  Dena'ina, only in this one Ahtna place name

tsa¹ *ttheˑ /rock/    /√::N!-lith/
    related to tsatl' chop
  tsa (n.) rock, boulder, stone generally thought to be larger than qałnigi portable rock (of smaller
  size)
     tsayan (a.n.) cliff
     tsayan dnayi (n.) OI mythic cliff people
     tsaq'a (a.n.) natural rock hole, rock fish bin Qeghnilen is famous for these rock fish bins
     tsatesq'e (a.n.) U tsaq'esda T crack or hole in cliff, boulder
     tsa'un (a.n.) cave
     tsaduq' (a.n.) U rock ledge
     Tsa Dnazdlut (p.n.) Sister Rock lit. 'rocks are there'
     Nutsada'iy (p.n.) Lions Head, 8 99 lit. 'rock that is standing' Nutsada'uyi qetnih q'u yet ghu
     tl'uduq' nastsut, yedghu tl'uduq' nastsut That ‘rock that extends' they call, there (Lions Head)
     she sat down on a ledge, there she sat down.; SP76-clan
     chabash (n.) stone skin scraper, ulu lit. 'rock stone'
     tsaghełniga (n.) UNL tsaghełnik'a OIl tsalts'esa T steambath rocks
     tsansla, tsansneł (n.) U stone axe
     tsaził (n.) scree, loose rocks, boulders, rock slide on mt.
     chaggesh (n.) UO fire tongs lit. 'rock fork'
     tsa'itl'di (n.) I fire tongs (flattened on one side) PT "two sticks to pinch rocks for steambath, place
    finger in middle to regulate width of opening"
     tsakegh (a.n.) U elevated plain, bluff, mesa
     huntsaya (a.n.) I rock reef
     qutsaht'in (n.) U mica lit. 'rock that is transluscent'
     tsahduya (n.) U marsh violet (Viola epipsila) lit. 'stays on rocks'
     chaq'ech' (n.) chert-type rock PB "harder than shale"
     Chaq'ech' Qelahi (p.n.) mt 3396 north of Chilchitna; 2.1, 19 lit. 'where stone type is gathered'
 tsa (i.) throwing dach'q'u sheł q'antsadghiłnik that is how he played around with me, threw me
 around; tutsałdał he is throwing rocks in the water; łnik'utsa'iłdał he is throwing a ball up in the
 air
     ełtsuntsa (n.) OIl tsunłtsa T ntsułtsa, tsułtsa U ełtsunłtsa NL high bush cranberry
     (Viburnum edule) widely distributed, analysis moot ełtsuntsa dnaghiłch'ik' the high bush

   front of us

tsatl' *ttheˑtl' /chop (sharply)/     /d√::Vsuc/ clearly derived from tsa¹ rock

   cons tsetl' "
   mom tsał tsatl' tseł tsał

   neu tsatl' 〃
   O+G+Ø+tsatl' (suc.) chop, split O with axe in O qutl' is more common than tsatl'
     yeghitsatl' he was chopping it; yiztseł he gave it a chop; ten tsał he is chopping
  ice; k'ak'nitutseł he'll begin to chop; chik'a anitsatl' he's starting to chop
  wood; k'ak'nitsatl' he's beginning to chop; k'ak'nestsatl' I am beginning to
  chop; baynitsał you chop down to a point; yeghitsetl' I he gave it a series of
  chops; k'dghintseł you are moving along chopping sth.
     chik'a ch'tsałi qit'a'ideshni I know how to chop wood; q'azestsatl' I chopped them
  all; q'utsa'i ch'k'etsałi we are done chopping now; K'uzhaghałen gun yeqank'et'a ghin
  yentsał K'uzhaghałen was chopping away on his Achilles tendon; ch'eyighitsatl' he chopped into
  it (den); ch'qeyghitsatl' they chopped a hole (through the beaver's house); quk'dghitsatl' he
  chopped sth. up; quk'dnanstsatl' I accidentally chopped sth. up; yaq'a nik'nitsatl' he chopped
  the entire quantity of sth.; k'nanstsatl' he chopped it all down
   P+eł chop down, to fall veł k'tnazitsatl' you chopped it down; yeł k'taztsatl' he chopped it
  down; veł k'tenstsał I am chopping trees down; ch'vala eł dnaztsatl' he chopped down a
  spruce; veł k'tnazitsatl' you chopped it down; veł k'tenghestsałni q'ayetsał he was trying to
  chop it down; sheł ts'ek'tentsała don't chop the tree down with me (on it); sheł k'ehtentsał they
  are chopping sth. (tree) down with me (on it); veł k'dnazitsatl' dahdi nda'ich' q'u t'tghilił? if you
  chop the tree down with him, how will you do it?
   pot. k'idagheltsatl' it is choppable; k'idaghiłtsatl' he has an ability to
  chop; huk'idaghiłtsatl' he is a good wood splitter
   tsatl' vs. qutl' k'atnitsay ch'ak'danełqutl' I knocked off (with blunt edge) the punk; k'atnitsay
  ch'ak'danestsatl' I chopped off (with blade edge) the punk
      hik'ełtsełi (n.) OI hnihik'ghiłtsatl'i U walrus (Odobenus rosmarus) lit. 'chops with teeth'
      -ghitsala (n.) UI -ghi k'ełtseła O fangs, incisors, front teeth

tsen¹ *tthən /downland/        /√::DIR‴/
d.f.: tsi, tsit, tsih, tsen, chench'
   -tsi- (dir.) towards river
   -tsen (dir.) toward the river
   -tsen (dir.) toward water, toward the lowland yutsen at a place by river
   -tsit (dir.) at a specific place toward water, in the lowland ch'q'u yutsit gheli kelugh nch'u
   qak'di'e' so down below at the timberline there was no brush; dutsit IO at a place by river
   -tsih (dir.) IO in, towards a place, by river
       Duntsih (p.n.) Lake Clark-Lake Iliamna lowlands
       duntsih t'an (n.) lower Lake Clark, Lake Iliamna Tanainas
       q'ghettsi (n.) U slough
       Q'eghetsigga (p.n.) small creek south of Susitna Station
       Yutsi Qilan (p.n.) Knutson Bay lit. 'lowlands are there'
       Htsit (p.n.) Tishimna Lake site,"Whitefish", 1.3 22 lit. 'lowland place'
   -ghentsit (a.n.) toward the water from P veghentsit yizdu she stays below him
       k'eghentsiden (n.) U second co-wife
   -tsih (dir.) toward the water, toward the lowlands Tsałt'eshi yutsih hghaztun 'Black Stone Axe'
   points to the lowland
   chench' (adv.) I towards water chench' ch'nudilen he rolled down towards water; chench'
   dach' qenq'eya tuyeniłt'eq' she slung it down the hill among the mountain alder
       dunchench'dna (n.) L Nondalton or Iliamna Dena'ina as called by Stony River people
       lit. 'people in the lowlands, people by the water'
   -tsen (p.p.) upon, over, encompassing P vetsen nuyditneł nułjił ni he keeps pouring over it so it
   will be dark, strong (tea); qetsen vesdghiłen they ganged up on him; vetsen vesdesix idahdi
   łuq'u nqiduk'dulzex' qełni łu dive in and everyone help yourselves to food' he told them; vetsen
   vesch'dusixni gu t'ehyiluq they tried to gang up on him, kill him in a gang; deggenqehnałyit ey
   gu tlegh ghu idi ghin łuq'u qeytsen vesdghiłen they all jumped up there grease all of it everything
   they went for it

       position", or possibly 'steer boat place' PK thought this place name might be the origin of the
       name Chickaloon; a Chief Chickaloon resided Chickaloon Bay in the early 1900s. Chickaloon
       River He was known as Tsicklu by Ahtna speakers

                                                    ts'


                                AAD1990:ts' (part) 398-418, KAD2000: tl' (part) 589-616,
   Dene Lexware dictionary comparisons:
  LTDD: tth' MTDD: ttth', YMM92: ts' (part): 623-646
   Den lacks


  ts'un, ts'eq listen

ts'a¹ /Ω/ several place names have this opaque syllable
  Vałts'atnaq' (p.n.) Mulchatna River; 2.1, 1 lit. '?-?-stream' classic founding place name by early
  Dene in the Southern Alaska Range; paired with names for Hook Creek Tałchatnaq' not
  analyzable by expert speakers
  Taq'ahts'a Vena (p.n.) Gusty's Lake, 1.3, 4 lit. 'timbered lowlands ? Lake'
  Sts'a, Qests'a (p.n.) L hill near outlet of Tundra Lake, Ven Dash Vena, 1.4, 60 archaic stative
  verb

  ono ts'a' "
   d+l+ts'a' IlU-O (op-ono.) be clinking, clanking sound, sound of the striking of metal, stone, teeth,
  bone hdelts'a sound of a hammer
   O+d+ł+ts'a' (suc.) strike O with object making sharp, clanking noise yedełts'a' he is striking it,
  making clanking sound

ts'i¹ *tth'i /straight/   /ⓐ::vpf#/ appears as a prefix in verbs, in directionals, also forms some
compounds
  ts'i (c.) straight in in various nouns
     ts'iti (n.) U major river lit. 'straight water'

     ch'iteshnich' (n.) midnight
     ts'ik'tl'ił (n.) U bow string
     ts'igek'a (n.) O ts'ingega U alpine blueberry, bilberry (Vaccinium uliginosum ssp. alpinum)
  ts'i# (v.pf.) straight

  posture; ts'itiztun the road is straight; ts'iqiztun stream channel is straight; tl'ił ts'inazdlu the
  rope extends straight
  ts'i+qu# (a.d.s. zneu) straight surface ts'iqutaz'u it is straight grained
  ts'i (pf.) straight prefix in directionals ts'itnun nughunesh he is coming straight across in a
  boat; ts'iyegh straight down; ts'ideq qeni'uyi IO ts'itgge hni'uyi U it sticks straight up
     ts'itnuni (n.) west wind (straight across); cross ribs in boat
  ts'i (i.) index finger, pointing bets'itansel I am pointing at him; qeyts'itazel they are pointing at
  him; nuk'uts'i'inyił point your finger up at it

x¹, sh *xʸ /customary/
  x, k (v.sf1.) customary suffix note the rule Cet+yh → CVk; also some vowel lengthening, w →
  u, e → i teyux I teyush UO he customarily goes
  x (n.f.sf.) customary noun
     ush (n.) snowshoe lit. 'customary compact object'
  nkix (n.) NL nkish OUIl rosehips (3 elements small objects-more-customarily
  suk (adv.) old, ancient

x², sh /momentaeous/
  x, sh (v.sf1.) momentaneous suffix

y¹, yin /3rd per. object/   /√ⓐ::PRO-v.pf./
  y+ (v.pf.) him, her, it yeghi'an he saw him; Sam ghi'an he saw Sam
  ye+ (v.pf.) epenthetic 4th person can insert ye into intransitive verb veł yizdu she stayed with
  him; beł ch'ayiniyu he went out with him/her

y² /y-qualifier/    /ⓐ::v.pf.+/ rare qualifier prefix, themes are often cognate in Alaska
   y+D+'an (ext.) be in view, be visible in a direction
   y+ł+'ech' I k'+ł+'ech' OU (suc.) P sneezes the y+ in I is the conservative form, cf. y+ in dog
  barks
   y+ł+'eq' (op.) to hiccup
   y+Ø+keł I-UO (op.) animal bark2s, coughs slightly
   y+Ø+tsa (suc.) sg. dog bark2s
   y+ł+qu (mot.) become dawn, daylight
  Grp. perhaps relevant to y+
   (i+)d+G+Ø+q'un (mot.) ignite, catch on fire, fire burns, moves
   O+(i+)d+G+ł+q'un to build a fire the i- prefix is significant in Den. and Ahtna verbs to burn
   COMP yn+Ø+zen (desc.) intend to, think of, want, desire COMP; with tran realize COMP prefix
  yn+ is from mind, thought ini

ya⁵ /good/     /√::C/ local to Dena'ina, in compounds, comparative meaning and distribution
uncertain

     greeting

     q'anhghutnish baydalgi n'at sometimes they all went across to Kenai Peninsula in a bidargi in

ya⁶ /be fat/ only in I
   neu yan ya' ya'
   tran yah ya' yah yah / yih
   l+ya I-UO (dim.) be fat dghelyan he, it is fat; I t'izelya' I got fat; t'itighelyah I will get
  fat; nuk'elyih animals cust. get fat; nch'u shtutda t'ilye' my father is not fat; nulyih he cust.
  gets fat; nughelyan it is fat again; dghelya'en it, he I was fat; denlyani nihdi yeghuyeniłkit he
  fed the fat parts etc. to him; dghelyanen qut'an a fat person; a yehdi dghelyan gheli ha t'ah,
  łiq'a elqadi ghuda that is when they get really fat, due to eating salmon

ya⁶ /filthy/   /√::PADJ/
  eya, edya (p.adj.) dirt, filth edyach' t'qeyilug they got it dirty; OI eya ezdlan it got dirty; OI
    eya'ina (n.) dirty people
    eyah niyi (n.) U gray-cheeked thrush (Catharus minimus) lit. 'it says filthy'
    edya'i (n.) I eya'i, eyayi OU filth, dirtiness edya nlanen I eyanen UO filthy person; ts'ix
    eyayi ggwa ninya mosquitos are filthy little animals
  eya (exc.) dirty! exclamation of revulsion, distress 'eya! qadak'daznex hnuyu
  ndunutsidalen 'oh dirty' when he heard it he pulled his head back in

ya⁷ /surprise/   /√::EXC/ only used in I, seerms distinct from ya⁶
  iya (exc.) I oh!, hey, oh my iya sh'in oh I don't know; iya yagheli yida oh! that's all right!; iya
  dundensht'ih shida oh! I am just teasing you; q'uyehdi iya hva jan dnudilzit then hey! the days
  began to get longer on them; iya gu nt'ant oh! are you really doing this
  ka iya (exc.) oh you! damn you!
  ayaya (exc.) I oh my

   cont ya "
   neu      ya' "

     sg. or dual talk, speak qenash UO qenax I he is talking; yeł qenax he is talking with
  him; qeshnax ha'it q'udigu I am talking now; yegh qenax he is talking about, is criticizing
  it; Dena'inaq' qenax he speaks the Dena'ina language; vech' hegheshya' I bech' hgheshnak
  UO I talked to him; niłch'qutnax ni let's talk together; qghinak UO hghiya' I he
  talked; shech' hghiya' he talked to me; shiłtun qinax you talk nonsense; qiz'unch' qenax he is
  giving a speech; shuqeł'i hqenax they were talking quietly, we're whispering; ch'du qenash he
  talks funny; nch'u qesyaŷ I k'usht'a qesnak' O nch'uk'a qesnak U he does not

  ni I ch'qunashni OU let's talk; ch'eqenashidghay U ch'eqenashigu O don't talk
     tl'eghench' nuqutnak he answered back; shtuqiniya he talked without making a
  point; niłch'equdya' we talked to each other; huł nuqetnax he talks to himself; yeghu
  qtenash O he talks sarcastically to him; tidahghiya' I he talked evilly,
  ominously; q'enuqidak U he talks backwards; "Bel', enge hq'e nuqinash" "Bel', talking like
  that is forbidden."; ch'aqiniya' I he spoke out; yeł ch'aqiniya' he quoted him; hch'ih nqeya he
  is lieing, talking deceptively; q'aqiniya he quit talking; nch'u k'i vech' hyiyal they didn't even
  talk to her; gun Angidahtnuh Dena'ina k'usht'a gasht'anaq' k'usht'a qiyal this Seldovia Native
  did not did not speak English
   q+i+ł+ya (pot.) deqighiłya' IU t'qughiłya' O he is talkative
   ch'i#q+i+Ø+ya I-OU (op.) to lie ch'inhghazya he lied; yeł ch'inqazya he lied to
  him; ch'inqiya he is lying; ch'indeqighiłya he tells lies, he tends to lie
   k'+q+Ø+ya (mot.) sg. utters, speaks, talks, says something nik'qiniya' he spoke sth. to a
  conclusion
   P#k'+q+ł+(y)a (mot.) cause P to move by talking yiqu dik'tałya' he directed him to do it, for
  it; yenik'qiniłya he settled ; he bawled him out

(y)a² (yu) /pick berries/ cf U ba
  durI ya      yan ya ya / yah

  momO yash yun yał yał / yax
  momI yax yan yał yał
  neu     ye'
   O+i+G+n+Ø+(y)a (op.) pick O (berries, cones) k'inghiya' k'inghiyan I k'inghiyun O she
  picked sth. (berries); giga iniya she is picking berries; k'inazun O she picked them all out; here
 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                           549

  z- is as stem-initial k'intuya O she will pick them all; nk'ineya O she moves along continually
  picking sth.; dlutdemisha idenya he is picking spruce cones; idenya he was
  picking; k'inuyał she is moving along picking sth; k'inghildi ch'itnuya we will pick
  cranberries; q'u gheli dak'itnazyan she almost had picked a full quantity (in basket); HD-
  bpick t'anch'q'u q'ahnazehyan du? did you pick them all out?; łuq'u kench' taydatl' k'qinuyay
  ghuda everyone went to the flat in order to pick berries; giga skiyeh nih łu k'qiniya
  k'inghildi under trees here and there they picked some cranberries; k'inaghiłye' O dek'inghiłye'
  I she is a good, fast berry picker; pot. ch'k'inaghiłye she is a slow berry picker; pot.

yik, yex, shesh /spirit, health/ potentially a derived root with yi inside
  -yiga (n.) shadow, spirit, reflection, image
     tuyiga (n.) OI fingerling, baby salmon
     tahyiga (n.) reflection in water
  shesh (n.) UO shex I lucky sign, good medicine, health shesh nuhudel'an may you (pl.) have
  good luck; said when someone sneezes shesh q'at'hul'an he supports himself; shesh
  nushghił'an I hope you give me good luck; shesh ak'eniłtan she delivered sth. (child)
     shesh k'nuqeł'ixi (n.) good luck sign
     shesh k'eliga (n.) UO shex k'eliga I protection song, lucky song
     shesh'a k'elashen (n.) U midwife lit. 'one who saves'
     K'eluyesh (ps.n.) king of the animal spirits who resides in the mts. Doroshin (in Schiefner 1874)
     describes Kenai beliefs re k'eluyesh. "out of respect to k'eluyesh a special mountain language
     is used, and many avoidances with food are practiced"
   dur yix yik yix
   sem yex "
   rep¹ yex "
   mom yix yik yex yix
   D+yex (conv.) escape, evade, become safe sdyex I eshjesh UO he is
  safe; yeshdyex I yeshjesh UO I am safe; yiłyex he saved him; nt'i q'u la'u ch'udyex well that
  time we might make it; yeda q'u iti'ihjesh there you will be safe; yada qa ita'ihjesh łuq'u qeł'i
  iłdeł de' whatever with you need to save yourselves all you hide away; SP-SuSty yethti dida ghun
  yiłyesh then his friend that he saved; dichinna ghuna qeyech' itujesh those people who are
  starving can save themselves with those (fern roots); N. Chickalusion 1980:36 t'anch'q'u qudi q'u
  huqulyex ni everyone's survival was dependent on it
   l+yik (mot.) breathe, gasp, exhale, snort telyex he is gasping; k'idiki nshełdałtuk' ch'u
  telyex he is gasping because he ran too much; tuk'dnulyik he cooled sth. by blowing on
  it; telyex he gasps; k'edeshex it (bear) is making a puffy, breathy noise; k'edashex it (bear)
  made a puffy, breathy noise; k'nalyik' he choked on food; note k' in perfective stem,
  err. tudnelyex it is exhaling underwater
   d+Ø+yex (mot.) light wind blows, vapor moves dazyex I dayesh UO it dried in the
  wind; hdedyex there are vapor waves (on land); tudendyex I vapor is rising; hdazyex there
  were vapor comwaves; hededyex it's blurry; tunutendyex water raises up and down slightly in a
  water hole; qanich'eyi dałyexi the wind dried it; hyidełyash they are drying it in the wind
     dazyexi (n.) I dayeshi OU fish wind-dried one day
     qiz'in dayeshi (n.) O lightly dried and smoked clams
     veq'en dazyexi (n.) I vequq' dazyexi Il bequq' dayeshi OU half-dried fish
   O+i+n+ł+yik (stat.) support, provide food for O yinałyix he is supporting him, hunting for
  him; hu'inalyix he supports himself by hunting; hu'inighelyik he supported himself

yit *yuˑd /chase/
  mom yit yit yeł yish
  prog yeł
  per   I  yeł yit yeł yit
  per   UO yeł yet yet yet
   dist yit 〃
   O+n+Ø+yit (mot.) to chase, pursue, startle O dakaq' qech' hdi qeytnazyit they started to chase
  them toward the door; yenaniyit I yeninyit UO he arrived chasing
  it; łuhtengheshyił I q'antghesheł O I will chase him around; łuhnanshyit I chased him
  around; shtunanshyit I chased him off; chuq'eya niqiynult'eq'da chuq'eya aqaghniyith when
  they drove it to a birch he chased it up the birch; niq' nił'ak'eqendyit they drove game on the
  island; qeytnazyit hnuyu ghini łu ye'u ntikahdałen they started to chase them when the animals
  started moving out; yadi ch'wala aqa nuyit t'a qitni niqinlt'eh they pound it (stake) into which
  tree they want him to chase it up; quqiynghiyet they drive it up; nił'aqenadyit O they drive
  them together; niyninyit he chased it to a place; nił'aqenadyit they drove them
  together; quqeyniyet they drive, chase it; qeynazyit they chased him; to spook, startle
  O k'yenghiyet sth. (animal) spooked him; ggagga shenghiyet a bear startled me
   h#q+n+ł+yit (mot.) group, herd goes at a fast pace,runs t'anch'q'u shtuhhnaniłyit they all ran

     nin'ijuyi (n.) old moose lit. 'one that stays put'
     quyushi (n.) U quyeshi NL qunchi S qunshi OIl qubishi U beluga whale (Delphinaterus
     leucas) lit. 'one that comes upward' much reinterpretation; the prefix qu upward is common to
     all terms; the term in U is most transparent, based on yu° sg. goes; note the OIl form is
     reanalyzed and blurs the stem; a second term in U means 'one that swims up'
     talyiya (n.) U semipalmated plover (Charadrius semipalmatus) lit. 'walks among waves'
     tuyushi (n.) U large muskrat (nickname) (Ondatra zibethicus)

  qighishin dudi'ehyish you guys didn't tie the sack closed well

                                                z, s


                                      AAD1990: (part) z/s 446-468 , KAD2000: (part) l/ł 374-426,
   Dene Lexware dictionary comparisons:
  LTDD: dh/th , MTDD: dh/th, YMM92: s: (part) 465-468, z: 730-764
    we integrate z and s and often these consonants stems alternate in Dena'ina For alphabetization z
  precedes s as with the other fricatives s and z alternate, however z is aways y- in U
   dh-z Den-LT check


z¹, s¹ *dh, *th /z mode-negative/      /ⓐ::vpf+/
    in the KAD:374-375, 391 without any elaboration we treated cognate Koy. prefixes as separate
  affixes (negative prefix, pp 374-375ł², l and p. 391 mode le³ drawing upon Urschel 2006, here we
  combine these into a single alternating affix with two separate functions
  Grp. 1. as mode prefix
  z, s- (v.pf.) z-imperfective/perfective mode zidu you are staying, sitting; tazyu I tazu O tayu
  U he left, went; nutasdyu I nutashju OU he returned
       z-neuter imperfective
  ls yeshdu
  2s zidu         yidu
  3s zdu          eydu
  1p ts'ezdu ch'ezdu          ch'eydu
  2p zehdu yehdu
  3p qezdu qeydu
   S is sitting, staying
     z+ROOT+t (if.s.) t-transitional perfective, changing takes an dh-perfective and the t verb
     suffix eba dhedlat QQ CHK he got sick; xwyh xudhdlat it became winter
  Grp. 2. dh/th negative note in 3rd person, Ø-classifer, th (voiceless) appears
  z, s (v.pf.)
  dh+,th+ (v.pf.) negative
  PROC z/s+ROOT+n~x+[voic] (if.s.) imperfective negative (neuter or active) ADD

  (fish); yedghizegh he scraped it (skin); dghesegh O dgheszegh I dghesa U I scraped it
  (skin); qghizegh he scraped, raked an area; veku qqinzah scrape out its insides; itigheszeh I I
  will scrape it once; vekuh gheszegh I scraped out it's insides (fish); qughesegh I cut it by
  scraping; k'idaghiłzegh he is good at scraping; pot. k'eyes gin k'idaghelzegh this skin is
  scrapeable; pot. huhgheszegh I scraped the area out; ngheszegh ve'iłgida k'u I scraped off the

 out; q'ench' nutdalen the sun started back down; dudeh k'dalnen it is noon, midday
  bun#d+gh+l+nen, zik U (mot.) birds, insects fly in a flock, in a swarm nubundghulyesh it flies
 in a flock
  d+n+l+nen, zik (mot.) fly quickly, sg. animate (human, fast flying bird, insect) speeds, (vehicle,
 airplane) speeds, travels quickly; sg. (human) travels quickly dnalnen UO dnalen I it arrived
 flying; dnilen I he (bird) took off in flight; nudnulzex nudnulyesh U nudulnił O it, he is
 flying around; nudnilen I he (bird) took off in flight again; sank'nal'iy nlugh dnilzex the plover
 didn't fly for a long time; vejex ghednalen he flew over a caribou; janq'u denghelnił O I am
 still flying; niqadnalen he flew in a circle; niqa'idenghalen he circled; hk'unudnilen away he
 flew; "Ch'u nik'unitaghelyesh" idiłdi nik'unilnen "and I'll fly up," he said to him, then he flew
 up; M. Chickalusion, 1980 nudnilen ch'łuq'u taq'a nadnulen he took off and he flew back down
 to the flats; ch'ggagga gin nudnilen chickadee flew away again; hełch' idi eł hvegh nuydnalen
 łu that evening to them he flew back; uł naqanqdetalnen O he is drunk; beł niqahdnalnen
 U he is drunk, dizzy; beł niqahdnghulyesh he will get drunk; deggech' diqidnulen he flew up
 through the smokehole; nudnulzex he was flying around; Chulyin gun dnilen Raven flew
 off; shtunudnalen he flew away again; q'uyehdi yeh ch'andnalen then there he flew back from
 that direction; di' yeh łu hdnalen hq'u k'qisen oops there he flew but there was nothing
 there; shtunudnalen ki nch'u nuqit'igh he flew away again and they never saw him
 again; kuntsulya dnayi hvegh eydnalen he flew to the woodpecker people; yechutl'a egh nituk'
 ch'q'u ey yegh dnalen through his belly he cut a hole and flew through him; ch'q'u
 yich'adnalen it he flew out; q'uyehdi Chulyin nuqesdlan łu hdnilen ha' shtuhdnalen then they
 became Ravens again and they took off and they flew away; qatnilnen it took off; yitgu' naq'ech'
 nanlnen I flew over here; yitgu' naq'ech' q'andenghelnik I was flying over
 here; qutnulnen fly upward; naqatnulnił O it is flying in a circle; naq' dnilnen it flew over
 us; q'esiq' qetnulnen it flew through the clouds; na'eł itnilen it flew by us
     dnulzexi (n.) one bird flying
     nudnulzexen (n.) pilot

sukli   Aleut suklux̂ Alutiiq cukluk bone ornament worn on nose
  sukli (n.) dentalium nose ring recalled by Peter Kalifornsky; a term cited by Zagoskin as tsukli
  (Michael:285,332) as well as by Jetté; apparently has been obsolete in Koyukon for some
  time copied by Jules Jetté "Given by Petrof as name of dentalia among the Kenai Peninsula Athabaskans
  ("Kenaitze"), both nose and ears of the men are pierced for the insertion of the white shells of the dentalium,
  or hyqua, here called "sukli". This shell was formerly in general demand among the Indian tribes of the
  Territory, but now this seems to be the only section where there is a steady call for the article. (Petroff
  1900:85)."


suk', tsuk' *thaˑk' /old/        /√::adv/
d.f.: suk, suk'i, d√:isuk' ☼ , r√:tsuk'
      reanalyzed in U
   suk, suk' (adv.) suk'i IO tsuk'i U old, ancient suk'i qghilat where it was old, an old
   place; suk'i zełan I got old; suk'i t'enł'an I am getting old; suk gheli really long ago

       suk qeyagh'a (adv.) old-style object, antique suk qeyagh'a ush old-time snowshoes
   suk' qayeh (n.) OI tsuk' qayeh U ancient abandoned village
   suk' ghu (adv.) OI tsuk' teh U long ago
       sukdu (n.) O sutdu L tsukdu U traditional story JT "sukdu explain everything, about the
     animals, first beginning, war, etc. qunsha stories told in summer only; nini and hawk tell anytime, stories
     about winter animals usually told in winter time on long nights." ch'u suk gheli hsukt'a nił'eł
     nuhqulnish they told each otherthe really old stories; ch'du sukdu hq'u lach'q'u t'qijuqch' an
     unbelievable story but it really happened; qaduqeydghinishi ch'aduch' qeych'quldin suk
     hsukt'a they heard how they learned from it old stories
  isuk' (n.) L bell-shaped clay pot, vessel ⇨ likely related to suk' old lit. 'oldish' rare in Den.,

related to nzhah pellet ice; this mainly appears with j due to a D- classifier; the causatives have the
underlying form ł+zhagh; this entry appears at jagh
   nzhah (n.,i.) OI nyah U snow; pellet ice, sluch ice first ice pellets in fall time nzhah
   ez'un there is a patch of snow; tanzhah'idah there is slush ice in river; looks like snow but goes
   clear to the bottom

      nzhah ghelqadi (n.) late spring snow that hastens melting
   mom jah jagh jeh jah

   of melting); nuhtinujeh U nuhnitujeh O it will get soft; elugh nuqnishjagh O nuqnishjagh
   n'ugha it hasn't softened yet; vetunuynashagh I itunuhninshagh U it (snow) melted other snow

zhak *zhʳeˑg /carve/      /√::Vop/
   dur-ł shax shak shax shax
   dur zhax zhak zhax zhaz
   durU yash yak yash yash
   conc shax shak shax shax
   sem shex "
   mom shax shak shex shax
   O+G+ł+zhak (op.) make O (shavings), carve on, whittle O k'eshax IO k'eshash U he is

shi¹, sh¹ *si, s /I, me/    /√ⓐ::PRO‴/

 Ø ' b/v/w ch ch' d dl dz f g gg gh/h ĥ j k k' l/ł m n p q q' r t t' tl tl' ts ts' y/x z/s zh/sh
Dena’ina Dictionary, Vers. 2.7, April 20, 2020                                                   622

  shi (pro.) I, me, 1st pers. pronoun
     shi k'i (adv.) I shi k'a U shi k'u O me too
  sh- (pf.) my, me, first person sg. object/possessive prefix shlik'a my dog
  sh- (v.pf.) me, first person sg. postpositional object shk'eza beside me
  sh- (v.pf.) me, first person sg. direct object shizch'et he scratched me
  esh (v.pf.) I, first person sg. subject
     esh-, esht-, eł-, el- (v.pf.) I, first person sg. subject shown here with prestem syllables with the
     four classifiers deshchsinh I am hungry; eshtnunh I am drinking it; ełtsi I am making
     it; nelget I am scared
  shi, shida, shit'i, shin, shina (enc.) evidential: speaker assert, really, actually, personal assertion a
  derived root from shi 1st sg. pronoun, Holton Lovick 2000 Holton Lovick 2000, "sh- enclitics occur
  when the speaker either talks about herself or when she offers a personal comment about a third person
  referent."
   shi   "ey ghu dunudełkesi shi" yełni łu "I'm tying it back me" he told him; undat chinudghełt'ik'i
  shi it is the last time for my killing (bears); el'ekna qubech' ch'inighet'an k'a shi maybe some
  medicine man wished them bad luck; Chida kuya gu nushiltan shit'i that old lady's grandchild
  brought me back here EVID; ntuqesa nu'idghułniłi shi it looks like your spear that fell; qil egedu
  shi ki no it'll hurt me; nch'u gheli egedu'i shi it really does not hurt; "dach' ghu ghinyuł ch'q'u
  qut'an dighelts'i qegh ninyu shi," yełni łu "thus as you were walking you came to the place where
  people lived," he said to him
   shit'i, shit, shida yagheli shit'i it is really good; ch'k'etnuni gheshchet shit'i I am really going to
  quit drinking; shk'dechit shit'i we are definitly quitting; shi sh'idi shi'i that's my property; veł
  dudensgis shida I am just joking with her; udi k'i dghiłchek' qu shit chitdatda she must have
  been small too, dear grandma; shi dach'u shq'uła chi'ełish shit this is how I kill
  marmots; ch'adach' daghiltey ghu shida he was still that strong; SP-HN shi shida, shi
  shit that's me; niqu tayeshyu nhegh neshyu shida after you sg., I came to you pl.; yagheli
  nugheshdlahi ghuda nch'u t'i'esht'e' shida good I won't be again so I am not well me; nch'u
  gganashziłchet; hq'u ggana'elchet shida don't lift me up; but I can get up by myself
  shin, shin'i (enc.) sg. human emphatic, he, she certainly nch'u idzi'iłkełen shin he is one you
  won't be able to grab; yin shin that's him (agreement with an identification); shegh eztanen
  shin he is the one laying by me (now); shgguya shin'i he is my son; Jim shi'i I am
  Jim; Tulchina shin'i I am Tulchina clan
      shina (enc.) emphatic plural humans yina shina that's them; niqanach'tghudnixni; veqisena
      shina' let's go back to the shore; there's no one there; "gun di Jilughun shina," qeyłni
      łu "these guys are Jilughun," they told him; qilna gheli shina they are really bad people
      shit (enc.) specific place, time enclitic enugha k'zilqat shit you haven't eaten yet; "negh
      neshyu shit" yełni łu I came to you he told her; zehdu shit you (pl.) are sitting; zidu shit you
      are sitting; q'u shit'i that is all
  shida (v.enc.) emphatic nch'u łdninldaq shida you're not sleeping; answer to above
  statement yił'ech' shida I am sneezing

shu¹, shi *Ɂiˑsiˑ, Ɂuˑsiˑ /perhaps/       /√::ENC/ cognate with Koy. eesee KAD:728 Holton Lovick
2000 note this is related to 1st sg. pronoun shi
d.f.: shu O
   qushi (adv.) evidently qushi ch'q'aynigen hvegh qisen evidently there was no child with them
   shu (enc.) UO k'ishi I maybe, perhaps iqech' shu dghini' shu UO iqehch' k'ishi dghini' shi
   I he might have said that; dnaldaq k'ishi I maybe he is asleep; nagh tuyuł k'ishi I he might
   come to us; k'usht'a k'u shu iłe O it didn't become much it seems; ndahdu k'u shu
   qch'aninyu? O where might you have come from?; ey gu q'udi gu hluzhunh ninłhdatun da
   k'ishi niqu łuhhtudeł I now in ten more days maybe they'll come looking for you her
   q'u shich'a (adv.) perhaps, could be so
   shuq'u (enc.) one wonders nada'ich' q'u shuq'u ch'tut'ał? one wonders how we will be
   doing?; yada shuq’ u inlan da? what might you be?
   shughu (enc.) emphatic, thus it is, in my opinion nt'i nayi k'i nch'u qit'anch'inizen shughu
   veqisil da well we don't know a reason it disappeared; shi shughu I'm the one; yen shughu, udi
   shughu he's the one; nen shughu you're the one; yina shughu they're the ones; shughu
   ndalvay huhdenlyah łu in my opinion they raised geese for themselves
   VERB -ghuda shughu (coll.) I suppose this is why shi sh-ghuda shughu t'an t'i all this is
   happening because of me!; ndał yeh hts'anindatl' ghuda shughu nts'uk'a qit'aniyen the cranes
   flew over, but he didn't know the reason; Nicolie 1976
   ADV shughu VERB t'i (coll.) that was the time that yethdi shughu izin nak'qizdlan t'i that was
   the time we got guns; yethdi shughu ghiłyuts t'i and that's when it snowed; yethdi shughu
   ghiłyis t'i and that's when it cust. snows
   shughu' (adj.) that way, at that time, that's when yithdi' shughu' gganelnek t'i that's when I
   woke up; yithdi' shughu' nak'qiz lent'i that's when we had (tools); izin yethdi shughu
   nak'qizdlan t'i that's when we got guns; duzhqugh shuq'u t'itułne' shuq'u' I wonder how fast it
   will go; enda'ich shu'u ch'tut'ał I wonder how we will be (health, etc.)

➡shu in shuch'a sow, female black or brown bear see yuch'a


shu² *shʳaˑ /sun/     /√::N!-env/ rare, reanalyzed vowels
d.f.: shu, sha, ja
   shu- (i.,c.) sun not productive in Dena'ina shuynałt'a he got sunburned in the face
       shu t'ił'in (n.) I nushuyułtałi U caribou in summer lit. one that gets sun,' 'one (herd) that sun

zhu, zhun *zhʳaˑ /plain/        /√::ADJ/
  Grp. 1. the adj.
  zhun, zhu (adj.) plain, bare, clear never appears alone
  yuzhun (n.) I clear sky yuzhun na'a qilan we have a clear sky
     yuzhun qilt'ani (adj.) NIl blue lit. 'like clear sky'
  zhuqut'anch' (adv.) I plainly zhuqut'anch' qadaqevdeshnex I hear them plainly; zhuqut'anch'
  ghesh'an I saw it plainly
  -azhu (n.) OIl-U sign, marker tinitun qughest'a qazhu qadil'an the trail is well
  marked; shazhu qadil'an I have a mark on me; qazhu qadil'ani hqugh niniyu he went as far as
  the mark
     qazhu hdił'ani, qazhu hdil'ani (n.) marker, sign
  dezhun (adv.) IO deyun U without a spouse, unmarried, out of wedlock dezhun zdu s/he stays
  unmarried; Gujun dezhuni ił nagh niyu Gujun has come to us with kindness, respect; dezhuni
  nudultan he is illegitimate, bastard
  Grp. 2. number ten
  qeluzhun (n.) O qelun U hluzhun I ten objects lit. 'bare hands'
     qeluzhunna (n.) O qelunna U hluzhunna I qeluzhunh OI ten persons
     qelundi (n.) U qeluzhunh OI ten times, places; two dollars hluzhunq'
     vek'iltench' 2.50; hluzhunq' ch'q'iluq'di 3.00; hluzhunq' ch'q'iluq' vek'iltench' 3.50
     hluzhunch', qeluzhunch' (n.) IO qelunch' U ten ways
